Assigned stewards, operators, educators, volunteers, and institutional partners can perform required roles with acceptable workload, competence, retention, documentation, and escalation capacity.

Volunteer participation alone does not establish operator capacity.

**CTH-6 — Educational Feasibility**

A school or educational module can deliver defined age-appropriate learning activities safely, inclusively, and within institutional workload and safeguarding boundaries.

Participation does not demonstrate improved learning.

Any learning-outcome claim requires an explicit educational method.

**CTH-7 — Civic Participation and Accessibility**

The deployment can sustain transparent and accessible participation without political conditioning, discriminatory exclusion, hidden data extraction, or dependence on a narrow group.

Attendance is an output, not proof of cohesion, trust, or inclusion.

**CTH-8 — Public-Value Formation**

The intervention generates evidence-supported public value within one or more defined domains:

• ecological;

• productive;

• educational;

• social;

• institutional;

• resilience-related;

• administrative;

• fiscal.

Public value shall remain separate from PRU and financial value unless a distinct admitted pathway exists.

**CTH-9 — Institutional Coordination**

The Prometheus architecture reduces selected coordination failures, documentation gaps, duplicated work, unassigned responsibility, or implementation friction relative to the defined starting condition.

Adoption of a framework or dashboard does not demonstrate institutional efficiency.

**CTH-10 — Legal and Governance Durability**

The legal, governance, asset-custody, data, stewardship, and continuity arrangements function in practice and remain aligned under defined stress conditions.

Execution of legal documents alone does not prove durable custody.

**CTH-11 — Regenerative Distribution Effectiveness**

Recipients of propagated or distributed material can establish, maintain, harvest, use, or re-propagate a defined proportion of the material under disclosed conditions.

Distribution counts do not demonstrate effectiveness.

**CTH-12 — Replication Feasibility**

The deployment can be adapted to another site or institution without losing evidence quality, legal clarity, maintenance capacity, community legitimacy, or ecological fit.

Replication feasibility cannot be inferred from success at one site alone.

Each hypothesis shall define:

• exact claim;

• unit of analysis;

• intervention;

• baseline;

• comparator where applicable;

• primary outcome;

• secondary outcomes;

• measurement period;

• expected direction;

• minimum detectable or decision-relevant change where feasible;

• confounders;

• missing-data treatment;

• falsification or weakening condition;

• Claims Register status;

• permitted wording.

**Pre-Registration Rule**

The Pilot shall be pre-registered before the intervention begins or before outcome data are examined for confirmatory purposes.

Pre-registration shall distinguish:

• confirmatory hypotheses;

• exploratory questions;

• descriptive indicators;

• operational targets;

• safety indicators;

• learning objectives.

The pre-registration record shall contain, where applicable:

• pilot identity;

• site and jurisdiction;

• intervention dates;

• module scope;

• hypotheses;

• indicators;

• primary outcomes;

• measurement methods;

• baseline period;

• comparator design;

• sample or unit assumptions;

• analysis plan;

• exclusion rules;

• missing-data rules;

• stopping rules;

• adverse-event rules;

• governance approvals;

• reviewer identities or roles;

• protocol version;

• method hash;

• document hash.

Post-hoc findings may be reported as exploratory.

They shall not be relabelled as pre-registered confirmation.

**Baseline Gate**

No improvement claim is permitted without an adequate baseline.

The baseline shall describe the applicable starting condition across:

• ecology;

• water;

• production;

• operations;

• governance;

• maintenance;

• public access;

• participation;

• education;

• legal structure;

• data quality;

• public expenditure;

• institutional coordination;

• risk;

• community context.

The baseline period shall be sufficient to capture relevant variability where feasible.

A single photograph, testimony, sensor reading, event count, or laboratory sample shall not be treated as a complete baseline.

Where prior data do not exist, the limitation shall be disclosed and the claim language reduced accordingly.

**Comparator and Causal-Identification Rule**

Claims of superiority, improvement attributable to the intervention, risk reduction, savings, or programme effect require an appropriate comparator or causal method.

Potential approaches include:

• matched control plot;

• paired plot;

• untreated or standard-practice area;

• staggered implementation;

• difference-in-differences;

• interrupted time series;

• matched school or institution;

• comparison cohort;

• synthetic control;

• contribution analysis;

• triangulated mixed-method design.

The comparator shall be ethically, ecologically, operationally, and institutionally appropriate.

A comparator shall not be constructed by denying essential benefits or exposing participants to avoidable harm.

Where a causal comparator is infeasible, Prometheus shall use bounded language and classify the result as observational, contributory, or exploratory.

**Pilot Cohorts and Sampling**

The Pilot shall distinguish:

• total programme population;

• measurement population;

• follow-up cohort;

• qualitative sample;

• comparison group;

• excluded units;

• lost-to-follow-up units.

Sampling shall address, where applicable:

• representativeness;

• voluntary participation;

• selection bias;

• attrition;

• seasonal variation;

• site heterogeneity;

• household capacity;

• school attendance;

• operator differences;

• climate;

• water access;

• socioeconomic barriers.

Convenience samples may be used for exploratory learning.

They shall not be presented as representative without justification.

**Civic and Public-Value Outcome Architecture**

Pilot outcomes shall be classified as:

**Implementation Outputs**

Examples include:

• hectares or plots prepared;

• plants established;

• propagation material produced;

• participants trained;

• events conducted;

• tools distributed;

• modules activated;

• legal instruments executed.

**Operational Outcomes**

Examples include:

• maintenance completion;

• plant survival;

• data completeness;

• volunteer retention;

• operator response time;

• incident closure;

• budget adherence;

• school-holiday continuity.

**Ecological Outcomes**

Examples include:

• infiltration change;

• soil-cover change;

• biomass return;

• soil-moisture persistence;

• erosion reduction;

• biodiversity indicators.

**Productive Outcomes**

Examples include:

• harvest;

• nursery output;

• edible production;

• plant survival;

• propagation multiplier;

• productive continuity.

**Educational Outcomes**

Examples include:

• ecological knowledge;

• demonstrated practical competence;

• curriculum delivery;

• teacher feasibility;

• student participation.

**Civic and Institutional Outcomes**

Examples include:

• repeat participation;

• accessibility;

• role clarity;

• conflict-resolution performance;

• coordination time;

• documentation completeness;

• partner retention.

**Public-Value Outcomes**

Examples include:

• food access;

• reduced operational duplication;

• improved public-space function;

• increased local productive capacity;

• avoided water or input cost;

• resilience indicators.

Each outcome shall retain its own evidence status.

Outputs shall not be described as outcomes.

Outcomes shall not be described as causal impacts without the applicable design.

Public value shall not be described as realised financial saving without a defined and evidenced fiscal pathway.

**Pilot Data Architecture**

The Pilot shall maintain a purpose-limited and role-separated data architecture.

Data classes may include:

• ecological;

• hydrological;

• productive;

• operational;

• educational;

• civic;

• financial;

• legal;

• safeguarding;

• incident;

• public-value;

• personal;

• political.

The system shall define:

• data owner or controller;

• data custodian;

• data processor;

• access permissions;

• collection purpose;

• format;

• provenance;

• method;

• retention;

• publication status;

• privacy status;

• anonymisation or aggregation;

• deletion or archive pathway.

No Pilot dataset shall be treated as fully interoperable, shareable, public, or capital-admissible merely because it is technically accessible.

**Evidence Quality and Confidence**

Each material indicator shall disclose, where applicable:

• source;

• method;

• unit;

• calibration;

• frequency;

• spatial coverage;

• temporal coverage;

• missingness;

• uncertainty;

• reviewer;

• conflict of interest;

• transformation method;

• confidence status.

Evidence confidence shall not be increased merely because multiple low-quality sources repeat the same claim.

Convergence requires independence, methodological relevance, and compatible units.

Pilot-level confidence does not automatically satisfy the PRU capital confidence gate.

**Governance and Role Architecture**

The Pilot shall identify, where applicable:

• Pilot Sponsor;

• Pilot Director or Coordinator;

• land custodian;

• public or institutional authority;

• Earth Custodian;

• Regenerative Operator or Aggregator;

• ecological design lead;

• MRV lead;

• data steward;

• school or educational lead;

• safeguarding lead;

• community stewardship circle;

• financial or procurement lead;

• legal reviewer;

• public-value evaluator;

• independent scientific reviewer;

• Claims Register custodian;

• incident-response owner;

• maintenance owner;

• communications lead.

The Pilot Sponsor shall not control evidence admission merely because it provides funding or political support.

The operator shall not certify its own performance without disclosed review.

The communications lead shall not override the Claims Register.

**Community Legitimacy and Participation**

The Pilot shall define:

• who is affected;

• who was consulted;

• who may participate;

• who holds decision rights;

• who may object;

• how objections are recorded;

• how non-participants are protected;

• how conflicts are escalated;

• how access and inclusion are reviewed.

Community participation is not a substitute for:

• land authority;

• professional competence;

• safeguarding;

• scientific review;

• legal compliance;

• maintenance responsibility.

Community legitimacy shall not be claimed solely from attendance, social-media activity, volunteer numbers, or endorsement by local leaders.

**Ethics, Safeguarding, and Vulnerable Populations**

Where the Pilot involves children, patients, elderly people, incarcerated individuals, disabled persons, low-income households, indigenous communities, migrants, or other vulnerable populations, the applicable safeguards shall precede research and communications objectives.

The Pilot shall not:

• use essential benefits as research inducements;

• condition food or plant access on data participation;

• publish identifiable vulnerability data;

• infer political preferences;

• convert participant hardship into investor or campaign narratives;

• use vulnerable participants as proof of mission legitimacy;

• collect special-category data without necessity, authority, minimisation, and review.

Participant welfare and dignity take precedence over Pilot completion.

**Maintenance and Minimum Operating Runway**

Before activation, the Pilot shall demonstrate a credible operating runway covering:

• establishment;

• seasonal maintenance;

• water;

• replacement planting;

• staff or operator time;

• safeguarding;

• evidence collection;

• data custody;

• public reporting;

• insurance;

• incident response;

• school or institutional closure periods;

• at least one material contingency.

A Pilot shall not be described as funded merely because installation capital is available.

Installation without maintenance funding is an incomplete deployment.

The Pilot shall disclose dependencies on:

• one founder;

• one donor;

• one operator;

• one political administration;

• one grant;

• one volunteer group;

• one technical provider;

• one water source.

**Pilot Financial Boundary**

The Pilot may receive:

• grants;

• philanthropy;

• public funding;

• donations;

• cooperative contributions;

• service revenue;

• research funding;

• concessional finance;

• approved commercial income.

Pilot funding does not create:

• validated returns;

• PRU value;

• RAP maturity;

• investor rights;

• market demand;

• financial scalability;

• proven cost effectiveness.

All material costs shall be recorded, including:

• design;

• legal review;

• procurement;

• installation;

• maintenance;

• staff;

• volunteers where relevant;

• water;

• tools;

• replacement planting;

• insurance;

• safeguarding;

• data;

• laboratories;

• independent review;

• administration;

• decommissioning.

A Pilot shall not be described as low-cost by excluding donated, public, volunteer, or in-kind resources.

**Pilot Risk Register**

The Pilot risk register shall include, where applicable:

• ecological underperformance;

• drought;

• flooding;

• fire;

• contamination;

• invasive species;

• plant disease;

• maintenance failure;

• operator loss;

• volunteer attrition;

• school closure;

• safeguarding;

• food safety;

• public injury;

• political capture;

• community conflict;

• data breach;

• public-resource misuse;

• legal challenge;

• procurement failure;

• cost escalation;

• funding withdrawal;

• evidence failure;

• missing data;

• reputational overstatement;

• premature financial claims;

• failure to replicate.

Each risk shall have:

• owner;

• trigger;

• mitigation;

• residual risk;

• review cadence;

• escalation pathway;

• Claims Register linkage where material.

**Adverse Events and Negative Evidence**

The Pilot shall record and disclose material adverse outcomes, including:

• ecological harm;

• reduced productivity;

• excessive water demand;

• unsafe access;

• maintenance burden;

• teacher or staff overload;

• participant exclusion;

• political misuse;

• plant mortality;

• programme abandonment;

• biosecurity failure;

• public conflict;

• financial irregularity;

• data misuse;

• safeguarding concern;

• failure of legal or governance arrangements.

Negative evidence shall not be excluded from the evaluation package.

Prometheus treats failure as admissible institutional evidence.

**Stopping, Suspension, and Redesign Rules**

Predefined stopping or suspension conditions may include:

• unacceptable safety risk;

• safeguarding breach;

• illegal activity;

• material ecological harm;

• contaminated soil, food, water, or plant material;

• loss of land authority;

• unavailable maintenance;

• critical funding failure;

• persistent operator failure;

• severe data or evidence failure;

• political capture;

• public-resource misuse;

• unresolved community harm;

• material deviation from pre-registration.

A Pilot may be:

• continued;

• conditionally continued;

• modified;

• narrowed;

• paused;

• suspended;

• terminated;

• decommissioned.

Stopping a Pilot is not institutional failure where stopping protects participants, living systems, public resources, or evidence integrity.

**Decision Gates**

The Pilot shall pass sequential decision gates.

**Gate CG-0 — Constitutional Fit**

The Pilot is consistent with the canon, Access Covenant, political-neutrality boundary, human-dignity safeguards, and non-bypassable sequence.

**Gate CG-1 — Authority and Legality**

Land, institutional, safeguarding, public-resource, data, and operating authority are identified and sufficiently documented.

**Gate CG-2 — Baseline and Pre-Registration**

The baseline, hypotheses, methods, comparators, analysis, stopping rules, and Claims Register entries are approved before confirmatory outcome analysis.

**Gate CG-3 — Operational Activation**

Operators, maintenance, safety, water, equipment, data, finance, and incident systems are active.

**Gate CG-4 — Evidence Integrity**

Provenance, method, calibration, missing-data, reviewer, confidence, and no-double-counting controls are functioning.

**Gate CG-5 — Outcome Review**

Outputs, outcomes, adverse events, costs, public-value claims, and limitations are reviewed.

**Gate CG-6 — Independent Review**

Applicable ecological, scientific, operational, safeguarding, legal, governance, public-value, financial, and technical reviewers have completed their scoped reviews.

**Gate CG-7 — Continuation or Replication Decision**

The governance body determines whether to:

• continue;

• modify;

• extend;

• replicate;

• suspend;

• close.

No gate may be presumed closed because the Pilot received funding, political support, positive publicity, community enthusiasm, or technical completion.

**Civic Genesis Pilot Readiness States**

Prometheus establishes the following pilot-readiness ladder.

**CGP-0 — Conceptual Pilot**

The territorial concept and preliminary mission are identified.

No implementation or outcome claim is permitted.

**CGP-1 — Pilot Boundary Identified**

The site, modules, institutions, communities, population, and preliminary research questions are defined.

**CGP-2 — Authority and Feasibility Assessed**

Land, legal, safeguarding, operational, water, maintenance, budget, data, and institutional feasibility are assessed.

**CGP-3 — Pre-Registered and Baseline-Ready**

Hypotheses, methods, indicators, comparators, baseline plan, governance, risk, Claims Register, and stopping rules are approved.

**CGP-4 — Operational Pilot**

The Pilot is active within its approved scope.

Claims are limited to implementation and observations.

**CGP-5 — Evidence-Complete Pilot Cycle**

The defined cycle has produced a sufficiently complete evidence, cost, incident, maintenance, and outcome record.

Completion does not imply success.

**CGP-6 — Independently Reviewed Pilot**

Independent reviewers have evaluated the defined domains and issued limitations, findings, and recommendations.

**CGP-7 — Decision-Ready Pilot**

Governance has sufficient evidence to decide continuation, redesign, replication, or termination.

**CGP-8 — Bounded Replication Admission**

A defined component may be replicated within an approved adaptation envelope.

CGP-8 does not establish universal validity, PRU eligibility, RAP maturity, capital readiness, or political-programme success.

The CGP ladder is distinct from all other Prometheus maturity ladders.

**Independent Review Package**

The Pilot review package shall contain, where applicable:

• pre-registration;

• protocol versions;

• baseline;

• site maps;

• legal and institutional authority;

• intervention record;

• raw-data references;

• method and data hashes;

• indicator calculations;

• missing-data analysis;

• adverse events;

• risk register;

• financial record;

• maintenance record;

• safeguarding record;

• public-value analysis;

• ecological analysis;

• comparator analysis;

• uncertainty;

• Claims Register;

• deviations from protocol;

• conflict-of-interest declarations;

• participant and stakeholder feedback;

• reviewer reports;

• replication recommendation.

Independent review shall be scoped.

A legal reviewer does not validate ecology.

An ecological reviewer does not validate electoral compliance.

A financial reviewer does not validate public value.

A community consultation does not replace technical review.

**Replication Decision Rule**

Replication may be admitted only where the component to be replicated has:

• a clearly defined function;

• evidence-supported performance;

• documented limitations;

• a maintenance model;

• an operator model;

• a legal and institutional pathway;

• a cost model;

• a safeguarding pathway where applicable;

• a data model;

• a risk and failure record;

• adaptation rules;

• a Claims Register status.

Replication shall identify what is:

• invariant;

• adaptable;

• site-specific;

• legally specific;

• ecologically specific;

• culturally specific;

• institutionally specific;

• unvalidated.

The Pilot shall not be copied as a branded package without local redesign.

**Pilot Communications Boundary**

Public communications may state:

• what the Pilot is testing;

• what has been implemented;

• what evidence has been collected;

• which outcomes are preliminary;

• which claims remain unvalidated;

• which risks or failures emerged;

• which gate remains open.

Public communications shall not state or imply:

• Prometheus is validated;

• the model has ended hunger;

• the model has proven community transformation;

• the model is universally scalable;

• public ROI is guaranteed;

• the programme is investor-ready;

• PRU value has been created;

• RAP market maturity has been reached;

• political success validates the intervention;

• a successful pilot proves national effectiveness.

**Civic Genesis Pilot Admission Gate**

A Civic and Territorial Genesis Pilot may be described as operational only when:

• its modules and boundaries are defined;

• competent authorities and responsible entities are identified;

• legal, safeguarding, public-resource, data, and operating permissions are sufficiently active;

• hypotheses and methods are pre-registered;

• baseline collection is complete or formally underway before intervention-dependent claims;

• comparators or bounded causal methods are defined;

• operators, maintenance, finance, water, safety, and incident systems are functioning;

• Claims Register entries are active;

• evidence provenance and method controls are operational;

• unresolved risks are disclosed.

It may be described as evidence-complete only when the defined evidence cycle, adverse-event record, maintenance record, cost record, and outcome record are sufficiently complete for review.

It may be described as independently reviewed only within the exact scope of completed reviews.

It may be described as replication-ready only for the specific components, contexts, conditions, and adaptation ranges admitted by the replication decision.

The lowest unresolved ecological, operational, legal, governance, safeguarding, public-value, data, financial, or review gate controls the permitted Pilot claim.

**Genesis Pilot Invariant**

Prometheus may construct an ambitious pilot.

It shall not design the evaluation to guarantee success.

It may seek positive ecological and civic outcomes.

It shall remain capable of detecting failure.

It may engage communities and institutions.

It shall not treat participation as validation.

It may generate public value.

It shall not automatically financialise that value.

It may prepare a replication pathway.

It shall not scale unresolved defects.

The constitutional rule is:

pilot before proof;

baseline before improvement;

comparator before superiority;

evidence before claim;

review before replication;

learning before scale.

# Canon-Wide Civic and Territorial Integration and Reconciliation

The Civic and Territorial architecture extends the operational scope of Prometheus without replacing, weakening, reinterpreting, or bypassing its existing canonical definitions, evidentiary hierarchy, financial boundaries, legal boundaries, governance rules, or capital-readiness gates.

The civic architecture is an application and deployment layer of Prometheus.

It is not a separate canon.

It does not create an alternative pathway from public benefit to financial value.

It does not create an alternative definition of the One Human Ecosystem, the Prometheus Regenerative Unit, the Regenerative Asset Pool, verified evidence, admissible value, legal rights, governance authority, or capital readiness.

The controlling rule is:

the canon defines Prometheus;

the civic architecture governs civic and territorial deployment;

site protocols govern implementation;

evidence governs claims;

law governs rights;

public authority governs authorised public use;

community legitimacy governs legitimate local participation;

no civic maturity status creates PRU, RAP, legal, market, runtime, or financial maturity.

**Canonical Civic Architecture Registry**

The following civic and territorial components form the current Prometheus Civic Deployment Architecture.

| **Component** | **Canonical function** | **Does not create** |
|----|----|----|
| Civic Neutrality, Political Independence, and Mission-Compatibility Boundary | Protects party independence, institutional pluralism, data separation, and non-capture | Political endorsement, campaign authority, ideological ownership |
| Civic and Territorial Deployment Architecture — CTD | Governs territorial application across communities, institutions, land, operators, and public authorities | Ecological proof, legal rights, PRU maturity, RAP maturity |
| Public Value and Civic Claims Architecture — PVM | Governs public-value domains, causal claims, attribution, and public reporting | Automatic monetisation, PRU uplift, financial return |
| Public-Land OHE Protocol — PL-OHE | Governs OHE deployment on public, educational, charitable, institutional, or community land | Ownership, possession, harvesting rights, permanent land entitlement |
| School Food Forest and Child Safeguarding Protocol — SFF | Governs child safety, educational integrity, privacy, food safety, and school operations | Educational effectiveness by participation, child scoring, political use of child data |
| Town Square and Community Hub Operating System — TSH | Governs civic hubs, markets, nurseries, tool libraries, training, public access, and continuity | Community-wide representation, social cohesion by attendance, political authority |
| Legal Commons and Stewardship Custody Stack — LCS | Governs title, use, stewardship, benefit, control, succession, insolvency, and remedies | Legal protection by terminology alone |
| Civic Data and Electoral Compliance Protocol — CDEC | Separates regenerative, beneficiary, public-resource, civic, and electoral systems | Voter profiling, campaign entitlement, political use of ecological or beneficiary data |
| Regenerative Propagation and Civic Distribution Protocol — RPCD | Governs species-neutral propagation, biosecurity, unconditional distribution, logistics, and evidence | Food security from distribution counts, political obligation, guaranteed cultivation |
| Civic and Territorial Genesis Pilot Framework — CGP | Governs pre-registration, baseline, comparators, evidence, stopping rules, review, and bounded replication | Validation by pilot completion, universal scalability, automatic capital readiness |

These components are subordinate to:

• the Institutional Boundary;

• the evidence-before-value principle;

• the non-bypassable sequence;

• the Claims Register;

• the Legal Opinion Gate;

• the MRV and no-double-counting controls;

• the human-dignity boundary;

• the AI non-authority boundary;

• the capital-readiness architecture;

• the lowest-unresolved-gate rule.

**Single-Source Discipline**

Each civic construct shall have one controlling definition.

Later references shall function as contextual references and shall not create competing definitions, alternative maturity states, broader rights, or weaker admissibility requirements.

The following are the canonical abbreviations:

• CTD — Civic and Territorial Deployment maturity;

• PVM — Public Value Maturity;

• PL-OHE — Public-Land One Human Ecosystem readiness;

• SFF — School Food Forest readiness;

• TSH — Town Square and Community Hub readiness;

• LCS — Legal Commons and Stewardship Custody maturity;

• CDEC — Civic Data and Electoral Compliance maturity;

• RPCD — Regenerative Propagation and Civic Distribution maturity;

• CGP — Civic and Territorial Genesis Pilot maturity;

• PRU-R — Prometheus Regenerative Unit maturity;

• RAP-M — Regenerative Asset Pool market maturity.

No document, dashboard, API, partner communication, public report, political material, data-room artefact, or investor document may redefine these abbreviations or substitute one maturity ladder for another.

**Maturity-Ladder Separation Matrix**

| **Ladder** | **Governs** | **Highest status means** | **Does not mean** |
|----|----|----|----|
| Evidence maturity | Claim-to-evidence progression | Evidence has passed the relevant evidentiary review | Financial value, legal rights, market demand |
| CTD | Territorial deployment readiness | A territorial deployment may be replication-ready within its defined envelope | PRU eligibility, public-value proof, market readiness |
| PVM | Public-value outcome maturity | A public-value claim may be institutionally admitted for a defined use | Cash flow, investor right, PRU uplift |
| PL-OHE | Public-land deployment readiness | Long-term stewardship is admitted for the defined site and period | Land ownership, permanent entitlement, universal replication |
| SFF | Educational and safeguarding readiness | A school deployment is independently reviewed and locally replication-ready | Universal educational effectiveness or clinical benefit |
| TSH | Community-hub readiness | A hub has a reviewed, replicable operating package | Representation of the whole community or social-impact certification |
| LCS | Legal commons and custody maturity | A legal stewardship structure is operationally effective and stress-tested | Permanence, bankruptcy immunity, ecological performance |
| CDEC | Civic and electoral compliance maturity | Civic neutrality and system separation have been independently reviewed | Political endorsement or universal legal compliance |
| RPCD | Propagation and distribution maturity | A reviewed propagation and distribution package exists | Food security, guaranteed establishment, ecological validation |
| CGP | Civic pilot maturity | A component is decision-ready or admitted for bounded replication | Prometheus validation, universal scalability, capital readiness |
| PRU-R | Unit-level regenerative analytical maturity | A unit may be legally structured at R7 | Market demand or liquidity |
| RAP-M | Portfolio and market maturity | A defined legally admitted structure may be market-active at M7 | Universal liquidity, guaranteed return, public-value proof |
| Runtime maturity | Software release and operational admission | A component may be relied upon within its admitted scope | Ecological truth, legal rights, public-value validity |
| Legal instrument status | Enforceable rights and obligations | Rights exist within a valid instrument and jurisdiction | Ecological performance or market demand |

A deployment may hold different statuses simultaneously.

For example:

CTD-5 does not imply PVM-5.

PVM-6 does not imply PRU-R4.

PL-OHE-7 does not imply legal ownership by the operator.

LCS-6 does not imply RAP-M6.

CGP-8 does not imply Prometheus-wide validation.

The governing rule is:

each claim shall cite the maturity ladder relevant to that claim;

no maturity shall be borrowed from another domain.

**Civic Claims Register Extension**

The existing Claims Register remains the controlling disclosure and admissibility index.

The following fields shall be added where a claim concerns civic, educational, territorial, public-value, public-land, political, propagation, community, or public-resource activity.

| **Additional field** | **Definition** |
|----|----|
| civic_claim_class | Constitutional aspiration, policy objective, programme theory, output, observed outcome, attributed outcome, independently reviewed outcome, institutionally admitted claim |
| public_value_domain | Ecological, productive, nutritional, educational, social, cultural, institutional, resilience, administrative, fiscal, health-supportive, intergenerational |
| deployment_archetype | Public-Land OHE, School Food Forest, Town Square, Legal Commons, propagation programme, Genesis Pilot, or other defined archetype |
| beneficiary_population | Defined population, institution, geography, or territorial group affected by the claim |
| maturity_ladder | CTD, PVM, PL-OHE, SFF, TSH, LCS, CDEC, RPCD, CGP, PRU-R, RAP-M, or other controlling ladder |
| maturity_status | Current level within the applicable ladder |
| safeguarding_status | Not applicable, required, pending, active, reviewed, breached, suspended |
| political_neutrality_status | Not applicable, separated, under review, limited interaction, breached, suspended |
| public_resource_status | No public resource, public-resource use disclosed, review pending, admitted, non-compliant |
| legal_custody_status | Proposed, analysed, drafted, executed, operational, independently reviewed |
| external_validity_boundary | Population, site, jurisdiction, period, climate, institution, and use beyond which the claim may not be generalised |
| public_permitted_wording | Exact language permitted in public, educational, policy, procurement, or partner communication |
| public_prohibited_wording | Language exceeding evidence, authority, legal status, or maturity |
| data_protection_status | Data classes, controller, purpose, access, retention, and unresolved privacy conditions |
| review_expiry | Date or condition triggering mandatory re-review |
| cross-gate_dependency | Other gates that must close before the claim may be externally used |

No civic or public-value claim may be upgraded merely because:

• a programme has political support;

• public funds have been allocated;

• the project has been inaugurated;

• participants provide positive testimonials;

• a dashboard displays the result;

• a pilot has ended;

• a legal structure exists;

• a community partner endorses the claim;

• a funder or administration requests stronger wording.

**Civic Claims Register Seed Entries**

| **Claim ID** | **Controlled claim** | **Status** | **Admissibility** | **Required gate** |
|----|----|----|----|----|
| CLAIM-CIV-001 | Prometheus is politically independent and institutionally plural. | Constitutional boundary | Admissible as governance boundary | Canon and governance preservation |
| CLAIM-CIV-002 | Mission compatibility does not create political affiliation, ownership, or canonical authority. | Constitutional boundary | Admissible | Political-neutrality controls |
| CLAIM-CTD-001 | Prometheus may support bounded civic and territorial deployments. | Architectural | Admissible as framework claim | CTD protocol compliance |
| CLAIM-CTD-002 | A civic deployment does not create PRU, RAP, legal, market, or financial maturity. | Boundary | Admissible | Maturity-separation rule |
| CLAIM-PV-001 | Public value is distinct from financial value. | Methodological boundary | Admissible | Public-value method |
| CLAIM-PV-002 | Public-value indicators have zero automatic weight in PRU valuation. | Methodological boundary | Admissible | Separate evidence, legal and governance admission for any deviation |
| CLAIM-PV-003 | Programme activity does not prove outcome; outcome does not prove attribution. | Methodological boundary | Admissible | Baseline, comparator, causal method |
| CLAIM-PLOHE-001 | Public-Land OHE participation creates no land, harvesting, data, PRU, RAP, or revenue rights by itself. | Legal boundary | Admissible | Valid legal instrument |
| CLAIM-PLOHE-002 | A planted public site is not long-term regenerative infrastructure without maintenance and continuity. | Operational | Pilot-required | Multi-cycle maintenance evidence |
| CLAIM-SFF-001 | Child welfare and safeguarding precede programme and communications objectives. | Constitutional boundary | Admissible | Safeguarding framework |
| CLAIM-SFF-002 | Child-related data have zero weight in PRU, RAP, political profiling, token allocation, and governance authority. | Data and dignity boundary | Admissible | Data-governance controls |
| CLAIM-SFF-003 | Student participation does not establish educational or health outcomes. | Empirical boundary | Admissible | Educational outcome evidence |
| CLAIM-TSH-001 | Attendance and activity do not prove community cohesion, trust, inclusion, or resilience. | Public-value boundary | Admissible | PVM evidence |
| CLAIM-TSH-002 | A Town Square does not automatically represent the whole community. | Governance boundary | Admissible | Valid representative mandate |
| CLAIM-LCS-001 | Legal labels such as Community Land Trust, cooperative, covenant, or asset lock do not create protection by name alone. | Legal boundary | Admissible | Executed instruments and legal review |
| CLAIM-LCS-002 | Stewardship durability requires aligned title, use, duties, benefit, control, remedies, and succession. | Legal and governance | Specification-level | Operational and stress-test evidence |
| CLAIM-CDEC-001 | Ecological, beneficiary, educational, and stewardship data shall not be converted into voter or campaign data. | Privacy and electoral boundary | Admissible | CDEC controls |
| CLAIM-CDEC-002 | Public or community benefits shall not be conditioned on political behaviour. | Constitutional and legal boundary | Admissible | Electoral and public-resource compliance |
| CLAIM-RPCD-001 | Propagation and distribution counts are outputs only. | Empirical boundary | Admissible | Establishment and outcome evidence |
| CLAIM-RPCD-002 | Regenerative resources distributed through the programme remain unconditional benefits. | Constitutional boundary | Admissible | Operational scripts, data and electoral controls |
| CLAIM-RPCD-003 | Plant distribution does not establish food security, ecological performance, or political legitimacy. | Public-value boundary | Admissible | PVM and ecological evidence |
| CLAIM-CGP-001 | Completion of a Civic Genesis Pilot does not validate Prometheus. | Scientific boundary | Admissible | Independent scoped review |
| CLAIM-CGP-002 | Replication may be admitted only for defined components and contextual ranges supported by evidence. | Methodological boundary | Admissible | CGP replication decision |
| CLAIM-CGP-003 | Negative evidence, adverse events, and failure records are part of the admissible Pilot evidence package. | Scientific integrity | Admissible | Complete review package |

These claims are controlling seed entries.

Site-, programme-, partner-, jurisdiction-, cohort-, and outcome-specific claims require separate Claim IDs.

**Risk Architecture Integration**

The Risk Architecture shall include the following civic and territorial risks.

| **Risk ID** | **Risk** | **Failure mode** | **Minimum control** |
|----|----|----|----|
| R-CIV-01 | Political capture | Prometheus becomes identified with or controlled by one party, candidate, ideology, or administration | Political-independence boundary, entity and data separation, governance veto |
| R-CIV-02 | Civic claim inflation | Outputs, attendance, publicity, or political support are presented as outcomes or impact | PVM ladder, Claims Register, causal-claim review |
| R-CIV-03 | Public-value financialisation | Social, educational, or public benefit enters PRU or investor narratives without an admitted pathway | public_value_weight_in_PRU = 0; legal and methodological gate |
| R-CIV-04 | Public-resource misuse | Public land, staff, schools, vehicles, data, or charitable resources support unauthorised private or political purposes | public-resource inventory, cost allocation, legal review |
| R-CIV-05 | Maintenance abandonment | Sites are planted or inaugurated without durable care, water, budget, or operator continuity | maintenance runway, assigned owner, closure and transfer pathway |
| R-CIV-06 | Safeguarding failure | Children or vulnerable participants face physical, privacy, political, data, or reputational harm | safeguarding lead, incident pathway, data minimisation |
| R-CIV-07 | Community-legitimacy failure | A narrow group presents itself as representing an entire community | stakeholder mapping, scope disclosure, objection and participation pathway |
| R-CIV-08 | Legal-commons illusion | A legal label is presented as creating permanent protection, community ownership, or insolvency immunity | Rights and Responsibilities Map, legal opinion, stress test |
| R-CIV-09 | Electoral-data leakage | Civic, beneficiary, ecological, volunteer, or school records enter campaign or voter systems | CDEC firewall, access logs, data-flow review |
| R-CIV-10 | Benefit-for-support conduct | Plants, food, tools, education, or services are linked to political behaviour | unconditional-benefit rule, scripts, compliance review |
| R-CIV-11 | Biosecurity failure | Propagation distributes pests, pathogens, invasive material, or unsuitable varieties | batch provenance, inspection, quarantine and recall |
| R-CIV-12 | Volunteer dependency | Essential functions depend on informal and unstable unpaid labour | responsible operator, workload and replacement plan |
| R-CIV-13 | Replication overreach | A local pilot is copied without ecological, legal, cultural, or institutional adaptation | CGP bounded replication gate |
| R-CIV-14 | Data overcollection | Programme delivery becomes a reason to collect excessive personal, behavioural, political, or geospatial data | purpose limitation, minimisation, retention schedule |
| R-CIV-15 | Public-health overclaiming | Nature, food, gardening, education, or community programmes are represented as medical treatment | health-supportive boundary, clinical-review gate |
| R-CIV-16 | Procurement-readiness inflation | Public interest or pilot evidence is presented as grant-, procurement-, or programme-ready | eligibility review, authority pathway, Claims Register |
| R-CIV-17 | Governance capture through funding | Donors, lenders, public sponsors, vendors, or investors obtain undisclosed control | reserved-powers map, conflict register, anti-capture review |
| R-CIV-18 | Closure and asset-loss failure | Tools, data, funds, plants, or public assets are lost or privately appropriated when a programme closes | asset inventory, closure, reversion, custody and handover plan |

Each risk shall carry:

• owner;

• trigger;

• mitigation;

• residual-risk statement;

• review cadence;

• escalation pathway;

• applicable maturity ladder;

• Claims Register linkage where material.

**Red-Team Matrix Extension**

| **Attack surface** | **Adversarial question** | **Required response** |
|----|----|----|
| Political neutrality | Is Prometheus effectively infrastructure for one political movement? | Demonstrate independent canon, plural access, separate entities, systems, finance, data, and governance |
| Public value | Are social benefits being converted into financial claims without evidence? | Show PVM status, zero automatic PRU weight, causal and legal boundary |
| Public land | Who actually controls, owns, maintains, and may terminate the site? | Produce title, use instrument, Rights Map, maintenance and reversion provisions |
| Children and schools | Are children used for publicity, data extraction, research, or political communication? | Show safeguarding, consent, data, media, food-safety and political-neutrality controls |
| Community representation | Who authorised the hub to speak for the community? | Define constituency, process, mandate, dissent, scope, and limitations |
| Legal commons | Can creditors, founders, governments, or investors still seize or redirect the asset? | Provide insolvency, control, security-interest, asset-lock, and enforcement analysis |
| Plant distribution | Are distributed plants healthy, suitable, traceable, and legally movable? | Provide provenance, batch record, phytosanitary review, quarantine and recall |
| Pilot validity | Was the pilot designed to learn or to guarantee a positive story? | Produce pre-registration, baseline, comparator, stopping rules, adverse evidence |
| Replication | Which components are actually transferable and which are site-specific? | Produce adaptation envelope and bounded replication decision |
| Data dignity | Can participant data be used for voter, donor, commercial, or investor targeting? | Demonstrate purpose separation, prohibitions, access controls, deletion and incident response |
| Maintenance | What happens after funding, political attention, or volunteer enthusiasm ends? | Demonstrate operating runway, succession, closure and transfer plan |
| Public claims | Can an external reader distinguish output, outcome, attribution, and institutional admission? | Show claim class, maturity, permitted wording, uncertainty and next gate |

**Parallel Civic Implementation Track**

The existing Prometheus implementation phases remain controlling for the core evidence, PRU, RAP, legal, market, and runtime architecture.

The civic architecture adds a parallel track and does not renumber or replace the existing phases.

| **Civic phase** | **Required output** | **Gate** |
|----|----|----|
| C-0 — Constitutional boundary | Political independence, mission compatibility, dignity, and public-value separation | No civic partner may alter canonical meaning |
| C-1 — Territorial definition | Territory, community, institutions, land, need, actors, and deployment archetype | Scope and responsible parties identified |
| C-2 — Authority and custody | Land-use pathway, safeguarding, legal commons options, public-resource and data boundaries | No implementation without lawful authority |
| C-3 — Pilot design | Baseline, hypotheses, comparators, evidence plan, maintenance, budget, risk and Claims Register | Pre-registration before confirmatory analysis |
| C-4 — Operational pilot | Bounded implementation with assigned operators, safety, data, incident, and maintenance systems | Claims limited to implementation and observation |
| C-5 — Evidence cycle | Repeated ecological, operational, civic, educational, and public-value evidence | No outcome upgrade without evidence review |
| C-6 — Independent review | Scoped scientific, ecological, legal, safeguarding, governance, public-value, electoral, and operational review | Claims Register and maturity statuses updated |
| C-7 — Continuation decision | Continue, redesign, narrow, suspend, terminate, or replicate | Decision record and unresolved-risk disclosure |
| C-8 — Bounded replication | Replication package for admitted components and contexts | Every new territory begins its own local gate cycle |

A project may progress differently across the core and civic tracks. For example, a public programme may be:

• CTD-6 and PVM-5;

• PRU-R0;

• RAP-M4 or lower;

• LCS-4;

• CDEC-6;

• CGP-7.

Such a project may be a credible public programme without being a financial asset, PRU-eligible unit, or market-active structure.

**Capital and Public-Value Separation Rule**

Civic readiness, public-value maturity, legal commons maturity, and political acceptance shall not increase PRU value by default. The controlling expression remains:

public_value_weight_in_PRU = 0.

No CTD, PVM, PL-OHE, SFF, TSH, LCS, CDEC, RPCD, or CGP status creates:

• V_base;

• regenerative uplift;

• Conf_total;

• LegalGate;

• MRVGate;

• PRU-R maturity;

• RAP-M maturity;

• investor demand;

• market price;

• liquidity;

• collateral;

• revenue rights.

A civic or public-value component may become financially relevant only when:

1.  the underlying benefit is separately measurable;

2.  a recognised contractual, fiscal, procurement, insurance, crediting, revenue, or avoided-cost pathway exists;

3.  the payer, beneficiary, obligation, amount, period, and counterfactual are defined;

4.  evidence maturity is sufficient;

5.  no-double-counting is controlled;

6.  legal and accounting review admit the treatment;

7.  Claims Register wording permits the use;

8.  governance admits the use;

9.  the relevant financial or market gate independently closes.

**Additions to “What Prometheus Is Not”**

Prometheus is not:

• a political party or campaign platform;

• a voter, donor, persuasion, turnout, or political-profiling system;

• an authority for distributing public benefits according to political support;

• a universal representative of any community;

• a social-credit or human-worth system;

• a child, student, patient, beneficiary, or volunteer scoring system;

• a substitute for public-law authority, land title, concession, safeguarding, food-safety, procurement, election-law, or biosecurity compliance;

• a guarantee that public-land planting will become durable infrastructure;

• a guarantee that a school garden will improve educational or health outcomes;

• a guarantee that a Town Square will create trust, cohesion, or economic development;

• a guarantee that distributed seeds or plants will establish, produce food, or create food security;

• a Community Land Trust, cooperative, commons, asset lock, or stewardship covenant merely because those terms are used;

• a mechanism for converting public value automatically into PRU value, financial value, or investor rights;

• validated because a civic pilot has been completed;

• replication-ready because a pilot received positive publicity or political support.

**Additions to “What Must Be True for Prometheus to Work”**

For civic and territorial use:

• political independence must remain substantive, not merely nominal;

• local communities and affected stakeholders must have legitimate and reviewable participation pathways;

• public-land and institutional use must be lawfully authorised;

• maintenance responsibility, funding, water, operator capacity, and continuity must be credible;

• child and vulnerable-participant safeguarding must precede programme and communications goals;

• public value must be measured separately from financial value;

• outputs, outcomes, attribution, institutional admission, and financial meaning must remain distinct;

• public, charitable, school, and community resources must be protected from political and private capture;

• ecological, beneficiary, educational, volunteer, and civic data must remain separated from voter, donor, campaign, and political-targeting systems;

• legal commons must align title, use, stewardship, benefit, control, remedies, insolvency, and succession;

• propagated and distributed biological material must be identifiable, traceable, suitable, and subject to biosecurity controls;

• pilots must preserve negative evidence and be capable of stopping;

• replication must be bounded by ecological, legal, institutional, cultural, and evidence conditions;

• no civic maturity ladder may substitute for evidence, PRU, RAP, legal, market, or runtime maturity.

**Civic and Territorial Glossary**

| **Term** | **Canonical meaning** | **Is not** |
|----|----|----|
| Civic deployment | A bounded Prometheus application involving communities, institutions, territory, public resources, or public value | Political endorsement or financial maturity |
| CTD | Civic and Territorial Deployment readiness ladder | PRU-R, RAP-M, or evidence maturity |
| Public value | Evidence-supported benefit serving a legitimate public, community, ecological, educational, cultural, or intergenerational purpose | Automatic financial value |
| PVM | Public Value Maturity ladder | PRU valuation or public ROI |
| Public-Land OHE | An OHE deployed under a lawful public, institutional, educational, charitable, or community-use pathway | Ownership or permanent land entitlement |
| School Food Forest | A child-safe educational regenerative deployment | Automatic educational or health impact |
| Town Square / Community Hub | A governed civic operating environment for defined community functions | Automatic representation of the community |
| Legal Commons Stack | The rights, duties, custody, governance, benefit, enforcement, and succession architecture supporting commons or stewardship | Legal protection through branding |
| Civic neutrality | Substantive institutional independence from party, candidate, or electoral control | Absence of public-policy relevance |
| CDEC | Civic Data and Electoral Compliance maturity ladder | Electoral endorsement or voter identity |
| RPCD | Regenerative Propagation and Civic Distribution readiness ladder | Food-security or ecological-performance evidence |
| Unconditional benefit | A legitimate programme resource not conditioned on unrelated political, commercial, media, data, or financial behaviour | Unrestricted or ungoverned distribution |
| CGP | Civic and Territorial Genesis Pilot maturity ladder | Prometheus-wide validation |
| Bounded replication | Replication of defined components within an evidenced and approved contextual envelope | Universal scalability |
| Public Claims Register | Claims Register fields and controls governing civic and public-value statements | Political-message approval |
| Community legitimacy | A documented and bounded process through which affected people may participate, object, review, or hold responsibilities | Universal community consent |
| Stewardship custody | Legally and operationally aligned custody of purpose, rights, duties, assets, data, continuity, and remedies | Moral intention alone |

**Civic Final Acceptance Checklist**

A Prometheus release containing civic or territorial architecture is acceptable only when:

• all civic protocols are registered under one canonical hierarchy;

• Civic Neutrality remains a constitutional boundary;

• mission compatibility is not described as political affiliation or endorsement;

• CTD, PVM, PL-OHE, SFF, TSH, LCS, CDEC, RPCD, CGP, PRU-R, and RAP-M remain distinct;

• no civic maturity status upgrades PRU-R or RAP-M;

• public_value_weight_in_PRU remains zero by default;

• public-value claims distinguish aspiration, objective, theory, output, outcome, attribution, review, and institutional admission;

• civic claim fields are integrated into the Claims Register;

• public, political, educational, health, community, and propagation claims have permitted and prohibited wording;

• public-land deployments identify title, authority, use, access, maintenance, produce, data, incident, and termination pathways;

• School Food Forest deployments include safeguarding, privacy, accessibility, food safety, operator responsibility, and holiday maintenance;

• Town Square deployments include access, governance, treasury, market, tool, food, data, political, conflict, maintenance, and closure controls;

• Legal Commons structures include a Rights and Responsibilities Map;

• no legal label implies permanence, community ownership, asset lock, insolvency protection, or enforceability beyond the legal opinion;

• civic, ecological, school, beneficiary, volunteer, and public-value data remain separated from voter and campaign systems;

• public and community benefits remain unconditional with respect to political behaviour;

• propagation programmes include identity, provenance, batch, biosecurity, logistics, guidance, recall, and failure controls;

• distribution counts remain outputs only;

• Civic Genesis Pilots are pre-registered;

• no pilot improvement claim exists without baseline;

• no superiority or attribution claim exists without an appropriate causal method;

• negative evidence and adverse events are retained;

• stopping and suspension rules are defined;

• replication decisions identify invariants, adaptations, site-specific conditions, and unvalidated elements;

• public resources, charitable resources, donations, volunteer labour, and in-kind support are recorded;

• maintenance and operating runway are not omitted from cost claims;

• children and vulnerable populations are never used as investor, political, reputational, or promotional instruments;

• AI remains non-authoritative in civic participation, safeguarding, political inference, public-value admission, and community representation;

• no dashboard, poll, consultation, assembly, testimonial, or social-media metric is treated as community consent or causal evidence;

• the Risk Architecture includes civic, political, public-resource, safeguarding, biosecurity, maintenance, legal-commons, data, and replication risks;

• the current civic gate status is disclosed;

• the lowest unresolved civic, ecological, legal, safeguarding, evidence, political, operational, market, or financial gate controls the permitted claim.

**Current Canonical Civic Gate Status**

The Prometheus Civic and Territorial Architecture is closed at specification level only.

The following have been specified:

• political-independence and mission-compatibility boundaries;

• civic and territorial deployment architecture;

• public-value and civic-claims architecture;

• Public-Land OHE protocol;

• School Food Forest and child-safeguarding protocol;

• Town Square and Community Hub operating system;

• Legal Commons and Stewardship Custody Stack;

• civic-data and electoral-compliance protocol;

• regenerative-propagation and civic-distribution protocol;

• Civic and Territorial Genesis Pilot framework;

• maturity ladders;

• Claims Register integration;

• risk and acceptance controls.

The following remain implementation-open:

• actual site authority;

• jurisdiction-specific legal review;

• executed land-use or custody instruments;

• operating entities and responsible operators;

• safeguarding admission;

• public-resource review;

• electoral and political-interaction review;

• propagation-node and biosecurity validation;

• pilot pre-registration;

• baseline collection;

• comparator implementation;

• operational data;

• independently reviewed outcomes;

• public-value admission;

• legal-commons stress testing;

• bounded replication approval.

The current admissible statement is:

Prometheus has specified a civic and territorial deployment architecture.

The following statements are not yet admissible unless separately documented:

• the civic architecture is empirically validated;

• the public-land model is operationally proven;

• the School Food Forest model improves educational or health outcomes;

• the Town Square model improves cohesion, resilience, or local economic performance;

• the Legal Commons model is legally effective in a specific jurisdiction;

• the civic-data architecture is independently compliant in a specific electoral context;

• the propagation system creates food security;

• the Civic Genesis Pilot is successful;

• the civic architecture is replication-ready;

• civic or public value contributes to PRU or RAP value.

**Canonical Civic Reconciliation Invariant**

Prometheus may expand from sites to communities and territories.

It shall not weaken its evidence discipline in doing so.

It may recognise public value.

It shall not automatically financialise public value.

It may cooperate with political actors.

It shall not become politically controlled.

It may use public land and public resources.

It shall not convert them into private or partisan assets.

It may involve children, families, schools, communities, and vulnerable populations.

It shall not turn them into data products, scoring subjects, political instruments, or investor narratives.

It may create legal structures for stewardship.

It shall not confuse legal terminology with enforceable protection.

It may distribute regenerative biological capacity.

It shall not confuse distribution with food security or ecological success.

It may conduct ambitious civic pilots.

It shall not design the evaluation to guarantee a favourable conclusion.

The final rule is:

local legitimacy before territorial deployment;

lawful authority before public use;

safeguarding before participation;

baseline before improvement;

causal method before attribution;

public-value evidence before public-value claims;

legal instruments before rights;

maintenance before infrastructure status;

independent review before replication;

no civic narrative may bypass the Prometheus canon.

# Verification-first Positioning

Prometheus treats verification as infrastructure.

Regeneration becomes financeable when living systems are observed with integrity, measured with discipline, verified with transparency, and structured with responsibility.

Every claim matures into value only through evidence, provenance, integrity, and admissibility.

**The foundational sequence is:**

claim\
evidence\
provenance\
integrity\
admissibility\
value.

This sequence protects the system from narrative inflation while allowing verified regeneration to become economically legible.

Prometheus therefore represents value only when regenerative performance has become observable, traceable, verifiable, and admissible under defined rules.

In this sense, Prometheus is structured first as regenerative infrastructure.

Digital instruments, financial instruments, and market-facing representations may emerge only as compliant expressions of verified value.

The central thesis is that value should increase when the living systems that generate value become healthier, more resilient, more productive, and more verifiably regenerative over time.

Prometheus is the architecture through which that increase can be observed, protected, measured, governed, and translated into a new form of capital-ready regenerative infrastructure.

# Abstract

Human civilization is entering a period in which economic stability, ecological stability, technological coordination, and human coherence can no longer be treated as separate domains.

The systems that sustain value are the same systems that sustain life: soil, water, biodiversity, climate regulation, biological productivity, human health, community trust, and long-term territorial resilience.

For much of modern economic history, these living foundations have been treated as externalities. Their degradation has often remained invisible within financial accounts until it reappeared as systemic cost: declining soil fertility, water stress, biodiversity loss, chronic disease, supply-chain fragility, disaster exposure, insurance pressure, and social fragmentation.

Prometheus begins from a different premise.

Living systems are not background conditions for the economy. They are productive infrastructure.

The h•eart•h Prometheus framework introduces a regenerative infrastructure model through which ecological regeneration, distributed intelligence, human stewardship, verification systems, and capital formation are integrated into a single operational architecture.

Its purpose is to make regeneration observable, measurable, verifiable, governable, financeable, and eventually representable through compliant institutional or digital instruments.

At the core of this architecture lies the One Human Ecosystem (OHE): a minimum deployable unit of regenerative infrastructure based on syntropic food-forest design, soil restoration, water-cycle stabilization, biodiversity enhancement, nutritional capacity, and measurable ecological performance.

The OHE functions as the biophysical base layer of Prometheus.

It is the place where regeneration occurs, where evidence is generated, where human stewardship becomes operational, and where living systems begin to produce measurable increases in ecological coherence.

Prometheus then translates this biophysical performance through a verification-first architecture.

Field sensors, laboratory analyses, satellite data, mobile observations, ecological surveys, operational records, and human audits contribute to an evidence fabric in which claims mature into observations, observations mature into verified indicators, and verified indicators may become financially admissible value.

This process is essential.

Regeneration becomes financeable only when it can be observed with integrity, measured with discipline, verified with transparency, and structured with responsibility.

The Prometheus Regenerative Unit (PRU) is introduced as institutional interface between regeneration and capital through which verified regenerative performance becomes comparable, underwritable, risk-assessable, and capital-admissible.

PRUs do not replace ecological reality.

They translate verified ecological performance into a structured form that capital can understand without detaching value from the living systems that generate it.

Regenerative Asset Pools (RAPs) extend this logic by aggregating verified PRUs into portfolio structures capable of supporting diversification, institutional underwriting, risk analysis, and future compliant financial representation.

**In this architecture, value formation follows a clear sequence:**

living system\
regenerative process\
measured evidence\
verified indicator\
PRU value-analysis layer\
portfolio structure\
capital allocation.

This sequence distinguishes Prometheus from sustainability narratives, speculative token systems, and conventional financial abstraction.

Prometheus is structured first as regenerative infrastructure.

Digital instruments, governance tokens, market interfaces, and financial products may emerge only as expressions of verified value, legal structuring, and institutional discipline.

The framework also integrates agent-centric digital infrastructure, Valueflows / REA-compatible accounting, governance protocols, MRV discipline, anti-greenwashing controls, no-double-counting logic, and pilot-first implementation boundaries.

These layers are intentionally separated in order to preserve semantic clarity, auditability, operational correctness, and capital-market readability as the system evolves.

Prometheus does not claim to create biological intelligence.

It creates the conditions through which the intelligence of living systems can become observable, protected, coordinated, verified, and translated into durable forms of economic value.

The central thesis of this White Paper is that verified regeneration can become a new real-world asset class when ecological performance is measured, validated, standardized, risk-assessed, legally structured, and connected to disciplined capital formation.

In this sense, Prometheus represents a transition from extractive value accounting to regenerative capital architecture.

It proposes a system in which capital is no longer allocated primarily toward the depletion of living foundations, but toward their restoration, stabilization, and long-term productive capacity.

The future belongs to infrastructures that can heal while they produce.

Prometheus is designed to become one of them.

**Team and Governance Transparency**

Capital-facing Prometheus materials require a public governance transparency schedule. The schedule identifies responsible stewards, technical maintainers, legal reviewers, release custodians, conflict-of-interest disclosures, treasury controllers and independent reviewers. No founder allocation, contributor allocation, token allocation, treasury schedule, compensation plan, lock-up, vesting arrangement or key-custody structure is final unless approved through a signed governance decision record and applicable legal review.

| **Transparency item** | **Minimum disclosure before capital-facing use** |
|----|----|
| Roles and mandates | Named or role-based accountability for governance, MRV, legal, technical, capital formation and release custody. |
| Conflicts register | Disclosure of financial, advisory, family, vendor, token, land, investment or governance conflicts. |
| Key custody | Independent custody map for release keys, admin credentials, treasury keys and emergency recovery materials. |
| Token and treasury policy | No public allocation claim without legal review, decision record, vesting/lock-up schedule and treasury-risk controls. |
| Reviewer independence | At least three independent signatures for canonical release, technical release or capital-facing methodology release. |

# Executive Summary

**The structural transition**

Human civilization is entering a phase in which the stability of economic systems can no longer be separated from the stability of the living systems that sustain them.

Soil fertility, water cycles, biodiversity, climate regulation, biological productivity, nutritional quality, human health, and community coherence are not peripheral conditions. They are the foundational infrastructure beneath every durable economy.

Modern economic systems have generated extraordinary productive capacity, technological sophistication, and global integration. Yet this expansion has often depended on architectures that convert living systems into extractive inputs while treating their degradation as external cost.

The result is a growing structural imbalance.

Capital continues to flow toward downstream mitigation, crisis response, health burdens, insurance losses, supply-chain fragility, and ecological restoration, while upstream regenerative capacity remains undercapitalized.

Prometheus is designed to address this imbalance by creating an architecture through which regeneration can become observable, measurable, verifiable, governable, financeable, and institutionally scalable.

Its purpose is not to place a financial label on nature.

Its purpose is to align capital with the regenerative capacity of living systems.

**A regenerative infrastructure framework**

Prometheus introduces a new category of infrastructure: verified regenerative infrastructure.

This infrastructure is not limited to land restoration, ecological monitoring, digital coordination, or regenerative finance as separate activities. It integrates them into a single architecture capable of transforming living-system performance into evidence-based, financially legible, and institutionally admissible value.

Within the Prometheus framework, regeneration is treated as a productive process.

**Regenerative systems can:**

• increase soil fertility and biological productivity;

• stabilize hydrological cycles and water availability;

• restore biodiversity and ecosystem resilience;

• improve nutritional quality and food-system stability;

• reduce exposure to ecological and climatic volatility;

• strengthen community health, trust, and long-term territorial resilience.

These outcomes represent forms of value that have historically remained difficult to measure, verify, compare, underwrite, and finance.

Prometheus addresses this limitation by establishing the technical, governance, verification, and financial conditions through which regenerative processes can become observable, auditable, and capital-admissible.

**The One Human Ecosystem**

At the foundation of the Prometheus architecture lies the One Human Ecosystem, or OHE.

The OHE is the minimum deployable unit of regenerative infrastructure.

It is conceived as a syntropic food-forest and ecological production system capable of integrating nutrition, soil regeneration, water retention, biodiversity enhancement, biomass cycling, human stewardship, and measurable ecological performance within a compact territorial unit.

The OHE is not merely a productive land parcel.

It is a biophysical unit through which regeneration becomes operational.

It is where ecological processes occur, where data is generated, where stewardship becomes visible, and where living systems begin to produce measurable increases in coherence, resilience, and productive capacity.

When replicated and connected across landscapes, OHE units can form local clusters, bioregional networks, and eventually regenerative infrastructure portfolios.

In this sense, the OHE functions as the germ cell of the Prometheus system: small enough to be deployable, rich enough to be measurable, and structured enough to become financeable.

**Verification as infrastructure**

Prometheus treats verification as infrastructure.

Regeneration becomes financeable when ecological performance can be observed with integrity, measured with discipline, verified with transparency, and structured with responsibility.

**The system therefore distinguishes between different levels of informational maturity:**

claim;

observation;

verified indicator;

financially admissible value.

A statement about regeneration does not become value merely because it is coherent, desirable, or aligned with sustainability language.

It becomes value only when it is linked to evidence, provenance, integrity, confidence, and admissibility.

Prometheus is designed to integrate multiple evidence sources, including field sensors, laboratory analyses, satellite imagery, drone or mobile observations, ecological surveys, climate data, soil data, biodiversity records, operational records, and human or third-party audits.

No single source is treated as absolute.

Value emerges through convergence, validation, confidence scoring, and disciplined interpretation.

This verification-first architecture protects the system from narrative inflation while enabling genuine regenerative performance to become economically legible.

**The Prometheus Regenerative Unit**

The Prometheus Regenerative Unit, or PRU, is the value-analysis interface of the system.

Its function is to translate verified regenerative performance into a structured unit that can be compared, underwritten, classified by maturity, aggregated, monitored, and connected to capital allocation.

The PRU does not replace ecological reality.

It does not create value independently from living systems.

It translates verified ecological performance into a financially interpretable form while preserving the connection between value and the regenerative processes that generate it.

A PRU may incorporate multiple dimensions of performance, including soil function, hydrological resilience, biodiversity, productive stability, biomass dynamics, input-dependency reduction, MRV integrity, and confidence-adjusted evidence.

The purpose of the PRU is to create a disciplined bridge between regeneration and finance.

Through this bridge, capital can begin to evaluate regenerative infrastructure not as a vague impact claim, but as a measurable and risk-assessable real-world asset.

**Regenerative Asset Pools**

**RAP Demand, Liquidity, and Offtake Discipline**

A Regenerative Asset Pool is portfolio-readable before it is market-readable. Portfolio aggregation, diversification and risk analysis do not by themselves prove demand, liquidity, pricing or exit. A RAP becomes capital-facing only after demand evidence, legal structure, custody path, data-room completeness and counterparty readiness have been documented.

| **Activation condition** | **Required documentation** |
|----|----|
| Buyer or offtake evidence | Letter of intent, procurement mandate, buyer qualification, public-program demand, insurer/bank/family-office interest, or documented market thesis. |
| Price discovery | Pricing memo separating ecological performance, cash-flow assets, credits, public incentives, optional upside and scenario-only values. |
| Liquidity path | Hold period, exit route, refinancing pathway, secondary-market limitation, redemption limitation and illiquidity disclosure. |
| Counterparty readiness | Banking, custody, KYC/AML, regulated exchange or transfer-agent pathway where applicable. |
| Investor protection | Risk factors, no-guarantee language, no-yield assumption without cash-flow source, and clear distinction between analysis and legal rights. |

**Anchor Demand File and Market-Readiness Boundary**

No RAP may be described as market-ready, investable, liquid, buyer-validated, offtake-backed, insurance-recognised, procurement-ready, secondary-market-ready or portfolio-deployable unless an Anchor Demand File has been completed, reviewed and linked to the Claims Register.

Portfolio aggregation does not prove demand.

Diversification does not prove liquidity.

PRU maturity does not prove buyer appetite.

Risk modelling does not prove exit.

A data room does not create a market.

A counterparty conversation does not create offtake.

A non-binding indication does not create liquidity.

Market-facing language requires documented external validation.

Prometheus distinguishes four market-readiness states:

1.  Portfolio-readable\
    A RAP is internally structured for evidence aggregation, diversification, reporting, risk review and diligence preparation. No external market actor is required.

2.  Counterparty-reviewable\
    A qualified external counterparty has documented non-binding interest, review, feedback, procurement relevance, insurer review, bank review, public-program pathway or equivalent institutional engagement.

3.  Legally wrapped\
    A jurisdiction-specific legal, institutional, contractual or public-private structure exists or is review-ready, with rights, disclosures, custody or settlement pathway, eligibility rules and legal boundaries documented.

4.  Market-active\
    A binding financing, offtake, insurance, procurement, public-program, institutional, contractual or legally enforceable arrangement has closed or become effective.

No Prometheus material may collapse these stages.

A RAP without an Anchor Demand File remains internal, analytical and non-market-facing.

A RAP with a non-binding Anchor Demand File may become counterparty-reviewable, but not market-active.

A RAP with a legal wrapper may become capital-facing only within the applicable legal, disclosure, eligibility, custody, settlement and jurisdictional boundaries.

A RAP becomes market-active only when external demand, legal structure, counterparty readiness, disclosure and governance admission converge in a binding or operationally effective pathway.

The Anchor Demand File shall include, at minimum:

1.  target counterparty category;

2.  counterparty qualification record;

3.  use case;

4.  demand rationale;

5.  documented interest, letter of interest, memorandum of understanding, procurement indication, insurer feedback, bank feedback, public-program pathway, public-private pathway or equivalent counterparty review;

6.  price-discovery memo;

7.  separation of ecological performance value, productive cash-flow value, credits, public incentives, contractual revenue, optional upside and scenario-only values;

8.  liquidity limitation disclosure;

9.  exit limitation disclosure;

10. hold-period assumption;

11. regulated counterparty pathway where applicable;

12. custody and settlement pathway where applicable;

13. legal review status;

14. data-room maturity score;

15. governance approval;

16. Claims Register update.

Permitted counterparty categories may include:

- research institutions;

- catalytic funders;

- philanthropic capital;

- public procurement bodies;

- municipalities and public agencies;

- insurers or reinsurers;

- banks and project-finance partners;

- family offices;

- ecological credit buyers where legally valid;

- food-system partners;

- hospitality or territorial-resilience partners;

- public-private resilience programs;

- regulated investment counterparties where legally admitted.

Each counterparty category requires its own claim boundary, legal review, disclosure standard and evidence threshold.

The absence of market validation must be disclosed.

If no suitable external counterparty exists in a given jurisdiction, asset class, pilot stage or legal structure, a RAP may remain portfolio-readable or counterparty-reviewable without being market-active.

No forced progression to market-active status is permitted.

The purpose of the Anchor Demand File is not to manufacture demand.

Its purpose is to document whether demand exists, what kind of demand exists, which claims it can support, which claims it cannot support, and which missing conditions prevent market-facing use.
