**P.2.7 External Human-Learning and Psychophysiology Research References — Non-Admissible**

The following references are preserved solely as external research context for voluntary human-learning, attention, emotional regulation, stress physiology and stewardship-training inquiry. They are non-controlling references and do not form part of the Prometheus core methodology, PRU value analysis, Conf_total, MRV admissibility, RAP underwriting, governance authority, investor-facing claims, clinical claims, public-health claims or financial representation.

These references may inform separate consent-based, ethics-reviewed research modules only. They may not be used to support ecological performance, regenerative value, PRU uplift, investor materials, medical claims, participant ranking, biometric scoring, governance eligibility or capital-facing admissibility.

• HeartMath Institute. Science of heart coherence. <https://www.heartmath.org/science/>\
• Elbers, J., & McCraty, R. (2025). From dysregulation to coherence: Exploring the HeartMath® approach to emotional and physiological regulation. Global Advances in Integrative Medicine and Health, 14. <https://doi.org/10.1177/27536130251408821>

# Appendix Q: Token Boundary, Interoperability, and Future Integration Registry

**Q.1 Purpose and Boundary**

**Q.1A Investment-contract and securities-law review gate**

Disclaimers do not determine legal classification. Any PRU, RAP, TRBK, token, note, fund interest, revenue right, governance right, access right, digital representation or bundled pathway that is offered to third parties must pass jurisdiction-specific legal review before capital-facing use.

| **Risk lens** | **Prometheus treatment** | **Required gate** |
|----|----|----|
| Contribution of money or value | Any capital contribution, membership payment, token purchase, service fee or portfolio investment must be classified by legal counsel. | Securities, consumer, tax and AML/KYC review where applicable. |
| Common enterprise | OHE, PRU, RAP, operator, DAO, SPV or portfolio structures may create common-enterprise analysis depending on facts. | Jurisdiction-specific opinion before offer or solicitation. |
| Expectation of profit | Yield, return, appreciation, liquidity, asset-backed or investment-performance language is prohibited unless legally structured and disclosed. | Public-claim review and offering-document control. |
| Efforts of others | Earth Custodians, Regenerative Operators, Digital Guarantors and protocol stewards may be material managerial actors. | Rights mapping, risk factors and legal wrapper before investor use. |

Legal classification must be based on facts, rights, marketing, economic reality and jurisdiction. Prometheus therefore treats Howey-style analysis, EU/MiCA/MiFID-style analysis and local securities-law analysis as mandatory gates, not as optional disclaimers.

Prometheus may interact with token systems, digital assets, settlement rails, governance tokens, access tokens, contribution records, accounting units, stablecoins, Holochain-native assets, HoloFuel-related infrastructure, TRBK, verifiable credentials, digital identity systems, or future legally structured instruments. However, token interoperability must remain strictly bounded.

A token is not regeneration, not evidence, not MRV, not PRU, not RAP, not land, not food, not soil, not biodiversity, not human dignity, not constitutional authority.

A token is not investor exposure unless a compliant legal instrument expressly creates that exposure.

A token is not a right merely because it is transferable.

The purpose of this appendix is to define how Prometheus may evaluate, classify, document, monitor, and potentially integrate token systems without collapsing ecological value, governance participation, legal rights, financial exposure, external human-learning research data, community meaning, or source-layer authority into a single speculative object.

Tokens may be useful, may coordinate access, may support settlement, may record contribution, may signal participation, may represent legally structured rights where such rights already exist, may connect communities, protocols, DAOs, registries, and future compliant digital-instrument pathways.

But tokens must remain instruments. They do not become the source of truth, not the source of value, not become Prometheus.

The core principle is: tokens may represent bounded functions;

they may not replace evidence, law, governance, MRV, legal structure, human dignity, or the source layer.

**Q.1B Legal Opinion Package and Rights-Mapping Requirement**

**Q.1B.1 Purpose**

The Legal Opinion Package is the mandatory legal-admissibility gate for any Prometheus pathway that may become capital-facing, investor-facing, token-facing, instrument-facing, revenue-facing, land-rights-facing, governance-rights-facing or market-facing.

Its purpose is to prevent conceptual collapse between:

- ecological performance;

- evidence;

- PRU analysis;

- RAP maturity;

- governance participation;

- token representation;

- access rights;

- operational transaction capacity;

- investor exposure;

- legal rights;

- financial instruments;

- market claims.

Prometheus may define architecture, methodology, governance, evidence pathways and conditional legal pathways.

Prometheus may not create enforceable investor rights, financial rights, land rights, revenue rights, redemption rights, custody rights, token rights or market rights without valid legal instruments.

**Q.1B.2 Controlling Legal Rule**

The controlling legal rule is:

No legal opinion, no rights claim.

No instrument, no investor entitlement.

No wrapper, no capital-facing structure.

No counsel review, no offering language.

No disclosure pack, no third-party reliance.

No jurisdictional analysis, no market-facing use.

Disclaimers do not determine legal classification.

Governance approval does not cure securities-law risk.

Token labels do not determine token classification.

Utility language does not eliminate investment-contract analysis.

Non-transferability does not automatically eliminate regulatory risk.

Transferability does not automatically create investor rights.

A dashboard does not create legal status.

A registry entry does not create enforceability.

A methodology does not create an asset.

Legal classification must be based on facts, rights, obligations, marketing, economic reality, transferability, reliance, counterparties, jurisdiction and intended use.

**Q.1B.3 Legal Opinion Package Contents**

Any capital-facing, investor-facing, token-facing, instrument-facing, revenue-facing, land-rights-facing or market-facing implementation of PRU, RAP, TRBK, HoloFuel, digital representations or bundled rights shall require a Legal Opinion Package.

The Legal Opinion Package shall include:

1.  PRU legal-characterisation memo;

2.  RAP structure memo;

3.  TRBK governance and participation memo;

4.  HoloFuel operational-boundary memo;

5.  token classification memo where applicable;

6.  securities-law classification;

7.  MiCA/MiFID-style analysis where applicable;

8.  Howey-style analysis where applicable;

9.  local securities-law analysis;

10. tax treatment memo;

11. AML/KYC and sanctions screening memo where applicable;

12. investor-rights mapping;

13. land-rights mapping;

14. revenue-rights mapping;

15. governance-rights mapping;

16. access-rights mapping;

17. custody and settlement analysis;

18. data protection and privacy review;

19. cross-border enforceability analysis;

20. bankruptcy-remoteness analysis where applicable;

21. disclosure and risk-factor pack;

22. marketing-language restriction memo;

23. counsel sign-off;

24. governance approval;

25. Claims Register update.

A legal opinion may be conditional.

A conditional legal opinion must disclose assumptions, excluded jurisdictions, unresolved facts, pending regulatory issues, missing instruments and prohibited language.

**Q.1B.4 Rights-Mapping Requirement**

Each Prometheus pathway must be mapped before external use.

| **Layer** | **Possible meaning** | **Required legal treatment** |
|----|----|----|
| PRU | Analytical representation of verified regenerative performance | No rights unless legally instrumented |
| PRU reference | Methodological or data-room reference | Non-investment unless legally structured |
| RAP | Portfolio-readable aggregation of verified PRU logic | No liquidity or investor exposure without wrapper |
| RAP unit | Possible fund, SPV, note, membership or other interest | Legal classification required |
| TRBK | Governance, coordination, access or participation layer | No investor entitlement unless legally created |
| HoloFuel | Operational transaction and infrastructure layer | No PRU value or investor exposure by itself |
| Access Covenant | Source-layer participation and conduct boundary | Requires legal expression for enforceability |
| Dashboard status | Display or review state | Not legal status |
| Claims Register status | Claim admissibility state | Not legal right |
| Token representation | Digital representation within defined context | Classification depends on rights attached |
| Legal wrapper | Rights-bearing structure | Must be counsel-reviewed and jurisdiction-specific |

No material may collapse these layers into a single speculative, investment, ownership, yield-bearing, asset-backed, redemption-bearing or transferable rights object.

**Q.1B.5 PRU Legal Boundary**

PRU is an analytical interface.

It may describe verified regenerative performance when the evidence gate is satisfied.

It may support comparison, maturity classification, reviewability and capital-readability.

It does not by itself create:

- land rights;

- cash-flow rights;

- revenue rights;

- governance rights;

- redemption rights;

- securities exposure;

- asset ownership;

- fund interest;

- debt claim;

- equity claim;

- insurance claim;

- public-procurement claim;

- tax claim;

- token value;

- liquidity;

- market price.

Any PRU-related external use must state:

PRU is a value-analysis interface, not a legal instrument, not an investment product, not a security, not a fund interest, not a land right, not a cash-flow right and not a guarantee of ecological or financial performance unless expressly transformed through a valid legal instrument.

**Q.1B.6 RAP Legal Boundary**

RAP is a portfolio-structuring concept.

It may aggregate verified PRU logic across sites, maturity stages, operators, ecological contexts, revenue profiles and evidence packages.

It does not by itself create:

- a fund;

- an SPV;

- a note;

- a security;

- liquidity;

- redemption;

- transferability;

- investor rights;

- asset backing;

- regulated status;

- secondary market;

- custody protection;

- settlement rights.

A RAP becomes legally relevant only when structured through a jurisdiction-specific instrument.

Any RAP unit, fund interest, membership interest, note, revenue participation, tokenised representation or investment product must pass legal classification, disclosure, investor-suitability and custody or settlement review before external use.

**Q.1B.7 TRBK Legal Boundary**

TRBK may function as governance, coordination, access, participation, reputation or contribution infrastructure only within its legally admitted scope.

TRBK does not create:

- ownership of Prometheus;

- ownership of PRU;

- ownership of RAP;

- claim on land;

- claim on revenue;

- investor entitlement;

- profit expectation;

- redemption right;

- voting control over constitutional meaning;

- securities exposure;

- asset backing.

Any expansion of TRBK functionality into transferable value, economic participation, revenue linkage, access monetisation, investment exposure or rights-bearing representation requires legal review and governance approval.

Token-weighted governance may not determine constitutional validity.

Governance participation is not investor entitlement.

**Q.1B.8 HoloFuel Legal Boundary**

HoloFuel, where referenced, is an operational transaction and infrastructure layer within agent-centric systems.

HoloFuel does not create PRU value.

HoloFuel does not create RAP maturity.

HoloFuel does not create ecological truth.

HoloFuel does not create governance authority.

HoloFuel does not create investor rights.

HoloFuel does not create financial exposure unless a valid legal instrument expressly creates such exposure.

Any settlement, transaction, accounting, exchange, bridge, custody or value-transfer use involving HoloFuel-related infrastructure must pass technical, legal, tax, AML/KYC, custody and operational review.

**Q.1B.9 Marketing and Language Control**

Prometheus materials shall not use any of the following terms for PRU, RAP, TRBK, HoloFuel, dashboards, credentials or future representations unless legally admitted:

- investment;

- investable;

- yield;

- return;

- appreciation;

- ownership;

- asset-backed;

- guaranteed;

- insured;

- liquid;

- redeemable;

- tradable;

- security;

- fund;

- note;

- share;

- bond;

- certified value;

- regulated product;

- approved instrument;

- protected investor exposure.

Permitted pre-legal-review language includes:

- architecture;

- framework;

- methodology;

- analytical interface;

- pilot;

- research;

- evidence pathway;

- governance pathway;

- legal structuring requirement;

- future compliant pathway;

- non-investment participation;

- capital-readability condition.

Any market-facing document shall require public-claim review and legal-language control.

**Q.1B.10 Investor Protection and Disclosure Boundary**

No Prometheus capital-facing material may be circulated unless it includes, where relevant:

1.  no-offer statement;

2.  no-investment-advice statement;

3.  no-guarantee statement;

4.  no-liquidity statement;

5.  no-redemption statement;

6.  PRU analytical-interface statement;

7.  RAP non-market-ready statement where applicable;

8.  token non-rights statement where applicable;

9.  ecological-performance risk;

10. operational risk;

11. legal and regulatory risk;

12. tax risk;

13. liquidity risk;

14. valuation risk;

15. governance risk;

16. AI and runtime non-authority statement;

17. data and MRV limitations;

18. jurisdictional limitation;

19. investor eligibility where applicable;

20. counsel review status.

Disclosure does not create compliance by itself.

Disclosure is a minimum precondition for review, not a substitute for legal structuring.

**Q.1B.11 Acceptance Criteria**

Gate 2 is specification-closed when the following artefacts exist:

1.  Legal Opinion Package index;

2.  PRU legal-characterisation template;

3.  RAP legal-structure template;

4.  TRBK boundary memo;

5.  HoloFuel boundary memo;

6.  securities-classification checklist;

7.  MiCA/MiFID-style review template where applicable;

8.  Howey-style review template where applicable;

9.  rights-mapping tables;

10. marketing-language restriction policy;

11. risk-factor template;

12. counsel sign-off template;

13. legal data-room index;

14. legal decision record.

Gate 2 is legally closed only when:

1.  counsel has reviewed the relevant pathway;

2.  jurisdiction-specific opinion exists;

3.  rights are mapped;

4.  instruments are drafted where required;

5.  disclosures are reviewed;

6.  marketing language is approved;

7.  governance has admitted the pathway;

8.  Claims Register status has been updated.

Specification closure is not legal closure.

Legal closure is not market validation.

Market validation is not liquidity.

**Q.1B.12 Go / No-Go Rule**

Go:

Prometheus may continue to develop architecture, methodology, pilot protocols, legal templates, rights maps, data-room structures and non-investment materials while Gate 2 is specification-closed.

Conditional Go:

Prometheus may discuss future legal pathways only as conditional, non-offering, non-investment structuring scenarios.

No-Go:

Prometheus may not describe PRU, RAP, TRBK, HoloFuel, dashboards, certificates, digital records, credentials, governance participation or access pathways as creating rights before legal review and instrument formation.

Prometheus may not circulate market-facing, investor-facing, token-facing or rights-facing materials without legal-language review.

Prometheus may not imply regulatory clearance, investor protection, liquidity, yield, redemption, ownership or asset backing without legal admission.

**Q.1B.13 Gate 2 Status Statement**

Gate 2 is closed at specification level when the Legal Opinion Package structure, rights-mapping tables, marketing-language policy, disclosure template, counsel sign-off template, legal data-room index and decision record are complete.

Gate 2 remains open at legal-effect level until counsel has produced jurisdiction-specific legal opinions and the relevant instruments have been drafted, reviewed and admitted.

The correct status is:

Legally gated.

Not yet legally admitted.

Not yet rights-bearing.

Not yet investor-facing.

Not yet market-facing.

Not yet token-facing.

Not yet instrument-ready.

Prometheus may define the pathway.

Only legal instruments may create enforceable rights.

**Q.1C No-Double-Spending, Bridge and Wrapped Representation Gate**

**Q.1C.1 Purpose**

This section defines the technical and institutional boundary for any Prometheus pathway involving token representation, bridge logic, wrapped representation, cross-chain transfer, external registry synchronization, oracle dependency, custody rail, settlement rail, digital credential, public API, dashboard state or multi-ledger reporting.

Its purpose is to prevent double-counting, double-claiming, double-representation and digital double-spending across ecological, legal, financial, registry and digital systems.

Prometheus may evaluate digital representations.

Prometheus may design interoperability pathways.

Prometheus may maintain registry mappings.

Prometheus may define future token-facing scenarios.

Prometheus may not admit any token-facing, bridge-facing, settlement-facing, registry-facing or cross-chain pathway unless uniqueness, lineage, authority, finality, revocation, conflict resolution and Claims Register status are controlled.

**Q.1C.2 Controlling Rule**

The controlling rule is:

No uniqueness, no representation.

No lineage, no admissibility.

No threat model, no bridge.

No finality model, no settlement claim.

No oracle review, no oracle reliance.

No lock / mint / burn / release proof, no wrapped representation.

No conflict-resolution rule, no external registry sync.

No Claims Register update, no public reliance.

No Prometheus digital representation may be treated as valid merely because it exists on a blockchain, registry, dashboard, API, credential system, bridge contract, custody system or external ledger.

A blockchain transaction does not prove ecological uniqueness.

A bridge event does not prove admissibility.

A token wrapper does not prove valid representation.

An oracle does not create ecological truth.

A custody record does not create PRU validity.

A dashboard state does not create legal or market status.

A registry entry does not prevent incompatible reuse.

A cross-chain message does not prove that the source event remains valid, unspent, unrevoked and unclaimed elsewhere.

**Q.1C.3 Representation Boundary**

The following objects must remain distinct:

| **Object** | **Meaning** | **Boundary** |
|----|----|----|
| Regenerative event | Underlying ecological, operational or stewardship event | Source of evidence, not itself a financial instrument |
| Evidence package | Provenance-linked record supporting a claim | Must remain linked to method, source, time, location and reviewer trail |
| Verified indicator | Admissible metric derived from evidence | May support PRU analysis only within admissibility scope |
| PRU reference | Analytical reference to verified regenerative performance | Not transferable value by itself |
| RAP reference | Portfolio-readiness or market-readiness reference | Not liquidity or investor exposure by itself |
| Token representation | Digital representation within a defined legal and technical context | Valid only if rights, uniqueness, transfer logic and legal status are admitted |
| Wrapped representation | Representation of a representation across systems | Prohibited unless lock / mint / burn / release and conflict controls are proven |
| Bridge message | Cross-system transfer or synchronization signal | Not valid unless source state, target state, finality and replay protection are verified |
| Oracle input | External data input into digital logic | Assistive only unless source, integrity, latency and manipulation risk are reviewed |
| Registry entry | Public or private record of status | Does not create admissibility, legal status or market readiness by itself |

No document may collapse these objects into a single speculative, transferable, yield-bearing, asset-backed, duplicate-proof or legally settled digital asset.

**Q.1C.4 No-Double-Spending Threat Model Requirement**

Any token-facing, bridge-facing, wrapped-representation, external-registry, custody, settlement or cross-chain pathway shall require a No-Double-Spending Threat Model before external use.

The threat model shall include, where applicable:

1.  underlying event identifier;

2.  spatial boundary;

3.  temporal boundary;

4.  process boundary;

5.  evidence package identifier;

6.  method hash;

7.  data hash;

8.  Claims Register entry;

9.  PRU reference namespace;

10. RAP reference namespace;

11. legal-instrument reference where applicable;

12. token or digital-representation identifier;

13. source-system identifier;

14. target-system identifier;

15. registry identifier;

16. lock / mint / burn / release rule where applicable;

17. finality assumption;

18. replay-protection mechanism;

19. oracle dependency;

20. bridge dependency;

21. custody dependency;

22. duplicate-claim detection rule;

23. conflict-resolution rule;

24. revocation pathway;

25. quarantine pathway;

26. adverse integrity record;

27. governance review;

28. legal review where applicable.

If any required element is absent, unresolved, untested or inconsistent with the Claims Register, the pathway remains non-admitted.

**Q.1C.5 Bridge, Wrapper and Cross-Chain Boundary**

Prometheus shall not treat bridge-based or cross-chain interoperability as a default pathway for PRU, RAP, token, investor, insurance, procurement or market-facing use.

Bridge, wrapper or cross-chain use is prohibited unless the following conditions are satisfied:

1.  source event uniqueness is established;

2.  evidence lineage is preserved;

3.  source state and target state are verifiable;

4.  finality assumptions are documented;

5.  replay protection exists;

6.  duplicate minting or duplicate representation is prevented;

7.  lock / mint / burn / release logic is tested where applicable;

8.  oracle dependencies are reviewed;

9.  cross-chain message validation is tested;

10. failure and rollback scenarios are documented;

11. registry conflict rules are defined;

12. revocation and quarantine procedures exist;

13. legal treatment is reviewed;

14. governance has admitted the pathway;

15. Claims Register status supports the representation.

No wrapped PRU, wrapped RAP, bridged PRU, bridged RAP, tokenised PRU, tokenised RAP, cross-chain regenerative asset, bridge-ready credential or transferable ecological representation may be described as admitted before these conditions are satisfied.

**Q.1C.6 Oracle and External Data Boundary**

Oracles and external data feeds may support observation, synchronization, price reference, weather reference, registry lookup, custody status, settlement confirmation or external validation only within a defined scope.

An oracle is not a source of ecological truth.

An oracle is not a legal opinion.

An oracle is not MRV validation.

An oracle is not PRU admissibility.

An oracle is not RAP market-readiness.

An oracle is not investor protection.

Any oracle-dependent pathway shall disclose:

1.  oracle source;

2.  data type;

3.  update frequency;

4.  latency;

5.  failure mode;

6.  manipulation risk;

7.  fallback source;

8.  reviewer responsibility;

9.  governance override rule;

10. Claims Register dependency.

Oracle failure, manipulation, delay, inconsistency or disputed output shall trigger quarantine where it may affect PRU, RAP, legal, market, token, custody or settlement status.

**Q.1C.7 Prohibited Language**

The following language is prohibited unless supported by the No-Double-Spending Threat Model, legal review, security review, governance decision and Claims Register status:

- bridge-ready;

- cross-chain-ready;

- interoperable value;

- transferable PRU;

- transferable RAP;

- tokenised PRU;

- tokenised RAP;

- wrapped regenerative asset;

- duplicate-proof;

- double-spend-proof;

- oracle-verified;

- registry-synchronised;

- settlement-ready;

- custody-ready;

- externally redeemable;

- cross-chain collateral;

- legally transferable digital representation;

- finality-guaranteed;

- bridge-secured;

- multi-chain asset.

Permitted pre-admission language includes:

- interoperability thesis;

- bridge-risk analysis;

- no-double-spending design requirement;

- external-registry mapping;

- oracle-risk review;

- cross-system threat model pending;

- duplicate-claim controls under review;

- token representation not yet admitted;

- external settlement not yet admitted.

**Q.1C.8 Acceptance Criteria**

Gate 4 is specification-closed for Appendix Q when the following artefacts exist:

1.  No-Double-Spending Threat Model template;

2.  bridge-risk checklist;

3.  oracle-risk checklist;

4.  wrapped-representation checklist;

5.  external-registry synchronization checklist;

6.  finality and replay-protection memo template;

7.  lock / mint / burn / release test template where applicable;

8.  duplicate-claim detection rule;

9.  conflict-resolution rule;

10. revocation and quarantine procedure;

11. adverse integrity record template;

12. Claims Register seed entries.

Gate 4 is implementation-closed only when the specific pathway has been tested, security-reviewed, legally reviewed where applicable, red-teamed, versioned, admitted by governance and linked to Claims Register status.

Specification closure is not implementation safety.

Implementation safety is not legal admission.

Legal admission is not market validation.

Market validation is not liquidity.

**Q.1C.9 Go / No-Go Rule**

Go:

Prometheus may research interoperability, define threat models, map registry relationships, design duplicate-claim controls, prepare oracle-risk reviews and document future token-facing pathways as non-admitted architecture.

Conditional Go:

Prometheus may test bridge, wrapper, oracle, registry or settlement pathways in sandbox environments when clearly labelled as non-production, non-capital-facing, non-investor-facing and non-market-facing.

No-Go:

Prometheus may not represent any PRU, RAP, token, digital credential, bridge message, registry entry, custody record, settlement record or dashboard state as transferable, settled, duplicate-proof, double-spend-resistant, bridge-ready, cross-chain-ready, market-admissible or legally admitted unless the corresponding threat model, security review, legal review, governance decision and Claims Register status support that claim.

**Q.1C.10 Status Statement**

Gate 4 is closed at specification level when the no-double-spending boundary, bridge-risk controls, oracle-risk controls, wrapped-representation controls, duplicate-claim detection rule, conflict-resolution pathway, quarantine process and Claims Register seeds are complete.

Gate 4 remains open at implementation level until real pathways have been tested, reviewed, red-teamed and admitted.

The correct status is:

Interoperability boundary specified.

Bridge use not assumed.

Oracle reliance not authoritative.

Wrapped representation not admitted by default.

Cross-chain readiness not presumed.

No token-facing or settlement-facing pathway without threat model.

No duplicated value.

**Q.2 Token Boundary Doctrine**

Prometheus should maintain functional separation among all token-relevant domains. This separation prevents category collapse. Category collapse occurs when one technical object is allowed to impersonate multiple legal, ecological, governance, financial, or constitutional meanings at once. Prometheus may integrate multiple systems. It may not allow one system to impersonate all systems.

| **Domain** | **Meaning** | **Boundary** |
|----|----|----|
| MRV | Measurement, Reporting, and Verification of evidence | MRV produces and reviews evidence; it does not create rights by itself |
| PRU | Methodology-bound analysis of verified regenerative performance | PRU is evidence-derived and review-bound; it is not automatically a token |
| RAP | Regenerative Asset Pool logic based on eligible PRU-relevant units | RAP is portfolio logic; it requires legal, evidentiary, governance, and reporting structure before capital use |
| TRBK | Potential governance, coordination, access, contribution, or community token within defined boundaries | TRBK does not automatically create PRU value, land rights, investor rights, MRV validity, or constitutional authority |
| HoloFuel / infrastructure capacity | Possible transaction, hosting, or infrastructure-capacity layer within Holochain-compatible systems | Operational utility does not create ecological value, investor entitlement, or PRU maturity |
| Stablecoin / fiat settlement rail | Payment, settlement, treasury, or transaction mechanism | Settlement does not create regeneration, governance authority, or verified impact |
| Security token / note / fund interest | Regulated or legally structured financial instrument | Rights arise from legal documents, disclosures, jurisdictional review, and investor protections |
| Access credential | Permission, membership, or eligibility record | Access does not create ownership, financial entitlement, or PRU value |
| Contribution record | Record of work, stewardship, participation, or role performance | Contribution may support recognition or review; it does not automatically create value rights |
| Reputation badge / credential | Signal of role, achievement, credential, or contribution | Must not become human ranking, social-credit machinery, or automatic authority |
| human-state research-related signal | Voluntary human-state or coherence-related data | human-state research never becomes token value, human ranking, MRV proof, PRU value, or financial entitlement |
| Canonical registry record | Status record for release, fork, compatibility, or public verification | Registry status supports verification; it does not create rights unless a separate legal structure does so |

**The doctrine is simple:** connect systems technically; separate meanings constitutionally.

**Q.3 Token Classification Framework**

Any token, credential, digital unit, settlement asset, or blockchain-based representation seeking Prometheus interaction should be classified before integration. Classification comes before integration. Integration without prior classification creates governance ambiguity, legal uncertainty, public-claim risk, and potential source-layer contamination.

| **Classification** | **Description** | **Prometheus implication** |
|----|----|----|
| Utility / access token | Grants access to a function, service, permission layer, or community process | Requires scope definition and no implied investor right |
| Governance token | Participates in defined governance processes | Requires governance boundary, anti-capture design, and constitutional limits |
| Contribution token | Recognizes contribution, participation, work, stewardship, or service | Requires contribution-policy clarity and no automatic PRU, RAP, or investor conversion |
| Settlement token | Used for payment, settlement, or transaction accounting | Requires compliance, custody, tax, AML/KYC, sanctions, and operational review where relevant |
| Accounting unit | Tracks internal values, balances, credits, points, or records | Requires accounting policy and non-misrepresentation controls |
| Reward token | Provides rewards, incentives, or distribution logic | Requires legal, tax, labor, consumer, securities, and community-governance review |
| Security / investment token | Represents financial rights, revenue, debt, equity, fund interest, note, or similar exposure | Requires full legal structuring, disclosure, investor suitability, and jurisdictional compliance |
| Reputation token / badge | Signals achievement, role, credential, contribution, or community status | Must not become human ranking or social-credit machinery |
| Verifiable credential | Confirms identity, role, qualification, authorization, or review status | Must remain purpose-limited and revocable where appropriate |
| MRV-linked representation | References evidence, indicators, ecological performance, or reporting records | Requires MRV admissibility and careful public-claim controls |
| PRU-referenced representation | References PRU analysis, maturity, or reporting | Requires reviewed PRU methodology, admissibility, legal boundary, and controlled language |
| RAP-referenced representation | References portfolio or pool logic | Requires eligible underlying units, legal wrapper, disclosure, and portfolio governance |
| Non-admissible token claim | Claims regenerative, PRU, RAP, investor, legal, or public certification meaning without basis | Not eligible for protected Prometheus pathways |

A token may be technically sophisticated and constitutionally irrelevant.

A token may be liquid and legally unsafe.

A token may be popular and methodologically empty.

Prometheus compatibility requires more than code.

It requires source-layer compatibility.

**Q.4 Token Integration Readiness Review**

Before Prometheus interacts with any token system, the following review dimensions should be assessed.

| **Review dimension** | **Key question** |
|----|----|
| Purpose | What function does the token perform? |
| Rights | What rights, if any, does the token legally confer? |
| Claims | What public, ecological, financial, governance, or community claims are being made? |
| Legal classification | Is the token a security, payment token, utility token, reward, credential, accounting unit, access token, or something else under relevant law? |
| Jurisdiction | Which jurisdictions are involved? |
| Issuer / controller | Who issues, controls, upgrades, burns, mints, pauses, or administers it? |
| Governance | How are token decisions made? |
| Concentration risk | Are rights or influence concentrated among insiders, founders, investors, whales, custodians, or automated agents? |
| Treasury risk | How are reserves, treasuries, revenues, proceeds, or protocol funds governed? |
| Bridge risk | Are cross-chain or cross-system integrations involved? |
| Custody risk | Who holds assets, credentials, wallets, keys, treasury controls, or admin privileges? |
| MRV link | Does the token claim ecological performance, verification, impact, or evidence linkage? |
| PRU / RAP link | Does the token imply PRU value, PRU maturity, RAP exposure, portfolio exposure, or investor rights? |
| human-state research link | Does the token interact with human-state, biometric, coherence, health, behavioral, or sensitive personal data? |
| AI / automation | Can AI systems mint, issue, allocate, vote, modify, classify, pause, or alter token rights? |
| Disclosure | Are limitations, risks, exclusions, and rights clearly disclosed? |
| Source-layer compatibility | Does the token preserve Prometheus constitutional boundaries? |
| Public-claim boundary | Could the token’s marketing language mislead communities, investors, partners, or public users? |
| Exit and incident response | What happens if the token, bridge, issuer, key, treasury, or claim becomes unsafe? |

A token may be technically compatible and constitutionally incompatible.

Prometheus compatibility requires both.

**Q.5 Integration Status Categories**

A token system may receive one of several Prometheus-facing statuses.

Status should be versioned, time-bound where appropriate, reviewable.

No token should receive permanent compatibility by default.

| **Status** | **Meaning** |
|----|----|
| Observed | Token is known to Prometheus but not integrated |
| Research / sandbox | Token may be studied or tested in controlled, non-sensitive conditions |
| Compatible for limited use | Token may interact with specific non-sensitive functions under defined limits |
| Operationally compatible | Token may support defined operational workflows after technical, governance, and legal review |
| Governance-compatible | Token may participate in defined governance functions without overriding source-layer constraints |
| Settlement-compatible | Token may support payment or settlement functions where legally reviewed |
| MRV-reference compatible | Token may reference MRV records only under strict admissibility and public-claim controls |
| PRU-reference compatible | Token may reference PRU analysis only where PRU methodology, evidence, admissibility, and language boundaries are reviewed |
| RAP-reference compatible | Token may reference RAP logic only where legal wrapper, eligible units, disclosures, and portfolio governance exist |
| Investor-structure compatible | Token may be used only inside a compliant legal wrapper with disclosures and jurisdiction-specific review |
| Review required | Additional legal, technical, MRV, governance, or constitutional review is needed |
| Non-admissible | Token claim or pathway cannot enter protected Prometheus pathways |
| Quarantined | Token presents serious legal, ecological, financial, technical, governance, custody, bridge, or source-layer risk |
| Revoked | Previous compatibility status has been withdrawn due to risk, misuse, drift, breach, or changed conditions |

Compatibility is not a trophy.

It is a monitored status.

**Q.6 Interoperability Architecture**

Prometheus may support interoperability with multiple technical and legal systems.

**Potential integration layers may include:**

• Holochain-compatible records;

• Valueflows / REA-compatible accounting structures;

• conventional databases for dashboards;

• legal documents for enforceable rights;

• data-room records for diligence;

• fiat payment rails;

• regulated stablecoin or electronic-money rails where legally available;

• token-gated access systems;

• verifiable credentials;

• decentralized identifiers;

• APIs;

• bridge adapters;

• accounting exports;

• custody or settlement providers;

• institutional reporting systems;

• public verification registries.

Interoperability does not imply equivalence.

A bridge may move information or value across systems.

It does not merge the legal meaning of those systems.

A record in one system may reference another.

It does not automatically inherit its authority.

A settlement rail may move money.

It does not verify regeneration.

A credential may prove access.

It does not create ownership.

A blockchain record may preserve a timestamp.

It does not establish admissibility.

**The interoperability principle is:**

connect systems technically;

separate meanings constitutionally;

verify status publicly;

route protected-domain actions through review.

**Q.7 Bridge and Cross-System Risk Controls**

Bridge architecture is one of the highest-risk areas in token systems.

Prometheus should treat bridges, wrappers, synthetic assets, mapped tokens, cross-ledger references, tokenized representations, proof bridges, oracle feeds, and cross-system rights references as protected-domain integrations.

**Risks include:**

• double counting;

• unauthorized minting;

• false collateralization;

• bridge exploit;

• liquidity illusion;

• legal misclassification;

• asset mismatch;

• governance mismatch;

• custody failure;

• key compromise;

• replay attack;

• synthetic PRU claim;

• unsupported RAP exposure;

• false investor exposure;

• user misunderstanding;

• jurisdictional ambiguity;

• public-claim drift.

**Bridge integration should require:**

• technical audit;

• legal review;

• custody review;

• rights mapping;

• disclosure;

• source-layer compatibility check;

• emergency pause pathway;

• incident response;

• user-facing claim control;

• no-double-counting controls;

• canonical registry reference;

• post-incident review pathway.

**The correct bridge rule is:**

bridges may transmit records, references, settlement instructions, or legally defined rights only according to the rights actually created, disclosed, and legally structured.

A bridge cannot manufacture legitimacy.

**Q.8 Token Integration Registry**

Prometheus may maintain a Token Integration Registry.

The registry should record token status, classification, approved use, prohibited use, review status, risk class, and public limitations.

| **Registry field** | **Purpose** |
|----|----|
| Token name / symbol | Identifies the token |
| Issuer / controller | Identifies who controls issuance, upgrades, governance, treasury, or administration |
| Network / infrastructure | Identifies the technical substrate |
| Token classification | Utility, governance, settlement, contribution, credential, security, or other |
| Prometheus status | Observed, sandbox, limited, operational, review-required, non-admissible, quarantined, revoked |
| Approved use | Defines permitted Prometheus-facing functions |
| Prohibited use | Defines what the token may not claim or perform |
| Legal review status | Records whether jurisdictional review has occurred |
| MRV relationship | Defines whether and how the token may reference evidence |
| PRU relationship | Defines whether and how the token may reference PRU analysis |
| RAP relationship | Defines whether and how the token may reference RAP logic |
| Governance relationship | Defines whether token participates in governance and under what limits |
| Custody / treasury notes | Records key custody, treasury, reserve, or admin-control considerations |
| Bridge exposure | Records whether bridge, wrapper, cross-chain, or synthetic exposure exists |
| AI / automation exposure | Records whether AI systems can influence allocation, review, minting, governance, or rights |
| Risk class | Low, moderate, high, or critical |
| Review date | Defines when status must be reviewed |
| Public disclosure link | Links to public limitations and risk disclosures |
| Canonical registry reference | Links to official Prometheus status verification |

The Token Integration Registry should be part of the broader Global Canonical Registry where public status verification is appropriate.

This allows any user, investor, community, partner, reviewer, institution, or AI agent to verify whether a token claim is Prometheus-recognized, limited, under review, non-admissible, quarantined, revoked, or misleading.

**Q.9 Token Claims and Public Communication**

Prometheus should tightly classify token-related language.

**Token communication should distinguish:**

• access;

• contribution;

• recognition;

• settlement;

• governance participation;

• utility;

• accounting;

• reward;

• investment exposure;

• PRU reference;

• RAP exposure;

• legal right;

• public benefit claim;

• certification status;

• canonical status.

**The following claims require enhanced review:**

• backed by PRU;

• linked to verified regeneration;

• RAP exposure;

• impact-backed;

• yield;

• return;

• revenue share;

• ownership;

• real asset;

• security;

• credit;

• offset;

• guaranteed;

• certified;

• MRV-verified;

• Prometheus-approved;

• Prometheus-canonical;

• Holochain-native value;

• AI-validated impact;

• regenerative asset-backed.

Such language should not be used unless the relevant evidence, legal structure, disclosures, methodology, governance authorization, and jurisdictional review exist.

Token language is powerful because markets can move faster than truth.

Prometheus therefore makes token language reviewable.

**The public-claim rule is:**

if token language can influence capital, governance, public trust, ecological claims, or community expectations, it belongs in a review pathway.

**Q.10 PRU, RAP, TRBK, HoloFuel, and Token Separation**

PRU, RAP, TRBK, HoloFuel, settlement rails, access credentials, and legally structured instruments must remain functionally distinct.

A PRU is evidence-derived analysis.

A RAP is portfolio logic.

TRBK may support coordination, access, contribution, participation, or governance within defined boundaries.

HoloFuel-related infrastructure may support Holochain ecosystem hosting or transaction capacity.

A settlement rail moves payment or value.

A token is a technical, legal, or economic instrument.

A legal instrument creates rights.

These may interact only through defined pathways.

| **Interaction** | **Permitted only if** |
|----|----|
| **Token references PRU** | PRU methodology exists, evidence is admissible, claim is reviewed, and public language is bounded |
| **Token references RAP** | RAP structure exists, underlying units are eligible, legal wrapper exists, and disclosures are reviewed |
| **Token represents investor exposure** | Securities, fund, debt, equity, revenue-right, note, or legally structured framework exists and is disclosed |
| **Token participates in governance** | **Governance domain is defined and bounded by source-layer constraints** |
| **Token grants access** | Access scope is defined and does not imply ownership, PRU value, or investor rights |
| **Token rewards contribution** | **Labor, tax, securities, consumer, and community-governance issues are reviewed** |
| **Token supports settlement** | Payment, custody, AML/KYC, tax, sanctions, and operational issues are reviewed where applicable |
| **Token uses Holochain-related infrastructure** | Operational function is defined and does not imply ecological value, PRU status, or investor exposure |

**The separation rule is:**

PRU may inform value;

RAP may aggregate eligible units;

TRBK may support bounded ecosystem functions;

HoloFuel-related infrastructure may support operational capacity;

settlement rails may move payment;

tokens may represent legally and technically defined functions;

none of them may impersonate the others.

**Q.11 Token Boundary and Legal Wrapper Function**

Prometheus may interact with tokens, digital credentials, access systems, governance signals, contribution records, settlement rails, or future legally structured digital instruments.

However, token representation must remain downstream from evidence, methodology, governance, legal structure, disclosure, and jurisdictional review.

**The token boundary may be expressed as:**

**Token ≠ Value**

A token does not create ecological value.

A token does not create PRU value.

A token does not create RAP exposure.

A token does not create land ownership.

A token does not create investor rights.

A token does not create constitutional authority.

A token does not create human worth.

A token may represent a defined function only when that function already exists through evidence, governance, legal structure, contract, disclosure, or technical authorization.

**The correct Prometheus sequence is:**

Evidence → Methodology → Admissibility → Legal Wrapper → Defined Rights → Possible Compliant Representation

This sequence protects the system from premature tokenization.

**A compliant representation may be considered only when:**

T_valid = E_v · M_a · A_r · L_w · D_s · C_j

**Where:**

• T_valid = representation-validity condition;

• E_v = evidence verified;

• M_a = methodology approved or applicable;

• A_r = admissibility reviewed;

• L_w = legal wrapper established;

• D_s = disclosure satisfied;

• C_j = jurisdiction-specific compliance reviewed.

If any required factor is zero, the representation is not valid for protected Prometheus pathways.

If T_valid = 0, representation is non-admissible for financial, PRU, RAP, investor-facing, or rights-bearing use.

This does not prevent research.

It does not prevent sandbox testing.

It does not prevent community discussion.

It does not prevent technical experimentation.

It prevents premature claims.

The relationship among PRU, RAP, TRBK, HoloFuel, legal instruments, and land rights may be expressed as:

**PRU ≠ TRBK ≠ HoloFuel ≠ RAP ≠ Security ≠ Land Right ≠ Governance Authority**

Each layer has a distinct function.

PRU represents methodology-bound verified regenerative performance.

RAP represents portfolio aggregation logic.

TRBK may represent governance, coordination, access, contribution, or participation functions within defined boundaries.

HoloFuel-related infrastructure may support Holochain ecosystem hosting or operational transaction capacity.

A security, note, fund interest, revenue right, debt instrument, equity interest, or investment instrument exists only where a legal structure expressly creates it.

Land rights arise from property law, contracts, trusts, titles, leases, easements, licenses, or other legal instruments.

Governance authority arises from role, mandate, process, source-layer constraints, and governance rules.

No token may impersonate all of these layers.

**The token boundary rule is:**

tokens may represent defined functions;

they may not manufacture the underlying right.

A token may reference a PRU only if the PRU exists under methodology and evidence.

A token may reference RAP exposure only if a RAP has been legally and institutionally structured.

A token may support governance only within defined and bounded governance scope.

A token may support contribution records only under contribution-policy rules.

A token may support access only within defined access conditions.

A token may support investor exposure only where a legally compliant instrument creates such exposure.

**The operational pathway is:**

claim → evidence → review → legal meaning → disclosure → representation

not:

token → value → legitimacy.

**Prometheus therefore separates:**

symbol from substance;

record from right;

access from ownership;

governance signal from constitutional authority;

community participation from investor exposure;

verified regeneration from speculative token value.

This separation protects Prometheus, Tribeke, future partners, investors, communities, and stewards from category collapse.

Formula boundary.

T_valid is a methodological and legal-readiness condition.

It does not create automatic token issuance, investor rights, securities, legal enforceability, governance authority, PRU value, RAP exposure, land rights, or market price.

It defines the internal Prometheus boundary for evaluating whether a representation may enter protected pathways.

Formal consequences require the relevant legal, governance, technical, MRV, token, and source-layer review pathway.

Operational rule.

No legal wrapper, no rights-bearing token.

No MRV admissibility, no PRU-backed claim.

No source-layer fidelity, no Prometheus token legitimacy.

Prometheus may use tokens.

Prometheus may interoperate with token ecosystems.

Prometheus may collaborate with Tribeke.

Prometheus may support future compliant digital instruments.

But tokens remain instruments.

They do not become the source of truth.

They do not become the source of value.

They do not become Prometheus.

**Q.12 TRBK Boundary**

TRBK may be relevant to Tribeke, community coordination, governance, access, contribution, or ecosystem participation depending on its actual legal, technical, and governance structure.

Prometheus may interact with TRBK only through sovereign alignment and clearly scoped interoperability.

**TRBK should not be presumed to represent:**

• Prometheus constitutional authority;

• PRU value;

• RAP exposure;

• land ownership;

• investor rights;

• securities exposure;

• human worth;

• MRV validity;

• external human-learning research appendix status;

• automatic governance power over Prometheus.

Prometheus should respect Tribeke’s own governance and token architecture.

The correct relationship is not absorption.

It is interoperability with source-layer boundaries.

**Possible TRBK-Prometheus interfaces may include:**

• access or coordination functions;

• contribution records;

• community participation pathways;

• role-based permissions where legally and technically appropriate;

• governance signaling within defined scope;

• non-binding community sentiment;

• settlement or reward mechanisms where legally reviewed;

• future structured integration through legal and governance review.

**The TRBK principle is:**

Tribeke may remain sovereign;

Prometheus may remain sovereign;

interoperability may occur through alignment, not collapse.

**Q.13 Governance Token Boundary**

Token-mediated governance may exist only within defined domains.

**A governance token may support:**

• signaling;

• proposal prioritization;

• access to forums;

• role nomination;

• non-binding sentiment;

• bounded decision processes;

• treasury or community processes within legal limits;

• contribution recognition where legally reviewed.

**A governance token may not override:**

• foundational articles;

• Eternity Clauses;

• Access Covenant;

• human dignity;

• MRV methodology;

• PRU admissibility;

• RAP eligibility;

• legal requirements;

• external human-learning research appendix privacy and consent;

• constitutional interpretation;

• source-layer non-bypassability;

• protected-domain review.

**The correct governance-token principle is:**

token governance may coordinate participation;

constitutional governance preserves the source layer.

**Q.14 Verification-Network Terminology**

Prometheus should avoid casual reliance on the term oracle where it implies that truth is delivered automatically by a technical feed.

The stronger language is verification network, review network, evidence source, data provider, or methodology reviewer, depending on context. A verification network may include:

• field stewards;

• scientists;

• auditors;

• laboratories;

• remote-sensing analysts;

• local ecological knowledge holders;

• community observers;

• methodology reviewers;

• technical reviewers;

• legal or reporting reviewers where necessary.

The network does not declare truth magically.

It reviews evidence.

It validates methods.

It assesses confidence.

It identifies uncertainty.

It supports admissibility.

This language is scientifically and institutionally stronger.

Prometheus does not need oracles.

It needs accountable verification.

**Q.15 Token Penalty and Slashing Terminology**

Prometheus should avoid casual use of slashing, forfeiture, automatic token loss, or automatic economic penalties.

**Such terms may be appropriate only where:**

• a legally structured staking mechanism exists;

• rights and risks are clearly disclosed;

• participants expressly agree;

• jurisdictional review has occurred;

• due process or review pathway is defined;

• the technical system is implemented and audited;

• the consequence is proportionate;

• appeal or dispute process exists where appropriate.

**In ordinary Prometheus governance, the preferred consequence vocabulary is:**

• review-required;

• restricted;

• suspended pending review;

• non-admissible;

• quarantined;

• access-limited;

• certification revoked;

• compatibility revoked;

• public correction required;

• legal escalation where necessary.

**The token consequence principle is:**

economic penalties require legal structure;

governance consequences require due process;

source-layer violations require canonical response.

No section, appendix, technical example, API specification, governance model, token model, or implementation note should imply automatic TRBK slashing, forfeiture, token loss, or economic penalty unless a separate legally reviewed instrument expressly creates such a mechanism, with disclosure, consent, due process, technical audit, and jurisdiction-specific compliance.

**Q.16 AI and Token Systems**

AI or ASI systems may assist with token-risk review, classification, anomaly detection, compliance issue spotting, bridge-risk analysis, public-claim review, and registry consistency checks.

**AI may not autonomously:**

• issue tokens;

• mint tokens;

• burn tokens;

• slash tokens;

• modify token rights;

• certify token compatibility;

• approve token governance participation;

• authorize PRU or RAP linkage;

• approve investor-facing token language;

• classify a token as legally compliant;

• determine human eligibility through token data;

• change registry status;

• create legal rights.

**AI-generated token analysis should be classified as:**

• draft;

• analysis;

• risk flag;

• review-required;

• non-admissible;

• recommendation.

Protected-domain token decisions require legal, governance, technical, MRV, token, and source-layer review.

A superintelligence may produce excellent analysis.

It may not manufacture authorization.

**Q.17 Token Security and Custody**

**Any token integration should consider:**

• key management;

• custody model;

• multi-signature requirements;

• threshold signing;

• hardware security modules where appropriate;

• key rotation;

• compromised-key response;

• treasury controls;

• smart contract audit;

• bridge audit;

• permissions;

• admin keys;

• emergency pause function;

• insider risk;

• user protection;

• incident response;

• insurance where available;

• public disclosure of material risks.

Token systems often fail through operational control, not philosophy.

Prometheus therefore treats custody and administration as governance-critical infrastructure.

**Q.18 Token Data Privacy**

Token data may appear public, but interpretation of token data can still create privacy and profiling risks. Prometheus should avoid:

• behavioral profiling without consent;

• wallet wealth as trust proxy;

• transaction history as moral rank;

• automated access decisions based only on wallet data;

• doxxing;

• unnecessary identity cross-linking;

• using token history to infer political, spiritual, medical, financial, behavioral, or personal attributes.

Token data should be used with purpose limitation. Wallets are not souls with addresses.

**Q.19 Future Token Integration Pathway**

A future token integration should follow a controlled sequence.

| **Step** | **Action** | **Purpose** |
|----|----|----|
| 1 | Intake | Token project or proposed integration is described |
| 2 | Classification | Token function, rights, claims, and status are classified |
| 3 | Source-layer check | Compatibility with foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, Access Covenant, and canonical glossary is assessed |
| 4 | Legal review | Jurisdictional, securities, tax, consumer, data, AML/KYC, sanctions, and contractual implications are reviewed where relevant |
| 5 | Technical review | Infrastructure, bridge, custody, security, and implementation risks are reviewed |
| 6 | MRV / PRU / RAP review | Any ecological, PRU, RAP, or impact claims are assessed |
| 7 | Governance review | Governance implications and anti-capture risks are assessed |
| 8 | Disclosure drafting | Public language, limitations, and risks are drafted |
| 9 | Sandbox | Integration is tested in limited, non-sensitive scope |
| 10 | Decision | Status is assigned, limited, operational, rejected, non-admissible, quarantined, or review-required |
| 11 | Registry entry | Token status is published or recorded where appropriate |
| 12 | Monitoring | Integration is reviewed periodically |

This pathway preserves innovation without letting token complexity outrun constitutional clarity.

**Q.20 Minimal Token Integration Registry Schema**

**A Token Integration Registry entry may include the following illustrative structure:**

json\
{\
"token_id": "token_registry_entry_0001",\
"name": "Token Name",\
"symbol": "TKN",\
"issuer_or_controller": "entity_or_protocol_reference",\
"network": "network_or_infrastructure_reference",\
"classification": "utility \| governance \| contribution \| settlement \| security \| credential \| other",\
"prometheus_status": "observed \| research_sandbox \| limited \| operational \| review_required \| non_admissible \| quarantined \| revoked",\
"approved_uses": \[\
"defined_use_1"\
\],\
"prohibited_uses": \[\
"PRU value claim without admissibility",\
"investor rights without legal wrapper"\
\],\
"legal_review": {\
"status": "not_started \| pending \| completed \| jurisdiction_specific",\
"jurisdictions": \[\]\
},\
"mrv_pru_rap_relationship": {\
"may_reference_mrv": false,\
"may_reference_pru": false,\
"may_reference_rap": false,\
"conditions": \[\]\
},\
"governance_relationship": {\
"may_signal": false,\
"may_vote": false,\
"scope": "none_or_defined_scope"\
},\
"bridge_exposure": {\
"has_bridge": false,\
"bridge_type": "none_or_defined_type",\
"bridge_review_status": "not_applicable \| pending \| completed"\
},\
"ai_automation_exposure": {\
"ai_can_allocate": false,\
"ai_can_change_status": false,\
"ai_can_modify_rights": false\
},\
"risk_class": "low \| moderate \| high \| critical",\
"review_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",\
"canonical_registry_reference": "registry_url_or_hash"\
}

This schema is illustrative. The operative schema should be versioned, reviewed, tested, and aligned with the canonical registry before implementation.

**Q.21 Final Token Boundary Principle**

Prometheus can interoperate with tokens.

It can learn from token communities.

It can collaborate with DAOs.

It can recognize contribution.

It can use settlement rails.

It can support future legally structured instruments.

It can connect with Tribeke through sovereign alignment.

**It can design future token-aware infrastructure. But the final boundary remains:**

life is not a token;

dignity is not a token;

evidence is not a token;

regeneration is not a token;

MRV is not a token;

PRU is not automatically a token;

RAP is not automatically a token;

governance is not reducible to token weight;

human continuity is not negotiable through market mechanics.

Tokens may serve Prometheus.

They may not become Prometheus.

**The final principle is:**

symbols may move faster than life;

markets may move faster than evidence;

code may move faster than law;

AI may move faster than governance;

but Prometheus must move only as fast as its source-layer fidelity, evidence discipline, legal structure, and living purpose allow.

Regeneration comes first; financial meaning comes later.

Life generates value. Evidence makes value legible. MRV makes value reviewable. Methodology makes value readable. Admissibility makes value usable. PRU analysis makes value comparable. Maturity classification makes value readable for diligence. Legal structure makes rights enforceable. Capital makes scale possible.

**Controlled Language for Capital-Facing Claims**

| **Avoid** | **Use** |
|----|----|
| Prometheus proves syntropic surplus. | Prometheus defines a testable cooperative-surplus hypothesis. |
| PRU creates value. | PRU analyzes verified regenerative performance. |
| AI validates MRV. | AI assists review; MRV validity follows method and evidence. |
| The system is risk-free or time-positive by design. | Risk and time-positive dynamics are conditional, evidence-based and hazard-adjusted. |
| Tokenization creates the asset. | Legal structure and verified evidence define the represented right. |

**Sensitivity, Robustness, and Anti-Gaming Controls**

| **Analysis** | **Purpose** | **Acceptance condition** |
|----|----|----|
| One-at-a-time sensitivity | Detect extreme dependency on one parameter. | No unexplained cliff effects. |
| Global sensitivity / Sobol | Identify dominant drivers. | Top drivers disclosed. |
| Threshold perturbation | Test sensitivity to gates. | No arbitrary gate gaming. |
| Missing-data stress | Assess missing indicators. | Conservative penalty applied. |
| Adversarial parameter selection | Test manipulative scoring. | Output remains bounded. |
| Red-team review | Attempt to inflate PRU. | Exploit documented and patched. |

**PRU Value-Function Axioms**

| **Axiom** | **Requirement** |
|----|----|
| Boundedness | For all admissible indicators, 0 \<= g(Z) \<= 1 and 0 \<= V_PRU_adj \<= V_base \* (1 + U_max) when all gates and confidence equal 1. |
| Indicator monotonicity | Holding penalties and confidence constant, improvement in verified SRI, RPI, CESI or MIS must not reduce V_PRU_adj. |
| Confidence monotonicity | Holding all else constant, lower Conf_total must not increase V_PRU_adj. |
| No value without base | If V_base = 0, LegalGate = 0 or MRVGate = 0, then V_PRU_adj = 0 for PRU admissibility purposes. |
| No uplift without evidence | A value uplift must be linked to normalized indicators, evidence sources, method version and reviewer trail. |

**Normalized PRU Value Stack**

| **Layer** | **Value class** | **Admissibility condition** |
|----|----|----|
| Layer 1 | Core productive value: food, biomass, operational revenue, substitution value where measurable. | Directly observable, cash-relevant or contractually grounded. |
| Layer 2 | Ecological performance value: soil, water, biodiversity, input reduction, resilience. | Measured, verified, confidence-scored and connected to a recognized pathway. |
| Layer 3 | Systemic and health-related value: nutritional quality, nature exposure, education, preventive health potential, community resilience. | Excluded from base-case cash flows unless tied to explicit contract, program, procurement, insurance, education or wellness mechanism. |

**Evidence Families and Minimum Controls**

| **Evidence family** | **Examples** | **Minimum control** |
|----|----|----|
| Soil | SOC, organic matter, aggregate stability, bulk density, microbial biomass, earthworm activity. | Sampling protocol, laboratory/method identity, depth, geolocation and date. |
| Water | Soil moisture, infiltration, runoff, water retention after rainfall, irrigation dependency. | Sensor/device identity, calibration, time series and weather context. |
| Biodiversity | Species richness, functional diversity, pollinator habitat, canopy/understory structure. | Survey protocol, observer calibration and repeatability. |
| Productivity | Yield, diversity-adjusted yield, temporal distribution, yield stability, input dependency. | Operational logs, measurement units and baseline comparison. |
| MRV integrity | Provenance, signatures, method hashes, chain of custody, reviewer trail. | Completeness, tamper checks and no-double-counting review. |

**PRU Maturity Classification**

| **Stage** | **Meaning** | **Capital-facing status** |
|----|----|----|
| PRU-R0 | Claim or conceptual regenerative intent. | Non-admissible. |
| PRU-R1 | Observation recorded with minimal time, source and location context. | Non-admissible except as raw evidence. |
| PRU-R2 | Evidence bundle with provenance and method metadata. | Review-only. |
| PRU-R3 | Verified indicator with quality checks and confidence score. | Conditionally usable in internal analysis. |
| PRU-R4 | Methodology-aligned indicator with admissibility gate passed. | Eligible for PRU analytical scoring. |
| PRU-R5 | PRU profile with reproducible scoring package and reviewer trail. | Eligible for diligence and portfolio review. |
| PRU-R6 | PRU integrated into RAP-level risk, no-double-counting and legal review. | Conditionally capital-readable. |
| PRU-R7 | PRU linked to compliant legal or institutional wrapper. | Potentially investor-facing according to applicable law and disclosure. |

# Appendix R: Mathematical Formalization of PRU Value Analysis, Confidence, Normalization, Maturity Classification, and Capital-Readiness

## R.1 Purpose and boundary

This appendix defines the mathematical and methodological structure of the Prometheus Regenerative Unit, or PRU.

The PRU is an analytical interface through which verified regenerative performance may become comparable, confidence-adjusted, maturity-classified and capital-readable.

It is not a legal right, not a token, not a security, not a credit by default, not a guarantee of return and not a market price.

The purpose of the formal framework is to make judgment disciplined, not to let formulas replace judgment.

Financial meaning remains downstream from evidence, method, governance, law and admissibility.

## 

## R.2 PRU controlling sequence

**The controlling PRU sequence is:**

living system -\> regenerative process -\> observation -\> evidence -\> provenance -\> integrity -\> verified indicator -\> admissibility -\> PRU value-analysis interface -\> maturity classification -\> RAP aggregation -\> legal or institutional wrapper.

Prometheus must never reverse this order.

Capital, tokens, dashboards, AI outputs and narratives do not create regeneration. They may represent, assist, visualize or structure evidence only after the living system has generated measurable performance.

## R.3 PRU boundary statements

A PRU is not land.

A PRU is not a speculative token.

A PRU is not an automatic asset.

A PRU is not a public investment product.

A PRU is not automatically tradable, pledgeable, securitizable, tokenizable, rights-bearing or investor-facing.

A PRU is a structured value-analysis interface.

A PRU may become relevant to capital only through legal wrappers, investor disclosure, jurisdictional review, governance approval, evidence lineage and admissibility controls.

## R.4 PRU maturity classification

PRU maturity classification is a readiness architecture, not a regulatory rating.

A PRU may mature as evidence continuity, MRV integrity, operating history, governance reliability, legal clarity and financial admissibility improve.

A minimum maturity record should include site identity, baseline, evidence bundle, methodology version, confidence score, gate results, risk flags, reviewer notes and legal/admissibility status.

Maturity may improve, remain static or regress. Regression must be recorded when ecological performance weakens, evidence quality deteriorates, governance conflicts arise, legal status changes, or no-double-counting uncertainty becomes unresolved.

## R.5 Evidence inputs and minimum PRU dataset

PRU analysis may use sensor, laboratory, remote-sensing, human audit, operational, governance and financial evidence.

The quality of PRU analysis depends on the weakest material evidence layer. Sophisticated modeling cannot compensate for poor provenance.

The minimum PRU dataset should include baseline conditions, site boundaries, land-use context, soil indicators, water indicators, biodiversity or functional-diversity indicators, productivity indicators, operational records, MRV integrity fields, governance records, risk flags and legal/admissibility notes.

For early pilots, the minimum scientific set should prioritize soil moisture, infiltration rate, soil organic carbon, organic matter, microbial or biological proxy, earthworm or soil-fauna proxy, aggregate stability, useful yield, yield variance, mortality or replacement rate, input dependency, georeferenced photographs and operational records.

## R.6 Base value stack

The PRU base value is organized by epistemic and financial admissibility.

The stack is not an automatic monetization formula; it is a disciplined classification structure.

Layer 1 includes core productive value that can be linked to cash-relevant outputs, direct revenues, substitution value or operating cost reduction.

Layer 2 includes ecological performance value where soil, water, biodiversity, resilience, carbon, habitat or input-dependency improvements are measured, verified and connected to recognized contractual, underwriting, procurement, insurance, crediting or avoided-cost pathways.

Layer 3 includes systemic co-benefit and research context only. SyntroGIFT, human-health hypotheses, wellbeing narratives, nature-exposure claims, preventive-health potential and community-coherence claims are non-admissible for PRU value analysis, RAP underwriting, Conf_total, investor-facing claims, capital-readiness interpretation, legal-wrapper value or financial representation inside the Prometheus core. Any future health, insurance, reimbursement, procurement or public-health pathway requires a separate legally and scientifically reviewed framework outside the PRU base value stack.

Layer 4 includes portfolio and resilience value at RAP level, where diversification, correlation, tail-risk and maturity effects can be analyzed.

V_base is derived only from admissible components. Non-admissible or research-only value may be disclosed as context but cannot increase PRU value-analysis outputs.

## R.7 Bounded PRU value function

The controlling PRU value-analysis function is bounded.

It prevents arbitrary regenerative uplift and forces confidence, legal admissibility and MRV completeness to constrain the output.

V_PRU_adj = V_base \* LegalGate \* MRVGate \* (1 + U_max \* g(Z)) \* Conf_total

**State-dependent PRU performance expression**

The bounded PRU value function is the controlling capital-readable expression. A complementary state-dependent expression may be used only to describe time-series ecological performance before legal or financial representation:

**V_PRU_state(T) = integral_0^T \[Delta S(t) \* W_Eco\] \* Conf_total(mu, sigma, t) dt**

Where Delta S(t) is the measured change in regenerative state variables, W_Eco is the disclosed ecological weighting vector, and Conf_total(mu, sigma, t) is the confidence model at time t. This expression is evidentiary and analytical. It creates no land rights, cash-flow rights, securities exposure, governance rights, token rights or investor entitlement by itself. Capital-facing PRU analysis remains subject to LegalGate, MRVGate, A_total, U_max, admissibility review, reproducibility package and legal wrapper.

Z = beta_S\*SRI + beta_R\*RPI + beta_C\*CESI + beta_M\*MIS - beta_P\*Penalty

where V_base \>= 0; LegalGate and MRVGate are binary admissibility gates; U_max is the maximum allowed regenerative uplift; g(Z) is a bounded response function in \[0,1\]; Conf_total is the aggregate confidence score; and Penalty captures regression, unresolved data quality issues, no-double-counting uncertainty, adverse audit findings or governance flags.

A conservative implementation may use g(Z) = min(1, max(0, Z)) when all components are normalized and weights are versioned.

A logistic or saturating function may be used where justified by method governance and sensitivity analysis.

## R.8 PRU value axioms

Axiom 1 - boundedness: for all admissible indicators, 0 \<= g(Z) \<= 1. Therefore, when LegalGate = 1, MRVGate = 1 and Conf_total = 1, V_PRU_adj cannot exceed V_base \* (1 + U_max).

Axiom 2 - monotonicity in verified indicators: holding penalties and confidence constant, improvement in normalized SRI, RPI, CESI or MIS must not reduce V_PRU_adj.

Axiom 3 - confidence monotonicity: holding all else constant, a lower Conf_total must not increase V_PRU_adj.

Axiom 4 - no value without base: if V_base = 0, LegalGate = 0, MRVGate = 0 or Conf_total = 0, then the financially admissible PRU value-analysis output is zero.

Axiom 5 - penalty dominance: unresolved integrity, legal, no-double-counting or adverse audit flags must reduce or suspend the relevant component until resolved.

## R.9 U_max governance

## R.9A U_max calibration rule

U_max is not a narrative premium. It is a governance-controlled cap derived from pilot evidence, conservative comparables, risk-adjusted performance and legal review. Before pilot evidence is sufficient, U_max should be set conservatively or held at zero for capital-facing use.

| **U_max input** | **Interpretation** |
|----|----|
| Pilot performance distribution | Median or lower-bound verified improvement across eligible indicators. |
| Comparator evidence | Observed difference versus paired plots, matched controls or synthetic controls. |
| Confidence adjustment | Lower credible bound or conservative confidence-weighted estimate. |
| Risk adjustment | Reduction for ecological tail risk, operator risk, legal uncertainty and data maturity. |
| Governance review | Annual review with published assumptions and versioned method hash. |

U_max must be explicitly stated, justified by reference class, pilot evidence or conservative governance decision, versioned by method hash, disclosed in reports and subject to sensitivity analysis.

Before pilot evidence, U_max may be used only for scenario analysis and must not be presented as validated underwriting value.

Any upward revision of U_max requires governance review, scientific or methodological rationale, sensitivity analysis and red-team comment.

No narrative, dashboard output, AI response, branding language or market enthusiasm may increase U_max.

**R.9B Pre-pilot capital-facing lock**

Before sufficient pilot evidence exists, the capital-facing uplift effect of U_max is zero. Research, internal planning or scenario materials may display alternative U_max values only when clearly labelled non-validated scenario analysis.

Operational rule: if PilotEvidence = 0, then U_max_capital = 0 and V_PRU_adj may not include regenerative uplift for investor-facing, RAP-facing, financing-facing or public valuation use.

A non-zero capital-facing U_max requires pre-registered pilot evidence, disclosed reference class, sensitivity analysis, red-team comment, legal review and annual governance review.

## R.10 Raw-to-normalized indicator mapping

All PRU-relevant indicators must be transformed into dimensionless scores before aggregation.

Each indicator must define raw variable name, unit, measurement method, direction of improvement, baseline value, reference distribution, normalization operator, winsorization rule, missing-data rule, confidence effect and evidence tier.

Positive-direction operator: N_pos(x) = clip((winsor(x,p05,p95) - baseline) / (target - baseline), 0, 1).

Negative-direction operator: N_neg(x) = clip((baseline - winsor(x,p05,p95)) / (baseline - target), 0, 1).

Target-band operator: N_band(x) = 1 within the valid range and declines with distance from the range.

Binary gates use N_gate(x) in {0,1}.

A normalization operator is acceptable only when two independent evaluators, using the same raw data, baseline, reference table and method version, obtain the same normalized score within the disclosed tolerance.

## R.11 Starter normalization table

| **Indicator** | **Unit** | **Direction** | **Operator** | **Reference basis** | **Notes** |
|----|----|----|----|----|----|
| soil organic carbon | % or g/kg | positive | N_pos | baseline + regional distribution | laboratory method required |
| infiltration rate | mm/h | positive/saturating | N_pos with cap | baseline + soil texture class | avoid unlimited uplift |
| soil moisture stability | variance or CV | negative | N_neg | sensor time series | freshness rule required |
| microbial biomass | mg C/kg | positive | N_pos | laboratory reference | higher cost; evidence-tier note required |
| earthworm count | count/m2 | positive/band | N_pos or N_band | field protocol | inter-rater controls required |
| external nitrogen dependency | kg/ha | negative | N_neg | baseline operational records | anti-gaming rule required |
| biomass returned | kg dry matter/ha | positive/saturating | N_pos with cap | management log + sample | avoid double counting with SOC |
| yield stability | CV or downside deviation | negative | N_neg | multi-season yield data | pilot-required for strong claims |
