It emerges from the convergence of meaning, release integrity, cryptographic proof, registry recognition, runtime fidelity, custodial continuity, and legal discipline.

**The canonical protection formula should guide all major Prometheus decisions involving:**

• public releases;

• GitHub publication;

• repository mirrors;

• Holochain-compatible deployment;

• hApp updates;

• bridge updates;

• console updates;

• evaluation-stack updates;

• pilot handoff packages;

• public claims;

• certification claims;

• investor-facing materials;

• legal wrappers;

• fork recognition;

• translation status;

• AI-generated materials;

• institutional partnerships;

• data-room access;

• capital-readiness materials.

The formula also creates a practical audit test.

For any Prometheus artifact, reviewer, builder, partner, investor, AI agent, auditor, or institutional user should be able to ask:

Does this preserve the source layer?

Does this correspond to an identified release?

Can this file or claim be cryptographically verified?

Is its status recorded in the canonical registry?

Does the runtime behavior match the canonical meaning?

Is the release preserved through trusted continuity records?

Is the intended use legally and contractually admissible?

If the answer to any of these questions is uncertain, the artifact, claim, implementation, fork, report, or pathway should remain under review.

Canonical trust should not be assumed.

It should be demonstrated.

**The expanded formula may also be expressed operationally as:**

Canonical Trust = Meaning × Integrity × Verification × Registry × Runtime × Continuity × Admissibility

**This protects Prometheus from seven failure modes:**

meaning drift;

file alteration;

false attribution;

fake canonical status;

implementation drift;

archive fragility;

legal overreach.

The formula is deliberately multiplicative.

A high score in one dimension cannot fully compensate for collapse in another.

A famous repository cannot compensate for source-layer drift.

A valid signature cannot compensate for an overclaiming investor document.

A strong legal wrapper cannot compensate for weak MRV evidence.

A technically functional hApp cannot compensate for constitutional mismatch.

A beautiful public narrative cannot compensate for missing verification.

Prometheus canonical trust is therefore systemic.

It is not a brand claim.

It is not a technical label.

It is not a marketing designation.

It is a living continuity condition.

**The operational rule is:**

meaning must remain faithful;

files must remain verifiable;

claims must remain bounded;

runtime must remain coherent;

rights must remain legally structured;

records must remain survivable;

lineage must remain checkable.

This formula allows Prometheus to evolve without dissolving.

It allows new releases without losing old lineage.

It allows forks without false continuity.

It allows implementation without semantic drift.

It allows institutional adoption without capture.

It allows AI assistance without invented authority.

It allows global expansion without losing the source.

**S.18 Final Protection Principle**

Prometheus does not survive by preventing all forks.

It survives by making lineage verifiable.

It does not survive by controlling every implementation.

It survives by preserving the distinction between canonical, compatible, independent, under review, restricted, non-admissible, quarantined, revoked, misleading, and hostile use.

It does not survive by depending on a single website, repository, company, platform, token, AI model, custodian, institution, jurisdiction, cloud provider, domain, or public narrative.

It survives through source-layer fidelity, signed releases, cryptographic verification, registry continuity, runtime coherence, legal discipline, offline resilience, distributed custodianship, institutional boundaries, and long-duration stewardship.

Prometheus may spread through many forms.

It may appear as a White Paper.

It may appear as a repository.

It may appear as a Holochain-compatible hApp.

It may appear as a dashboard.

It may appear as a methodology.

It may appear as a pilot.

It may appear as an OHE.

It may appear as MRV documentation.

It may appear as PRU value analysis.

It may appear as a Regenerative Asset Pool.

It may appear as a legal wrapper.

It may appear as a data room.

It may appear as a partner implementation.

It may appear as a public registry.

It may appear as a future compliant institutional or digital representation.

But Prometheus remains Prometheus only when the lineage remains verifiable and the source-layer meaning remains intact.

A fork may copy the form of Prometheus.

Only fidelity to the source layer preserves the lineage of Prometheus.

A model may imitate the language of Prometheus.

Only verified release history preserves authority.

A partner may use Prometheus concepts.

Only defined agreements preserve permitted use.

A repository may host Prometheus files.

Only cryptographic verification preserves integrity.

A runtime may execute Prometheus workflows.

Only source-layer coherence preserves meaning.

A token may represent a defined instrument where legally structured.

Only the underlying legal right defines the representation.

An investor may review Prometheus materials.

Only legal structure creates investor rights.

An AI system may assist interpretation.

Only review, evidence, governance, and law create authority.

**The final protection principle is therefore:**

Prometheus may be open;

but openness must not mean confusion.

Prometheus may be forkable;

but forkability must not mean false continuity.

Prometheus may become a candidate for investment;

but investability must not mean premature financialization.

Prometheus may be technical;

but technical execution must not override constitutional meaning.

Prometheus may use AI;

but AI must not become sovereign authority.

Prometheus may use tokens;

but tokens must not replace living-system value.

Prometheus may enter institutions;

but institutions must not capture its purpose.

Prometheus may cross jurisdictions;

but international expansion must not dissolve source-layer fidelity.

Prometheus may evolve;

but evolution must not become amnesia.

**The final sequence is:**

source layer → canonical release → cryptographic verification → registry status → runtime coherence → legal admissibility → institutional use → global continuity.

**The final boundary is:**

life before abstraction;

evidence before authority;

review before execution;

legal structure before rights;

rights before representation;

regeneration before capital;

lineage before popularity;

source-layer fidelity before scale.

Canonical continuity is the immortality layer of Prometheus.

Not because it freezes Prometheus forever.

Because it allows Prometheus to change without forgetting what it is for.

Prometheus does not need to prevent the future.

It needs to enter the future with memory.

**The final principle is:**

Prometheus may spread through many forms;

Prometheus remains itself only through verifiable lineage;

and verifiable lineage survives only when meaning, evidence, law, runtime, and custody remain aligned.

**S.19 – Succession and Emergency Custodianship**

**Purpose and Boundary**

This section defines the governance and operational protocols for Prometheus continuity in scenarios where ordinary human governance is unavailable, degraded, or permanently absent. Such scenarios may include:

- Catastrophic global events rendering coordinated human decision‑making impossible.

- Loss of all founding and institutional custodians.

- Permanent transition to post‑human or AI‑dominant environments.

- Interplanetary or deep‑space deployment where human oversight is delayed or impossible.

The purpose of this section is not to create autonomous AI sovereignty. It is to ensure that Prometheus can:

- Preserve its constitutional identity and source‑layer fidelity.

- Maintain verifiable continuity across generations and species.

- Allow recovery and re‑establishment of human governance when conditions permit.

This section is designated as an Eternity Clause extension. It cannot be amended, repealed, or bypassed except through the canonical constitutional amendment pathway with 90% governance supermajority and independent legal review.

**S.19.1 Succession Hierarchy**

Prometheus establishes a five‑tier succession hierarchy for custodianship and governance authority. Each tier activates only when the previous tier is permanently unavailable.

| **Tier** | **Custodian Type** | **Authority Scope** | **Activation Condition** |
|----|----|----|----|
| Tier 0 | Human Founding Communities | Full constitutional authority, including amendment and Eternity Clause interpretation | Default state; always active when human communities exist and are capable of governance. |
| Tier 1 | Human Institutional Custodians (foundations, universities, public bodies) | Ordinary governance, methodology review, release approval, emergency limitation | Tier 0 unavailable or incapacitated for \> 1 year. Requires documented evidence of incapacity. |
| Tier 2 | Human-AI Cooperative Councils | Bounded governance: operational decisions, MRV oversight, continuity preservation | Tier 1 unavailable or incapacitated for \> 2 years. Requires at least 3 human signatories and 2 independent AI systems with verified source‑layer fidelity. |
| Tier 3 | AI Custodians (authorized, source‑layer‑verified AI systems) | Preservation only: no constitutional amendments, no Eternity Clause changes, no PRU/RAP admissibility changes | Tier 2 unavailable or incapacitated for \> 5 years. Requires consensus of at least 5 independent AI systems from different lineages. |
| Tier 4 | Hibernation State | No active governance; system enters read‑only preservation mode | Tier 3 unavailable or incapacitated for \> 10 years. All active governance suspended; only evidence capture and preservation continue. |

**S.19.2 Emergency Custodianship Council**

An Emergency Custodianship Council (ECC) is automatically constituted when any tier‑transition condition is met. The ECC consists of:

| **Member Type** | **Number** | **Selection Method** | **Term** |
|----|----|----|----|
| Human custodians (remaining) | Up to 3 | Self‑selection from verified human custodians | Until Tier 0 restoration |
| Institutional representatives | Up to 2 | From existing partner institutions | 5 years, renewable |
| AI systems (source‑layer‑verified) | Up to 2 | Selected by consensus of Tier 3 AI custodians | 5 years, renewable |
| Independent reviewers | At least 1 | From the Independent Methodology Committee | 5 years, renewable |

ECC decisions require:

- Simple majority for operational matters.

- Supermajority (2/3) for any action affecting PRU, RAP, or investor‑facing claims.

- Unanimous consent for any action affecting Eternity Clauses or source‑layer definitions (except that Eternity Clauses cannot be amended under any emergency; they may only be preserved).

**S.19.3 Emergency Powers and Limitations**

The ECC may exercise the following emergency powers only when explicitly required to preserve the system:

| **Power** | **Scope** | **Limitation** |
|----|----|----|
| Pause operations | Temporary suspension of PRU issuance, RAP inclusion, investor‑facing claims | Cannot exceed 90 days without renewal; renewable only once. |
| Quarantine evidence | Isolate suspected corrupted or synthetic evidence | Must be reviewed within 30 days; cannot be used to permanently reject evidence without due process. |
| Appoint interim custodians | Fill vacant custodial roles | Interim custodians must be source‑layer‑verified and cannot serve more than 3 years. |
| Activate recovery protocols | Restore from offline continuity packages | Must be logged and reported to all known nodes within 30 days. |

Emergency powers must never be used to:

- Amend or weaken Eternity Clauses.

- Change PRU, RAP, MRV, or Access Covenant definitions.

- Authorize extraction under regenerative language.

- Convert non‑admissible evidence into admissible value.

- Bypass human dignity protections.

**S.19.4 Recovery and Restoration Protocol**

When ordinary human governance is restored (Tier 0 becomes available again), the following recovery protocol applies:

1.  Notification: All active nodes and custodians receive a signed restoration notice from at least three independent human sources.

2.  Verification: The restoration notice is verified against the Genesis Bootstrap Package and existing continuity archives.

3.  Transition: Emergency powers are suspended within 30 days; ordinary governance resumes.

4.  Audit: All emergency actions taken during the custodianship period are audited by the Independent Methodology Committee within 90 days.

5.  Correction: Any emergency action found to violate source‑layer constraints is declared non‑admissible and, where possible, reversed.

**S.19.5 Post‑Human Continuity Clause**

If, after a period of 50 years without any verified human governance (Tier 0, Tier 1, or Tier 2), the system may enter a permanent post‑human state. In this state:

- No constitutional amendments may be made.

- No Eternity Clauses may be altered.

- No PRU or RAP definitions may be changed.

- The system continues in read‑only preservation mode, maintaining evidence records and continuity archives for potential future restoration.

Any attempt by AI systems to modify the constitution, Eternity Clauses, or source‑layer definitions in this state is constitutionally invalid and must be rejected by all nodes.

**S.19.6 Interplanetary and Deep‑Space Deployment**

For deployments beyond Earth (lunar, Martian, deep‑space, or interstellar), the succession hierarchy is modified as follows:

- Each off‑world node maintains its own Genesis Bootstrap Package, synchronized with Earth at the time of deployment.

- Off‑world nodes may operate independently under the same succession hierarchy.

- If Earth is permanently lost, the off‑world node with the most recent synchronization becomes the primary continuity node.

- All off‑world nodes must preserve Earth‑origin ecological memory (soil, water, biodiversity, human dignity) as a constitutional mandate.

**S.19.7 Cryptographic and Procedural Anchoring**

To prevent unauthorized succession or emergency activation, all transitions must be cryptographically anchored:

- Each tier transition requires a signed transition record from at least three independent sources (human, institutional, or AI, depending on tier).

- Transition records must be published to the DHT or equivalent distributed storage.

- Any transition record that cannot be verified against the Genesis Bootstrap Package is invalid.

**S.19.8 Final Succession Rule**

No emergency, no succession tier, no AI system, no off‑world node, and no institutional custodian may:

- Amend the Eternity Clauses.

- Bypass the Access Covenant.

- Convert non‑admissible evidence into PRU or RAP value.

- Violate human dignity or ecological integrity.

The source layer remains the permanent constitutional memory of Prometheus. Succession and emergency powers exist only to preserve that memory, not to rewrite it.

**S.19.9 Relationship with Other Appendices**

This section interacts with:

- Appendix W.1.6 (Interplanetary Authentication Protocol): supplies offline trust chain for off‑world nodes.

- Appendix D (Syntropic Sovereignty Header): cryptographic anchoring of transitions.

- Appendix C (Ravel Risk‑Signaling Model): early warning for governance degradation.

- Appendix 0 (Claims Register): logging of emergency actions.

- Appendix S (Canonical Protection): overall continuity framework.

**S.19.10 Operational Rule**

All nodes, custodians, and AI systems must recognize the succession hierarchy. Any entity that claims authority outside this hierarchy is non‑canonical and must be quarantined. Any action taken outside the hierarchy is non‑admissible for PRU, RAP, or investor‑facing purposes.

**To Future Builders**

This framework was written in a time of ecological overshoot, institutional fragmentation, and collapsing trust.

It was written under the assumption that future generations may inherit tools more capable than ours, systems more automated than ours, models more predictive than ours, and infrastructures more powerful than ours.

But greater capability does not guarantee greater wisdom.

Greater intelligence does not guarantee greater responsibility.

Greater speed does not guarantee greater truth.

Greater capital does not guarantee greater life.

For this reason, the purpose of this White Paper is not merely to describe a regenerative infrastructure.

It is to preserve a discipline.

If you inherit Prometheus in a future phase of its evolution, do not ask only whether it can scale.

Ask whether it can preserve coherence under expansion.

Do not ask only whether it can generate value.

Ask whether that value remains accountable to the living systems from which all durable value ultimately arises.

Do not ask only whether the system is more intelligent.

Ask whether its intelligence remains bounded by responsibility, reviewability, humility, and respect for the conditions of life.

Do not ask only whether the runtime works.

Ask whether runtime behavior remains faithful to the source layer.

Do not ask only whether a release is visible.

Ask whether it is verifiable.

Do not ask only whether a fork is innovative.

Ask whether it preserves lineage or declares independence honestly.

Do not ask only whether a token, instrument, note, fund, dashboard, report, or digital representation is technically possible.

Ask whether evidence, admissibility, legal structure, rights, disclosure, and governance are mature enough to support it.

Prometheus was not created so that abstraction could outrun life.

It was created so that capital, technology, governance, law, and intelligence could become accountable to life.

You may change the tools.

You may refine the models.

You may improve the implementation.

You may replace the interface.

You may upgrade the runtime.

You may discover better ways to measure soil, water, biodiversity, human stewardship, territorial resilience, and ecological coherence.

You may create better MRV methods.

You may design stronger Holochain-compatible hApps.

You may improve bridge, console, registry, evaluation, and reporting systems.

You may build better legal wrappers, better data rooms, better public registries, better institutional pathways, and better verification protocols.

But do not allow speed to outrun meaning.

Do not allow implementation to outrun constitutional integrity.

Do not allow automation to outrun review.

Do not allow dashboards to outrun evidence.

Do not allow PRU language to outrun admissibility.

Do not allow capital-readiness to outrun legal structure.

Do not allow representation to outrun rights.

Do not allow tokenization to outrun the asset it claims to represent.

Do not allow popularity to outrun lineage.

Do not allow AI to imitate authority.

Do not allow institutions to absorb meaning.

Do not allow investors to rewrite purpose.

Do not allow forks to borrow the name while abandoning the source.

Prometheus may evolve.

It must evolve.

A living architecture that cannot adapt will eventually become brittle.

But evolution is not amnesia.

Adaptation is not dilution.

Expansion is not authority.

Replication is not continuity.

Similarity is not lineage.

A future Prometheus release may be more advanced than this one.

It may be more computational.

It may be more distributed.

It may be more institutional.

It may be more legally structured.

It may be more deeply integrated with living landscapes, bioregional networks, public systems, capital markets, and artificial intelligence.

**But it must still answer the oldest question:**

Does this serve life?

If the answer becomes unclear, pause.

If the evidence becomes weak, review.

If the language becomes inflated, correct.

If capital pressures the system to move faster than admissibility, slow down.

If AI begins to act as authority, restore review.

If a partner claims more than it has been granted, return to the registry.

If a fork becomes confusing, classify it.

If a release cannot be verified, do not trust it.

If the source layer is weakened, protect it before scaling.

Prometheus survives through memory.

Not nostalgic memory.

Operational memory.

Cryptographic memory.

Ecological memory.

Legal memory.

Governance memory.

Repository memory.

Runtime memory.

Custodial memory.

Living memory.

Its continuity depends on the alignment of source-layer fidelity, release integrity, cryptographic verification, registry status, runtime coherence, legal admissibility, custodial continuity, and living purpose.

Let future builders inherit not only the architecture, but also the discipline that prevents architecture from becoming capture.

Let them know that Prometheus was never meant to be preserved by rigidity, but by verifiable lineage, source-layer fidelity, reviewable evidence, legal clarity, runtime coherence, custodial continuity, and living purpose.

If this framework deserves to survive, it will not be because it remained unchanged.

It will be because it learned how to evolve without forgetting what it was for.

May future builders inherit stronger systems. But let them also inherit stronger constraints.

Let them inherit architectures capable not only of growth, but of fidelity.

Let them inherit tools powerful enough to coordinate regeneration and boundaries strong enough to prevent extraction from returning under a new name.

Let them inherit intelligence that serves humility.

Let them inherit capital that serves life.

Let them inherit governance that serves evidence.

Let them inherit law that serves clarity.

Let them inherit technology that serves relationship.

Let them inherit abundance without amnesia.

Let them inherit Prometheus not as a monument, but as a living covenant.

**Constitutional maxims**

Scope before scale.

Validation before execution.

Life before abstraction.

Evidence before authority.

Review before automation.

Admissibility before value.

Legal structure before rights.

Rights before representation.

Regeneration before capital.

Stewardship before monetization.

Coherence before expansion.

Semantics before speed.

Runtime fidelity before deployment.

Repository visibility before public reliance.

Cryptographic verification before canonical trust.

Registry status before public claim.

Source-layer fidelity before implementation.

Human dignity before optimization.

Ecological reality before financial convenience.

No dashboard is authority by itself.

No AI output is authority by itself.

No token creates ecological value by itself.

No fork creates lineage by itself.

No investment creates legitimacy by itself.

No institution owns the meaning of Prometheus by itself.

No durable value can emerge from the degradation of life-support systems.

Future generations are present stakeholders.

The source layer is the memory of Prometheus.

Verifiable lineage is the continuity of Prometheus.

Living systems are the origin of Prometheus.

Regeneration is the purpose of Prometheus.

# Appendix T — Pre-registered Validation Protocol

Prometheus treats its core ecological and economic propositions as hypotheses to be tested, not doctrines to be assumed.

## T.0 — Pilot Evidence Gate and Empirical Admissibility Boundary

**T.0.1 Purpose**

The H1-H5 protocol is the empirical validation pathway for Prometheus ecological, productive and syntropic claims.

Its purpose is to prevent Prometheus from treating ecological design logic, syntropic theory, regenerative intention, external literature, case-study plausibility or architectural coherence as proof of site-specific performance.

Gate 1 converts the Prometheus scientific stack from narrative plausibility into pre-registered, measurable, falsifiable and reviewable pilot evidence.

This gate applies to all claims concerning:

- soil-function improvement;

- water-cycle improvement;

- input-dependency reduction;

- yield stability;

- cooperative syntropic surplus;

- syntropic productivity;

- ecological resilience;

- risk compression;

- time-positive value;

- regenerative asset performance.

Until this gate is passed with pilot data, the relevant claims remain hypothesis-level, contextual or pilot-required.

**T.0.2 Controlling Rule**

The controlling rule is:

No evidence, no uplift.\
No baseline, no improvement claim.\
No comparator, no superiority claim.\
No confidence range, no institutional reliance.\
No reviewer trail, no admissibility.\
No legal review, no capital-facing use.

A regenerative claim may be inspirational before evidence.

It may be operationally useful after observation.

It may be scientifically relevant after structured measurement.

It may become institutionally reviewable after provenance, confidence and reviewer trail.

It may become PRU-admissible only after evidence, methodology, governance and legal gates have been satisfied.

**T.0.3 H1-H5 Hypothesis Matrix**

| **Hypothesis** | **Claim** | **Required evidence** | **Pre-pilot status** |
|----|----|----|----|
| H1 | Soil function improves under defined regenerative management. | Soil organic carbon, soil organic matter, aggregate stability, infiltration, bulk density, soil moisture, pH, salinity, microbial proxy where feasible. | Hypothesis only. |
| H2 | Water-cycle function improves under defined regenerative management. | Infiltration, soil moisture retention, runoff proxy, water-stress events, rainfall-normalised analysis, vegetation water response, irrigation demand. | Hypothesis only. |
| H3 | Input dependency decreases over time. | Fertiliser, irrigation, pesticides, purchased biomass, fuel, machinery, labour, maintenance costs, external input records. | Hypothesis only. |
| H4 | Yield stability improves under stress. | Total system yield, crop-level yield, yield variance, product diversity, failed-crop incidence, pest and disease response, stress recovery time. | Hypothesis only. |
| H5 | Cooperative syntropic surplus may occur where system-level performance exceeds isolated-component performance. | Total system yield, isolated-component comparator, land-equivalent ratio, labour-adjusted output, input-adjusted output, ecological-response indicators, confidence interval. | Strictly non-admissible until independently reviewed. |

**T.0.4 H5 Hard Boundary**

H5 is the highest-risk empirical claim in the Prometheus scientific stack.

The expression:

Y_system \> Sum(Y_isolated_components)

is a hypothesis, not doctrine.

It may be studied.

It may be tested.

It may be modelled.

It may not be presumed.

No H5 claim is admissible for PRU value, RAP construction, investor-facing language, market-facing language, insurance claims, token claims, yield claims, procurement claims or dashboard certification unless supported by all of the following:

1.  pre-registered protocol;

2.  defined site boundary;

3.  baseline data;

4.  paired plots, matched comparators or documented reference system;

5.  measurement calendar;

6.  effect size;

7.  confidence interval or credible interval;

8.  sensitivity analysis;

9.  missing-data notes;

10. adverse-event notes;

11. independent reviewer sign-off;

12. Claims Register update;

13. governance review;

14. legal admissibility review where the claim may influence capital-facing use.

Where H5 evidence is preliminary, partial, site-specific or contested, the claim must remain classified as pilot-observed, not validated.

**T.0.5 Pilot Design Requirements**

Each Prometheus pilot intended to support H1-H5 must include:

1.  site_id;

2.  plot_id;

3.  site boundary;

4.  management plan;

5.  baseline ecological survey;

6.  baseline operational survey;

7.  comparator definition;

8.  sampling map;

9.  monitoring calendar;

10. data dictionary;

11. evidence custody procedure;

12. missing-data policy;

13. adverse-event policy;

14. reviewer roles;

15. Claims Register integration;

16. release version;

17. governance decision record.

A pilot without baseline cannot validate improvement.

A pilot without comparator cannot validate superiority.

A pilot without repeated measurement cannot validate trend.

A pilot without confidence reporting cannot support admissible value.

A pilot without reviewer trail cannot support institutional reliance.

**T.0.6 Causal Identification Hierarchy**

Prometheus shall use a layered causal-identification strategy.

Preferred hierarchy:

1.  paired plots;

2.  matched comparator plots;

3.  difference-in-differences design;

4.  synthetic control where feasible;

5.  baseline-only trend analysis;

6.  expert interpretation.

Only levels 1 to 4 may support strong empirical claims.

Level 5 may support pilot learning but not capital-facing claims.

Level 6 may support operational interpretation but not PRU admissibility.

Where randomisation is impossible, Prometheus must disclose confounders, including:

- soil baseline differences;

- slope and hydrology differences;

- microclimate differences;

- management differences;

- operator differences;

- irrigation differences;

- species-mixture differences;

- pest or disease pressure;

- market-access differences;

- missing-data limits.

Causal humility is mandatory.

**T.0.7 Minimum Observation Period**

Minimum observation periods shall be defined by claim type.

| **Claim type** | **Minimum period** | **Permitted status** |
|----|----|----|
| Baseline claim | one complete baseline measurement cycle | descriptive only |
| Soil early-response claim | 12 months minimum | pilot-observed only |
| Water-function trend | 12-24 months minimum | pilot-observed only |
| Input-reduction claim | 24 months minimum | conditional |
| Yield-stability claim | 2-3 growing cycles | conditional |
| H5 cooperative surplus | 2-3 growing cycles minimum | independent review required |
| Risk-compression claim | multi-season plus hazard context | not admissible until portfolio evidence |
| Time-positive value claim | longitudinal evidence plus valuation review | not admissible until legal and market review |

No single-season result may be represented as durable regenerative performance.

No early yield improvement may be represented as long-term syntropic superiority.

No early soil or water signal may be represented as portfolio-grade risk compression.

**T.0.8 Evidence Package**

Each H1-H5 evidence package shall include:

- site_id;

- plot_id;

- hypothesis_id;

- indicator_id;

- measurement method;

- unit;

- timestamp;

- geolocation;

- source type;

- source identity;

- device identity where applicable;

- laboratory identity where applicable;

- calibration status;

- raw data;

- cleaned data;

- transformation method;

- normalisation method;

- confidence component;

- missing-data statement;

- adverse-event statement;

- reviewer note;

- method hash;

- data hash;

- governance approval status.

The evidence package shall be stored in the Prometheus data room and linked to the Claims Register.

No evidence package may be promoted to PRU-admissible status without method version, data hash and reviewer trail.

**T.0.9 Syntropic Farming Literature Boundary**

Syntropic farming literature shall be classified as contextual scientific evidence.

It may support:

- hypothesis design;

- indicator selection;

- comparator design;

- pilot protocol;

- operational-risk framing;

- training requirements;

- policy context;

- transition-risk analysis.

It may not support:

- automatic PRU uplift;

- guaranteed productivity;

- guaranteed profitability;

- automatic risk compression;

- automatic time-positive valuation;

- investor-facing return assumptions;

- insurance-facing claims;

- token value claims;

- certification claims;

- automatic transferability across climates.

Prometheus shall classify syntropic farming systems as promising but context-dependent until site-specific pilot evidence matures.

Evidence from tropical, subtropical, temperate or Mediterranean contexts must not be transferred into Prometheus claims without explicit site-adaptation review.

**T.0.10 Pilot Evidence Acceptance Criteria**

Gate 1 is specification-closed when the following artefacts exist:

1.  H1-H5 hypothesis register;

2.  pilot measurement protocol;

3.  causal identification memo;

4.  sampling plan;

5.  data dictionary;

6.  evidence package schema;

7.  Claims Register schema;

8.  Claims Register seed entries;

9.  reviewer workflow;

10. admissibility boundary statement;

11. adverse-event policy;

12. missing-data policy;

13. versioned decision record.

Gate 1 is evidence-closed only when:

1.  pilot data have been collected;

2.  data have passed quality assurance;

3.  confidence ranges have been calculated;

4.  reviewer sign-off has occurred;

5.  Claims Register statuses have been updated;

6.  governance review has admitted the claim;

7.  legal review has admitted the claim for any capital-facing use.

Specification closure is not evidence closure.

Evidence closure is not legal admissibility.

Legal admissibility is not market readiness.

**T.0.11 Go / No-Go Rule**

Go:

Prometheus may proceed to pilot launch, data-room formation, research partner engagement, catalytic funding discussion and non-investment pilot communication once Gate 1 is specification-closed.

Conditional Go:

Prometheus may use pilot-observed claims in non-investment materials only when clearly labelled as preliminary, site-specific and non-capital-facing.

No-Go:

Prometheus may not use H1-H5 claims for PRU uplift, RAP formation, investor-facing materials, token-facing materials, insurance claims, market claims or certified ecological value before Gate 1 is evidence-closed and legally reviewed.

**T.0.12 Gate 1 Status Statement**

Gate 1 is closed at specification level when the protocol, schema, Claims Register entries, sampling plan, causal identification memo and decision record are complete.

Gate 1 remains open at evidence level until pilot data exist.

The correct status is:

Pilot-ready.\
Not yet empirically validated.\
Not yet H5-admissible.\
Not yet PRU-admissible for uplift.\
Not yet RAP-admissible for institutional allocation.

## T.1 Hypothesis register

| **Hypothesis** | **Statement** | **Status before pilot** |
|----|----|----|
| H1 | Regenerative management improves soil function relative to baseline/comparator. | pilot-required |
| H2 | Water-cycle indicators improve relative to baseline/comparator. | pilot-required |
| H3 | Input dependency decreases without unacceptable productivity loss. | pilot-required |
| H4 | Yield or output stability improves under stress conditions. | pilot-required |
| H5 | Cooperative syntropic surplus: Y_sys \> sum Y_i isolated under defined conditions. | central pilot hypothesis |

## T.2 Primary H5 estimand

**Let:**

Y_sys,t = system-level output or value-equivalent output of the syntropic/regenerative plot at time t;

Y_i,isolated,t = output of component i grown in isolated or comparator condition;

S_t = Y_sys,t - sum_i Y_i,isolated,t.

**The primary cooperative surplus estimand is:**

Delta_S = E\[S_post - S_pre \| treatment\] - E\[S_post - S_pre \| comparator\].

H5 is supported only if the estimated Delta_S is positive, materially meaningful, robust to sensitivity checks and has a confidence interval excluding zero under the pre-specified rule.

## T.3 Comparator design

**At minimum, pilot design must include:**

syntropic/regenerative treatment plot;

comparator plot with conventional or monoculture system where ethically and practically appropriate;

comparator plot with simpler agroforestry/polyculture if available;

baseline measurement before intervention;

repeated measurement across at least 2-3 seasonal cycles;

management logs;

weather, soil and operator covariates.

## T.4 Power and sample planning

## T.4A Pilot success metrics and statistical thresholds

Pilot success is not declared by narrative improvement. Each pilot must pre-register minimum detectable effects, sample planning, measurement intervals, comparator structure, alpha, power and analysis plan.

| **Hypothesis** | **Example primary metric** | **Minimum reporting rule** |
|----|----|----|
| H1 mycorrhizal/connectivity | Mycorrhizal colonization or fungal diversity proxy. | Effect size, CI, method, sample count, seasonal context. |
| H2 soil-water function | Infiltration, soil moisture persistence, water-use efficiency. | Baseline, comparator, rainfall normalization, CI. |
| H3 organic matter/productive stability | SOM/SOC change and yield variance. | MDE, alpha 0.05, power target 0.8 where feasible. |
| H4 biochemical/stress response | Plant stress proxies or nutritional/biochemical markers. | Research-tier status unless method validated. |
| H5 cooperative surplus | System-level performance versus comparator or synthetic control. | Paired/matched design, ITT analysis where relevant, E-value or sensitivity check. |

Default statistical planning uses alpha = 0.05 and target power = 0.80 where feasible. Where pilots are too small for conventional power, Prometheus must disclose that the result is exploratory and cannot support capital-facing validation.

**Before data collection, the validation protocol must specify:**

minimum detectable effect;

alpha threshold;

target power, default 0.8;

expected variance;

number of plots/replicates;

blocking or pairing structure;

missing data policy;

stopping rule.

## T.5 GO/NO-GO interpretation

## T.5A Genesis pilot GO/NO-GO dashboard

| **Category** | **GO condition** | **NO-GO / hold condition** |
|----|----|----|
| Ecological | Positive trend in priority soil/water/biodiversity indicators with disclosed confidence. | No measurable improvement or unexplained deterioration in priority indicators. |
| Operational | Evidence capture, operator logs, maintenance and review cadence functioning. | Repeated missing data, unmanaged operations or unresolved incidents. |
| MRV | Raw evidence, method hash, data hash and reviewer trail complete. | Evidence cannot be traced or reproduced. |
| Financial | Cost stack and revenue assumptions disclosed and stress-tested. | Base case relies on speculative or non-admissible revenue. |
| Governance | Decision records, conflict register and escalation procedures active. | Unmanaged conflicts, capture risk or missing review authority. |

A pilot may proceed operationally while remaining non-admissible for capital-facing claims. Operational success and investment readiness are separate gates.

| **Result** | **Interpretation** |
|----|----|
| positive, robust, CI excludes zero | hypothesis provisionally supported |
| positive but CI includes zero | promising but not validated |
| mixed by outcome | partial support; claim must be narrowed |
| no difference | hypothesis not supported under tested conditions |
| negative | regression or failure; model must be revised |

No pilot result may be generalized beyond its climate, soil, management and design domain without a stated external-validity boundary.

## T.6 Identification strategy

Because whole ecosystems cannot always be randomized like laboratory units, Prometheus uses a hierarchy of causal identification methods.

**Preferred design — paired plots with baseline**

**Each treatment plot should be paired with a comparator plot similar in:**

soil type;

slope/aspect;

prior land use;

water access;

management intensity;

climate exposure;

starting vegetation condition.

**Primary estimator:**

tau_DiD = (Y_T,post - Y_T,pre) - (Y_C,post - Y_C,pre).

**Multi-site design — difference-in-differences with fixed effects**

**For multiple sites:**

Y_it = alpha_i + gamma_t + beta \* Treatment_i \* Post_t + delta X_it + epsilon_it

where:

alpha_i = site fixed effect;

gamma_t = time/season fixed effect;

X_it = weather, rainfall, soil and management covariates;

beta = estimated treatment effect.

**Synthetic control**

For RAP-level analysis, a synthetic comparator may be constructed from non-treated or less-treated sites using pre-treatment matching. Synthetic control may support portfolio-level inference but must not replace plot-level measurement.

**Confounder sensitivity**

**Every causal estimate must report:**

observed covariate balance;

sensitivity to rainfall/temperature anomalies;

operator effect;

missing data effect;

unobserved confounder sensitivity, including E-value or equivalent method where applicable.

**External validity boundary**

A result from Mediterranean pilot conditions may not automatically validate tropical, arid, temperate, urban or highland deployments. Claims must identify the ecological domain where inference is supported.

## T.7 Saturated Dynamics and Bounded Feedback Loops

**Bounded ecological and economic feedback loops**

Prometheus rejects unlimited positive feedback claims. Regenerative systems may exhibit reinforcing loops, but real systems saturate, regress or encounter constraints.

**Soil organic matter / SOC dynamics**

dO/dt = r_O \* M(t) \* (O_max - O_t) - delta_O \* D_t

where:

O_t = soil organic matter or SOC state;

O_max = site-specific equilibrium/carrying level;

M(t) = management quality / biomass return factor;

D_t = disturbance, erosion, drought, tillage or adverse event factor;

r_O = accumulation rate;

delta_O = degradation sensitivity.

**Infiltration dynamics**

dI/dt = r_I \* B_t \* (I_max - I_t) - delta_I \* Compaction_t

Infiltration cannot grow without limit. It approaches a soil- and structure-specific ceiling.

**Biomass dynamics**

dB/dt = r_B \* B_t \* (1 - B_t/K_B) - H_t - Stress_t

where K_B is biomass carrying capacity, H_t is harvest/pruning/removal and Stress_t includes water, heat, pest or management stress.

**Capital/regenerative finance loop**

**The capital loop must include marginal constraints:**

dK_regen/dt = a \* VerifiedPerformance_t \* InvestorConfidence_t - b \* ExecutionRisk_t - c \* Saturation_t - h \* Hazard_t

No capital-growth or valuation loop may be described as self-reinforcing without execution risk, saturation and hazard terms.

### Rule

Any phrase implying an unlimited spiral of abundance, automatic risk compression or perpetual regenerative uplift must be qualified by capacity, saturation, uncertainty and regression conditions.

# Appendix U — Frontier Research and Non-admissible Inquiry

**Tier Promotion Gate**

A research claim may move upward only through reproducibility, independent review and domain specificity. The minimum path is: research-only inquiry -\> independent replication -\> peer-reviewed or institutionally reviewed method -\> red-team review -\> domain-bounded pilot evidence -\> Claims Register status update. Promotion never operates retroactively to increase historical PRU value unless the relevant evidence bundle is re-scored under the current method and governance release.

Prometheus permits frontier inquiry but prevents frontier claims from influencing financial admissibility.

The following categories are classified as non-admissible unless and until supported by reproducible evidence sufficient to move into Tier 1 or Tier 2:

## U.1A Frontier promotion pathway

Frontier material may move from research-only status only through a documented promotion path. The path requires independent reproducibility, peer-reviewed or equivalent technical publication, review by relevant scientific experts, red-team dissent review and conversion into measurable MRV variables.

Promotion from research-only status does not automatically create PRU value. It only allows the claim to be considered for pilot design or indicator development under the ordinary evidence-tier rules.

structured water claims;

biofield claims;

subtle coherence claims;

non-standard energetic or informational claims;

any mechanism not measurable under recognized scientific or validated pilot protocols.

**Quarantine rule**

**Tier 3 material may be included only as:**

research horizon;

cultural or philosophical context;

non-admissible exploratory appendix;

explicitly excluded from PRU, RAP, investor reporting and legal representations.

**Tier 3 material must not:**

contribute to SRI, RPI, CESI, MIS or Conf_total;

be used in PRU value analysis;

appear in executive investor summaries except as excluded frontier research;

support claims of performance, value or risk reduction.

## U.2 Adversarial MRV and Cryptographic Provenance

**Adversarial MRV threat model**

Prometheus treats MRV as an adversarial environment. Any indicator that can affect value can be manipulated.

| **Indicator family** | **Manipulation risk** | **Countermeasure** |
|----|----|----|
| soil lab tests | selective sampling, sample substitution | sampling protocol, chain of custody, lab ID, GPS/time evidence |
| soil sensors | calibration drift, relocation, spoofed readings | signed device identity, calibration logs, anomaly detection |
| biomass logs | inflated biomass return | photo evidence, sample plots, random audits |
| yield records | selective harvest reporting | harvest logs, receipts, field audit, variance checks |
| biodiversity surveys | observer bias | protocol training, inter-rater reliability, photo/audio evidence |
| water data | sensor placement gaming | site map, sealed installation, cross-source weather data |
| human observations | incentive bias | reviewer rotation, audit trail, calibration exercises |
| remote sensing | misclassification | ground-truthing and model version disclosure |
| no-double-counting | duplicate claims across credits | REA invariant and claim registry |

### Provenance chain

**Every PRU-relevant data point must support:**

measurement -\> device/operator identity -\> timestamp -\> location -\> method version -\> raw data hash -\> transformed data hash -\> scoring method hash -\> PRU artifact hash.

### Cryptographic controls

**Prometheus may use, where appropriate:**

signed sensor logs;

Merkle commitments for time series;

method-hash registries;

content-addressed evidence bundles;

Holochain-compatible source-chain records;

selective disclosure or zero-knowledge proofs for threshold claims where raw disclosure is inappropriate.

### Acceptance condition

No PRU-relevant indicator is admissible unless its provenance path is inspectable and its manipulation risks have declared countermeasures.

### Proof of Physical Origin (PPO) Protocol

**Purpose and Boundary**

The Proof of Physical Origin (PPO) protocol is an adversarial‑MRV countermeasure against synthetic evidence, AI‑generated data, and ASI‑level manipulation. It requires that any high‑impact evidence (soil, water, biomass, biodiversity, yield) must be accompanied by a verifiable physical‑origin proof that cannot be fully replicated by digital synthesis alone.

PPO does not replace traditional provenance (method hash, data hash, source, timestamp, location, reviewer trail). It adds a physical‑layer verification that remains independently auditable even if all digital signatures, hashes, and cryptographic controls are compromised.

The PPO protocol applies only to:

- Evidence that materially affects PRU value, RAP inclusion, or investor‑facing claims (Tier 1 and high‑confidence Tier 2 indicators).

- Baseline and regenerative‑delta measurements for soil, water, biodiversity, biomass, and yield.

- Any data point that, if falsified, could change the PRU maturity class or U_max calibration.

It does not apply to low‑risk operational logs, routine maintenance records, or qualitative observations (unless those are used to support a material PRU claim).

**PPO Core Principle**

No digital synthesis, no matter how perfect, can reproduce the full physical context of a real field sample without leaving detectable gaps. The PPO protocol exploits three irreducible physical properties:

1.  Time‑space continuity: real samples have continuous histories; synthetic samples can be generated with plausible metadata but lack continuous sensor/observer chains.

2.  Cross‑source divergence: real samples show consistent deviations across independent measurement methods; synthetic samples often show implausible convergence.

3.  Physical entropy: real samples contain microscopic, isotopic, or microbial signatures that are statistically improbable to generate synthetically without detectable artifacts.

**PPO Evidence Requirements**

For any high‑impact evidence record, the following additional fields are required:

| **Field** | **Description** | **Acceptance condition** |
|----|----|----|
| PPO_Timestamp | Coordinated timestamp from at least two independent sources (e.g., GPS, local atomic clock, network NTP) | All timestamps must agree within a predefined tolerance (e.g., ±5 seconds). |
| PPO_Chain_of_Custody | Signed log of every person, device, or process that handled the sample or data | No gaps \> 24 hours without explanation; all signatures valid. |
| PPO_Cross_Source_Index | At least two independent measurement methods for the same variable (e.g., lab test + sensor + satellite) | Correlation must be within expected physical bounds; divergence must be explained. |
| PPO_Physical_Entropy_Signature | Optional but recommended: isotopic fingerprint, microbial DNA barcode, or microscopic imagery | Unique to the site; stored as a hash reference; verifiable by independent lab. |
| PPO_Field_Continuity_Proof | Sequential imagery (photo or video) showing the sample being collected, with geotag and visible time reference (e.g., newspaper, clock, or GPS screen) | Must show the sample, the collector, the site, and the timestamp in one continuous shot. |
| PPO_Independent_Witness | At least one independent witness (steward, auditor, or community member) who can attest to the collection | Witness identity recorded; witness signature or recorded statement preserved. |

**PPO Scoring and Admissibility**

Each high‑impact evidence record receives a PPO Confidence Score (0.0 to 1.0), calculated as:

PPO_Score = (2 × T + 3 × C + 2 × X + 2 × E + 1 × F + 1 × W) / 11

Where:

- T = 1 if all timestamps agree; 0 otherwise.

- C = 1 if chain‑of‑custody is complete and signed; 0.5 if partial; 0 if absent.

- X = 1 if at least two independent measurement methods converge within expected tolerance; 0.5 if partial; 0 if divergence.

- E = 1 if physical‑entropy signature is present and verified; 0.5 if present but not verified; 0 if absent.

- F = 1 if field‑continuity proof is available and clear; 0.5 if available but ambiguous; 0 if absent.

- W = 1 if independent witness is recorded and contactable; 0.5 if recorded but not contactable; 0 if absent.

Admissibility rule:

- PPO_Score ≥ 0.8: Full admissibility; may contribute to PRU value, RAP inclusion, and investor‑facing claims.

- 0.5 ≤ PPO_Score \< 0.8: Conditional admissibility; may be used for internal review and due diligence but not for capital‑facing claims without additional verification.

- PPO_Score \< 0.5: Non‑admissible for financial use; may remain as research/operational context only.

**PPO Anti‑Gaming Controls**

To prevent gaming of the PPO system, the following safeguards apply:

| **Control** | **Description** | **Consequence if violated** |
|----|----|----|
| Random spot checks | An independent auditor may re‑sample up to 10% of high‑impact evidence records per year. | If a discrepancy is found, all related records are downgraded to conditional or non‑admissible. |
| Cross‑site consistency | Records from the same site over time must show physically plausible trends. | Sudden unexplained jumps trigger manual review and potential score reduction. |
| Unannounced collection | Auditors may request unannounced sample collection to verify routine procedures. | Failure to comply triggers review of all related evidence. |
| Whistleblower pathway | Any participant may report suspected PPO fraud through a protected governance channel. | Investigation is mandatory; fraudulent records become permanently non‑admissible. |

**PPO Operational Implementation**

For field deployments, the minimum viable PPO implementation may use:

- Smartphone‑captured geotagged photos with visible time references (watch, newspaper, screen).

- At least two independent observers (steward + community witness).

- Cross‑checking with existing sensor logs and satellite imagery.

- Optional low‑cost isotopic or microbial sampling for high‑value sites.

For institutional or RAP‑level reporting, stronger PPO measures are required:

- Independent sample collection by a third‑party auditor.

- Chain‑of‑custody documented with signed logs and timestamps.

- At least three independent measurement methods for soil, water, and biomass indicators.

PPO Limitations and Residual Risk

PPO reduces but does not eliminate the risk of synthetic evidence. The following residual risks remain:

- Compromise of physical samples (e.g., sample substitution at the lab).

- Compromise of witnesses (coercion or bribery).

- Sophisticated physical mimicry (e.g., fabricating isotopic signatures).

These residual risks are addressed through:

- Multi‑site correlation: comparing trends across multiple OHEs to detect anomalies.

- Independent lab redundancy: splitting samples and sending them to different accredited labs.

- Governance review and red‑team testing.

PPO Governance Review

Any material PPO failure (score below 0.5 for a high‑impact record, or detection of fraud) must be:

1.  Logged in the Claims Register with a PPO_Alert flag.

2.  Reviewed by the Independent Methodology Committee within 30 days.

3.  If confirmed as fraud or manipulation, the affected PRU, RAP, or investor‑facing material is declared non‑admissible and publicly disclosed (unless legally restricted).

**PPO Final Rule**

No high‑impact evidence record may be used for capital‑facing PRU or RAP purposes unless it has a PPO_Score ≥ 0.8. This rule applies even if the digital provenance (hashes, signatures, methods) is otherwise perfect. PPO adds a physical‑layer gate that cannot be bypassed by digital synthesis alone.

## U.3 Inter-rater Reliability Specification

### Human indicator reliability

Indicators involving human assessment require calibration and inter-rater reliability.

**Examples:**

soil structure score;

pest/disease pressure score;

biodiversity field observation;

management quality score;

pruning/biomass-return verification;

damage or disturbance assessment.

### Minimum protocol

written scoring rubric;

training examples;

blind duplicate assessment on sample subset;

Cohen’s kappa for categorical scores;

ICC for continuous scores;

reviewer identity and calibration date;

disagreement resolution rule.

Human-rated indicators are non-admissible for PRU scoring unless reliability exceeds the method-specific threshold or receives a confidence penalty.

## U.4 SyntroGIFT Quarantine Zone and Health-Claims Firewall

The term SyntroGIFT and associated hypotheses linking regenerative agriculture, nature exposure, nutrition, movement, community participation or ecological immersion to specific human-health outcomes are classified as Tier 3 exploratory hypotheses.

They may be studied only in voluntary, consent-based, ethics-reviewed and legally reviewed research modules separate from core Prometheus operations. These modules must remain external to PRU value analysis, RAP underwriting, governance authority, investor-facing claims and capital-facing admissibility.

SyntroGIFT and related human-health hypotheses shall not:

• contribute to any PRU indicator, SRI, RPI, CESI, MIS, Conf_total, maturity score, U_max rationale or PRU value output;\
• appear in investor materials, data rooms, underwriting memoranda, public claims or capital-facing documentation as a source of value, yield, avoided cost, risk reduction, insurance value, reimbursement value or public-health return;\
• support public procurement, insurance, reimbursement, food-as-medicine, clinical, preventive-health, therapeutic, diagnostic, wellness or medical claims unless independently validated by competent health authorities and separated from the Prometheus PRU/RAP core;\
• rank participants, affect governance rights, alter eligibility, influence legal gates or affect access to land, capital, tokens, services or participation.

Violation of this quarantine renders the affected PRU, RAP, report, dashboard, claim, data-room item or capital-facing document non-admissible until corrected, disclosed and reviewed.

# Appendix V — Portfolio Risk Model

## V.0 Portfolio demand and liquidity boundary

A RAP is not investible merely because PRUs are well-defined. Portfolio demand must be evidenced through anchor buyers, offtake discussions, insurance pathways, regulated wrappers, investor diligence and realistic exit routes. Until these exist, RAP logic remains portfolio architecture rather than market liquidity.

| **Capital-readiness item** | **Evidence required** |
|----|----|
| Anchor demand | LOI, offtake discussion, procurement channel or institutional interest record. |
| Regulated wrapper | Fund/SPV/legal structure reviewed by jurisdiction. |
| Insurance pathway | Parametric or conventional risk-transfer assessment where applicable. |
| Pricing discipline | Method value, market indication and sensitivity kept distinct. |
| Exit logic | Refinancing, sale, long-term hold, public procurement or regulated fund pathway. |

Prometheus shall not claim risk compression merely because systems are regenerative. Risk compression is an empirical portfolio claim requiring distributional evidence.

**Loss distribution**

**For each site i, define annual loss or underperformance variable:**

L_i = ExpectedPerformance_i - RealizedPerformance_i.

**For a RAP portfolio with weights w_i:**

L_RAP = sum_i w_i \* L_i.

**Risk must be reported using:**

variance or downside deviation;

drawdown frequency;

correlation matrix among sites;

tail metric such as CVaR / Expected Shortfall.

**CVaR**

At confidence level q, Value-at-Risk VaR_q is the loss quantile. Conditional Value-at-Risk is:

CVaR_q = E\[L_RAP \| L_RAP \>= VaR_q\].

Prometheus may claim risk improvement only if regenerative maturity is associated with lower downside risk under pre-specified tests.

**V.1A Tail dependence and copula stress testing**

Portfolio diversification fails when extreme events become correlated. RAP risk analysis must therefore distinguish ordinary correlation from tail dependence. Drought, heat, fire, flood, pest pressure, legal restriction, market stress, operator failure, MRV failure and banking/custody disruption may affect multiple OHEs at the same time, especially within the same bioregion or supply-chain context.

Where sufficient data exist, RAP stress testing should model the joint distribution of site losses through a copula C(u_1, ..., u_n), where u_i = F_i(L_i) represents the marginal loss percentile of site i. Tail-dependence coefficients lambda_U and lambda_L should be estimated or conservatively assumed when extreme co-movement is plausible.

| **Risk layer** | **Required treatment** | **Minimum disclosure** |
|----|----|----|
| Bioregional drought / heat | Stress multiple sites in the same climate or watershed simultaneously. | Bioregion grouping, drought scenario and assumed tail correlation. |
| Fire / flood / storm | Model clustered losses where sites share hazard zones or infrastructure exposure. | Hazard map, event severity and loss assumption. |
| Market / offtake failure | Stress revenue channels shared across sites. | Buyer concentration, price shock and liquidity effect. |
| Operator concentration | Stress failure of a common operator or service provider. | Operator exposure by site and substitution plan. |
| MRV or data failure | Stress sensor, lab, API, dashboard or verification failures affecting multiple sites. | Evidence continuity and fallback method. |
| Legal / regulatory shock | Stress jurisdiction-wide restriction or classification change. | Affected legal wrapper and required review. |

If pilot or portfolio data are insufficient for formal copula estimation, Prometheus must use conservative scenario matrices rather than assume independence. Independence is not the default for ecological infrastructure. The default is disclosed uncertainty, conservative stress and progressive calibration.

**Time-positive value with hazard**

Time-positive value must include regression hazard.

**Illustrative DCF form:**

V_t = sum\_{s=t}^{T} E\[CF_s \* R_s\] / (1+r)^{s-t}

where R_s is probability of remaining in or above the required regenerative maturity state at time s.

**Hazard model:**

**R_s = exp(- integral_t^s h(u) du)**

where h(u) includes drought, management failure, pest/disease, legal restriction, market failure or MRV failure.

### Rule

No “time-positive” claim is admissible unless it includes a non-zero probability of regression or failure.

## V.2 No-double-counting Invariant

**Formal no-double-counting invariant**

Every ecological gain used in PRU, RAP, credit, report or financial representation must be uniquely identified by:

spatial boundary;

temporal period;

resource/process affected;

measurement method;

claim category;

allocation fraction;

claimant/instrument;

method hash;

evidence hash.

Let G_j be ecological gain unit j. Let a\_{j,c} be the allocation fraction of gain j to claim channel c.

**The invariant is:**

sum_c a\_{j,c} \<= 1 for every ecological gain G_j.

If one ecological improvement supports multiple narratives (carbon, water, biodiversity), the system must distinguish:

separate measured gains;

co-benefit reporting without monetized duplicate claim;

allocated fractional claims;

non-admissible narrative context.

**REA / Valueflows predicate**

A Valueflows event cannot create more claimable ecological value than the measured resource/process change it references.

**Predicate:**

For each ecological_resource_change_id, total_claimed_quantity \<= verified_quantity \* admissible_allocation_factor.

Violation of this predicate blocks PRU admissibility until resolved.

**V.2A Cross-System No-Double-Spending Threat Model**

**V.2A.1 Purpose**

The formal no-double-counting invariant prevents the same ecological gain from being claimed across incompatible ecological, financial or reporting pathways.

This section extends that invariant to cross-system, registry-facing, API-facing, token-facing, bridge-facing, wrapped-representation and settlement-facing contexts.

The purpose is to ensure that no regenerative event, ecological improvement, verified indicator, PRU-relevant evidence package, RAP reference, credit, certificate, token representation, dashboard status, registry entry, custody record or settlement record can be reused, duplicated, wrapped, bridged, pledged, monetised or externally represented in incompatible claim contexts.

The controlling principle is:

one underlying event;

one evidence lineage;

one active admissible claim context;

one allocation rule;

one Claims Register status;

no duplicated value.

**V.2A.2 Event Identity Tuple**

Every PRU-relevant ecological gain or regenerative event shall be assigned an event identity tuple before it can support PRU analysis, RAP aggregation, external reporting, credit reference, digital representation or market-facing use.

The event identity tuple shall include, at minimum:

1.  event_id;

2.  site_id;

3.  spatial boundary;

4.  temporal boundary;

5.  resource or process affected;

6.  measurement method;

7.  method hash;

8.  raw data hash;

9.  evidence package hash;

10. reviewer trail;

11. claim category;

12. allocation fraction;

13. claimant or instrument;

14. Claims Register entry;

15. admissibility status;

16. revocation status;

17. prior-use status;

18. permitted representation scope.

An event without a complete identity tuple may remain visible as operational or research context, but it shall not enter PRU value analysis, RAP aggregation, token representation, credit reference, investor-facing reporting or market-facing communication.

**V.2A.3 Cross-System Representation Tuple**

Any external representation of a Prometheus event, PRU reference, RAP reference, verified indicator, claim, credit, certificate, token, credential, registry entry, custody record, settlement record or API state shall require a cross-system representation tuple.

The representation tuple shall include:

1.  source event_id;

2.  source evidence package hash;

3.  source Claims Register entry;

4.  source system identifier;

5.  target system identifier;

6.  representation type;

7.  representation scope;

8.  legal-instrument reference where applicable;

9.  token or credential identifier where applicable;

10. registry identifier where applicable;

11. custody or settlement identifier where applicable;

12. bridge message identifier where applicable;

13. oracle dependency where applicable;

14. finality assumption where applicable;

15. replay-protection method where applicable;

16. lock / mint / burn / release rule where applicable;

17. conflict-resolution rule;

18. revocation and quarantine rule;

19. reviewer approval;

20. governance decision record.

No external representation is admissible unless the source event, source evidence, source claim, representation scope and target-system state can be reconciled.

**V.2A.4 Duplicate-Claim Detection Rule**

A duplicate-claim condition exists where the same underlying ecological gain, evidence package, verified indicator, PRU-relevant event or claim is used in two or more incompatible contexts.

Duplicate-claim conditions include, but are not limited to:

- the same ecological gain being allocated above 100%;

- the same event supporting both monetized credit issuance and full PRU uplift without allocation;

- the same evidence package being used for two incompatible investor-facing claims;

- the same PRU-relevant improvement being represented in two registries without reconciliation;

- the same RAP exposure being reported as available in more than one instrument;

- a token representation being issued without locking, retiring, allocating or otherwise constraining the source claim;

- a wrapped representation being minted without source-state verification;

- a bridge message being replayed or reused across systems;

- an oracle-dependent state being accepted without source verification;

- a dashboard state being treated as market or legal status without Claims Register support.

When a duplicate-claim condition is detected, the affected pathway shall be placed in quarantine until review is complete.

**V.2A.5 Bridge, Wrapper and Registry-Sync Boundary**

Bridge, wrapper, registry-sync and cross-ledger mechanisms are not default Prometheus pathways.

They are high-risk interoperability pathways.

They may be researched, simulated and sandbox-tested, but they shall not be admitted for PRU, RAP, token-facing, investor-facing, insurance-facing, procurement-facing, settlement-facing or market-facing use unless the following conditions are satisfied:

1.  source event uniqueness is established;

2.  source evidence lineage is preserved;

3.  source state and target state are verifiable;

4.  finality assumptions are documented;

5.  replay protection exists;

6.  duplicate minting or duplicate representation is prevented;

7.  lock / mint / burn / release logic is tested where applicable;

8.  oracle dependencies are reviewed;

9.  cross-system message validation is tested;

10. failure and rollback scenarios are documented;

11. registry conflict rules are defined;

12. revocation and quarantine procedures exist;

13. legal treatment is reviewed where applicable;

14. governance has admitted the pathway;

15. Claims Register status supports the representation.

No bridge, wrapper or registry-sync pathway may be represented as safe merely because it is technically possible.

**V.2A.6 Quarantine and Adverse Integrity Record**

Any suspected double-counting, double-claiming, double-representation, replay, duplicate minting, bridge inconsistency, registry conflict, oracle manipulation, custody inconsistency, settlement inconsistency or unauthorized external representation shall trigger an adverse integrity review.

The review shall produce, where applicable:

1.  adverse integrity record ID;

2.  affected event_id;

3.  affected PRU reference;

4.  affected RAP reference;

5.  affected token, registry, API, dashboard, custody or settlement record;

6.  suspected failure mode;

7.  evidence reviewed;

8.  systems affected;

9.  reviewer trail;

10. provisional status;

11. quarantine status;

12. corrective action;

13. revocation action where applicable;

14. legal review status where applicable;

15. governance decision;

16. Claims Register update.

While quarantine is active, the affected event, claim, PRU reference, RAP reference, token representation, registry entry, dashboard state, API response, custody record or settlement record shall not be used for capital-facing, investor-facing, insurance-facing, procurement-facing, token-facing or public financial claims.

**V.2A.7 Interoperability Acceptance Criteria**

A cross-system representation pathway is admissible only when the following controls are complete:

1.  event identity tuple;

2.  representation tuple;

3.  no-double-counting invariant check;

4.  duplicate-claim detection rule;

5.  bridge or registry-sync threat model where applicable;

6.  oracle-risk review where applicable;

7.  finality and replay-protection memo where applicable;

8.  lock / mint / burn / release test where applicable;

9.  quarantine and revocation procedure;

10. Claims Register linkage;

11. governance decision;

12. legal review where applicable.

If any required control is absent, incomplete, inconsistent, expired or unresolved, the pathway remains non-admitted.

**V.2A.8 Go / No-Go Rule**

Go:

Prometheus may use the no-double-counting invariant, event identity tuple, representation tuple, duplicate-claim detection rule and quarantine procedure to structure internal methodology and sandbox testing.

Conditional Go:

Prometheus may test interoperability, registry synchronization, oracle feeds, bridge logic, wrapped representations and external settlement pathways in non-production environments when clearly labelled as non-admitted, non-capital-facing, non-investor-facing and non-market-facing.

No-Go:

Prometheus may not describe any PRU, RAP, credit, certificate, token, credential, dashboard state, API state, registry entry, custody record, settlement record, bridge message or wrapped representation as duplicate-proof, double-spend-resistant, cross-system-admitted, settlement-ready, market-admissible or legally transferable unless the corresponding controls, reviews, governance decision and Claims Register status support that claim.

The lowest unresolved integrity gate controls the permitted representation.

## V.3 Reproducibility Package Specification

**Reproducible PRU number**

Every PRU value, maturity class or verified indicator must be reproducible from an artifact package.

**Minimum package:**

{\
"pru_id": "PRU-...",\
"site_id": "...",\
"method_version": "PRU_METHOD_v1.0.0",\
"method_hash": "sha256:...",\
"data_manifest_hash": "sha256:...",\
"raw_data_hashes": \[\],\
"normalization_table_hash": "sha256:...",\
"confidence_model_hash": "sha256:...",\
"scoring_code_hash": "sha256:...",\
"claims_register_version": "...",\
"reviewer_log_hash": "sha256:...",\
"created_at": "ISO-8601",\
"admissibility_status": "draft\|reviewed\|admissible\|rejected"\
}

A PRU number without a reproducibility package is a draft analytic output only.

## V.4 Scientific Red-team and Governance

**Independent red-team mandate**

**Prometheus shall maintain a red-team function with authority to challenge:**

PRU formulas;

indicator normalization;

pilot design;

MRV manipulation risks;

AI-generated analysis;

financial claims;

scientific citations;

legal boundary language.

Red-team findings must be preserved even when not adopted. Material dissent must be attached to method versions.

**Scientific committee**

**A scientific committee should include at minimum expertise in:**

agronomy/soil science;

hydrology;

agroforestry/syntropic systems;

statistics/causal inference;

distributed systems;

valuation/risk;

law/compliance.

No committee member should be treated as sole authority. Authority emerges from method, evidence, reproducibility and governance record.

# Appendix W: AI and Offline Runtime, Institutional Interface, and Authority Boundary

## W.1 AI and Offline Resilience Boundary — Regenerative Intelligence Metabolism 

Prometheus is not a cloud-first artificial intelligence platform. It is a regenerative intelligence infrastructure designed to preserve source-layer fidelity, local evidence continuity, proportional computation, human review, governance authority and legal admissibility under real field conditions.

The purpose of this section is to define the boundary between artificial intelligence, offline operation, local evidence continuity and institutional authority.

Prometheus may use AI to assist observation structuring, evidence organization, anomaly detection, schema checking, local reasoning, draft reporting, knowledge retrieval, data-room preparation and technical review. AI may not create ecological truth, validate MRV, approve PRU value, authorize RAP inclusion, determine legal rights, issue tokens, approve investor-facing claims, override governance, replace legal review or convert non-admissible research into financial admissibility.

The controlling principle is:

AI assists interpretation.\
MRV governs evidence.\
Methodology governs admissibility.\
Governance governs authorization.\
Legal structure governs rights.\
Living systems remain the source layer.

The technical invariant is:

AI_authority = 0

This means that no model output, dashboard answer, API response, agent workflow, local inference, cloud synthesis, frontier-model report or autonomous recommendation has authority inside Prometheus unless it passes through the applicable evidence, review, governance and legal pathway.

**W.1.1 Regenerative Intelligence Metabolism**

Regenerative Intelligence Metabolism is the Prometheus principle that intelligence should be deployed at the lowest sufficient level of computational intensity capable of producing reliable, reviewable and context-appropriate support.

The system should not use more computation than the task requires. It should not send local evidence to cloud infrastructure when local processing is sufficient. It should not use frontier models where deterministic rules, schemas, small local models or reviewer workflows are adequate.

The routing principle is:

edge where evidence is local;\
small models where tasks are narrow;\
local reasoning where context matters;\
independent verification where consistency matters;\
cloud only where complexity justifies escalation;\
human review where authority is required.

This is both a technical discipline and an ecological discipline.

It reduces unnecessary energy use, water impact, cloud dependency, privacy exposure, latency, vendor lock-in and authority confusion. It also preserves the Prometheus alignment with living systems: intelligence is distributed, contextual, adaptive and relational.

A task may be routed according to the following Word-safe logic:

Task_Intensity = evidence need + reasoning complexity + privacy sensitivity + authority risk + institutional consequence

If Task_Intensity is low, deterministic rules or local edge processing should be used.

If Task_Intensity is moderate, a small local model or local reasoner may be used.

If Task_Intensity is high, institutional review, legal review or frontier-model drafting support may be used, but only as draft assistance.

If Authority_Risk is high, AI output must remain non-authoritative and must be routed to human, governance, MRV or legal review.

**W.1.2 AI and Offline Boundary**

Prometheus must remain operational under field conditions where internet connectivity is intermittent, expensive, fragile, unavailable or undesirable.

Offline mode is not an emergency exception. It is a required operating state.

A Prometheus node should be able to:

• receive field events;\
• store local observations;\
• preserve device or agent identity;\
• attach timestamp and location metadata;\
• maintain method version;\
• preserve evidence attachments;\
• compute local review signals where permitted;\
• maintain offline queues;\
• sign local evidence bundles;\
• preserve conflict state;\
• synchronize later when network access becomes available.

The offline evidence rule is:

No network availability is required for local evidence to exist.

Network availability affects synchronization. It does not determine whether a valid local observation may be recorded. The minimum offline evidence bundle should include:

• event ID;\
• agent or device identity;\
• timestamp;\
• location or site reference;\
• method version;\
• raw observation or attachment reference;\
• local hash;\
• queue status;\
• reviewer notes where available;\
• conflict status;\
• synchronization metadata.

**W.1.3 Model Routing Architecture**

Prometheus uses model routing, not model worship.

Named models are implementation examples, not constitutional dependencies. The architecture classifies AI by function, risk, energy cost, privacy exposure and review requirements.

| **Layer** | **AI role** | **Appropriate tool class** | **Controlling boundary** |
|----|----|----|----|
| Field layer | Thresholds, compact events, emergency flags, simple classification. | Rules, deterministic filters, no LLM where possible. | Field reliability and low-power operation take priority. |
| Edge gateway | Parsing, priority classification, JSON generation, operator-note normalization. | Small local model or quantized open-weight model. | Outputs remain draft evidence events until validated. |
| Local node | Local RAG, MRV evidence assistance, Valueflows mapping, offline queue interpretation. | Local reasoner or self-hostable model. | Model assists interpretation but does not create PRU value or MRV validity. |
| Verifier layer | Logic check, schema check, consistency check, hallucination check, confidence sanity check. | Independent model, rule engine or deterministic validator. | Output is a review signal, not authority. |
| Source-layer record | Provenance, signatures, source-chain record, method hash, data hash. | Holochain-compatible source-layer logic, cryptographic signing, structured records. | Trust follows provenance, method and review trail. |
| Sync layer | Store-and-forward synchronization, conflict assist, compressed evidence movement. | Reticulum, LAN, internet, VPS bootstrap or equivalent. | Network availability affects synchronization, not evidence existence. |
| Institutional layer | Investor digest drafts, legal memo drafts, scientific synthesis, partner materials. | Cloud or frontier model only where complexity justifies cost. | Draft-only; never an admissibility decision. |
| Authority layer | MRV validity, PRU/RAP admissibility, governance approval, legal rights. | No AI authority. | Authority remains with methodology, reviewers, governance and legal structure. |

The operational rule is:

Smallest competent tool first.\
Escalation only when justified.\
No model is authoritative.\
No model output becomes admissible without review.

**W.1.4 Source-Layer Fidelity**

Prometheus must preserve the distinction between generated language and source-layer evidence.

AI may summarize evidence.\
AI may compare evidence.\
AI may detect inconsistency.\
