| *AML / KYC / sanctions screening* | *Investor-facing participation, token-facing pathways, capital entry, custody, settlement and regulated counterparty workflows.* | *AML / KYC may become relevant where capital flows, investor onboarding, token services, custody or regulated counterparties are involved.* | *No capital-facing or token-facing pathway without counterparty classification, onboarding controls, sanctions screening where required and regulated-service-provider review where applicable.* |
| *GDPR / data protection* | *Field data, human observations, participant records, access logs, AI/offline modules, dashboards and data-room access.* | *Ecological data may be non-personal, but participant, operator, household, health, location or behavioral data may trigger obligations.* | *Requires data minimization, lawful basis, consent where applicable, access control, retention limits, data-room segregation and exclusion of human-state research from PRU admissibility.* |
| *AI governance / EU AI Act-type analysis* | *AI-assisted MRV, AI/offline modules, dashboards, inference engines, automated classification and decision support.* | *AI may assist analysis, review and coordination, but does not create ecological truth, legal rights, PRU value, RAP maturity, market demand, investor entitlement, governance authority or production safety.* | *Requires role classification, human oversight, data-governance review, model-output boundary, audit trail and Claims Register alignment where outputs influence external reliance.* |
| *Cybersecurity / operational resilience* | *Runtime releases, zomes, APIs, dashboards, VPS deployment, bridge connectors, registry connectors, data pipelines and key management.* | *Runtime components may support Prometheus only within admitted scope. Software existence, build success or local testing does not create production admission.* | *Requires Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package, security review where applicable, rollback, incident response, monitoring, Runtime Release Decision Record and governance approval.* |
| *IFRS / GAAP / accounting treatment* | *V_base, productive cash flow, contractual revenue, PRU-adjusted value, Layer 2 value and scenario-only uplift.* | *Productive or contractual value may be recognised only when accounting criteria are met. PRU uplift is analytical unless contractually and legally supported.* | *No asset-recognition, revenue-recognition, balance-sheet, fair-value or accounting-treatment claim from PRU score alone. Accounting review required.* |
| *Public procurement / public-program eligibility* | *Public resilience programmes, procurement pathways, municipal demand, public-private partnerships and grants.* | *Prometheus evidence may support public-sector diligence where procurement, eligibility and audit rules are satisfied.* | *No procurement-ready, public-program-recognised or grant-eligible claim without authority pathway, eligibility review, procurement counsel and Anchor Demand File support.* |
| *Data-room / due-diligence standards* | *Legal, MRV, integrity, runtime, operational, financial, market and governance evidence layers.* | *A Prometheus data room supports institutional review but does not create demand, legality, liquidity, certification or production readiness.* | *Data-room completeness must not be represented as regulatory approval, market validation, legal admission or investor readiness.* |
| *Claims Register / disclosure control* | *Public claims, investor-facing claims, dashboard labels, API labels, regulatory mapping and external representations.* | *Claims Register status is the controlling internal disclosure boundary.* | *No claim may exceed its Claim ID, evidence status, legal status, market status, integrity status, runtime status or governance status.* |
| *Anchor Demand / market validation* | *RAP-M5, RAP-M6, RAP-M7, counterparty review, offtake, insurance, procurement, bank or public-program pathways.* | *Market validation is not presumed from portfolio aggregation or legal structure.* | *Market-facing language requires Anchor Demand File, ADE classification, counterparty qualification, price-discovery memo, liquidity / exit disclosure, legal review and Claims Register update.* |
| *Jurisdictional legal opinion* | *PRU, RAP, TRBK, HoloFuel, token, dashboard, API, access covenant, legal wrapper and bundled structures.* | *Legal interpretation is jurisdiction-specific and instrument-specific.* | *Legal Opinion Package is mandatory before any capital-facing, investor-facing, token-facing, instrument-facing, revenue-facing, land-rights-facing or market-facing use.* |

> **Regulatory interpretation rule: no legal opinion, no legal-status claim; no Anchor Demand File, no market-readiness claim; no no-double-counting controls, no external representation claim; no Runtime Release Decision Record, no runtime-readiness claim; no Claims Register support, no public regulatory claim.**

**AA.3 Regulatory Risk Assessment by Instrument Type**

This section assesses regulatory risk by pathway, instrument type or external-use mode. The purpose is not to classify any Prometheus pathway as regulated or unregulated. The purpose is to identify when a Prometheus concept may move from architecture, methodology, evidence, governance or runtime support into a legal, financial, market, token-facing, custody-facing, settlement-facing, procurement-facing, insurance-facing or investor-facing pathway requiring professional review.

The regulatory risk level is indicative only. Actual classification depends on jurisdiction, facts, instrument terms, counterparty type, marketing language, transferability, custody pathway, settlement pathway, rights created, revenue exposure, investor expectations, data-processing context, AI use and external reliance.

| ***Pathway / instrument type*** | ***Primary regulatory domains*** | ***Key risks*** | ***Required controls before external use*** | ***Permitted status before controls are complete*** |
|----|----|----|----|----|
| *PRU-linked private contract* | *Contract law, tax, anti-greenwashing, consumer / commercial law where applicable.* | *PRU may be misread as a legal right, asset, price, guarantee, certification or enforceable entitlement.* | *Legal review, rights mapping, clear contract terms, PRU boundary language, Claims Register support and evidence scope disclosure.* | *Analytical reference only. No enforceable-rights, return, liquidity, asset-backing or certification claim.* |
| *PRU value analysis used in reporting* | *Sustainability disclosure, anti-greenwashing, accounting review, audit / assurance standards where applicable.* | *PRU score may be treated as regulated valuation, rating, accounting value or verified impact certificate.* | *Methodology disclosure, bounded PRU function, U_max rationale, Conf_total, evidence package, reviewer trail, uncertainty disclosure and Claims Register status.* | *Internal analytical use only. No public valuation, rating, accounting, certification or investor-reliance claim.* |
| *RAP as internal portfolio-readiness structure* | *Governance, portfolio-risk review, data-room diligence, anti-greenwashing.* | *RAP-M4 may be mistaken for market-readiness, investability, liquidity or demand.* | *RAP-M4 record, eligible PRU screening, diversification logic, portfolio-risk review, data-room maturity and Claims Register status.* | *Portfolio-readable only. Not market-facing.* |
| *RAP as fund, SPV, note programme or pooled exposure* | *Fund regulation, securities law, MiFID / AIFMD-type analysis, tax, investor-protection rules.* | *Pooled capital, managerial reliance, investor expectation, marketing restrictions and investor eligibility.* | *Jurisdiction-specific legal opinion, fund / SPV / note structuring, investor eligibility rules, disclosure package, governance decision and Claims Register status.* | *Not investable. No fund, note, SPV, pooled-return or investor-exposure claim.* |
| *RAP-M5 counterparty-reviewable pathway* | *Market conduct, procurement / insurance / banking review, anti-greenwashing, disclosure controls.* | *Non-binding interest may be overstated as demand, liquidity, offtake, financing or market validation.* | *Anchor Demand File, ADE classification, counterparty qualification record, binding / non-binding status, price-discovery memo, liquidity and exit disclosures, Claims Register update.* | *Counterparty-reviewable only. No binding demand or market-active claim.* |
| *RAP-M6 legally wrapped pathway* | *Legal structuring, securities / fund law, tax, custody / settlement review where applicable.* | *Legal wrapper may be mistaken for liquidity, demand, regulatory approval or investor protection.* | *Legal wrapper, rights mapping, disclosure package, investor eligibility, custody / settlement pathway where applicable, governance decision and Claims Register support.* | *Legally wrapped only. No liquidity, secondary-market or market-active claim.* |
| *RAP-M7 market-active pathway* | *Securities / fund law, market conduct, custody, settlement, AML / KYC, tax, disclosure and counterparty law.* | *Market-active status may be extended beyond the actual instrument, counterparty, jurisdiction or binding arrangement.* | *Binding instrument, closed financing, binding offtake, insurance / procurement / public-program pathway or operationally effective external demand, legal review, disclosure package and Claims Register status.* | *Market-active only within admitted scope. No generalized investability or liquidity claim.* |
| *Token-facing PRU / RAP representation* | *MiCA / crypto-asset regulation, securities law, AML / KYC, custody, transfer rules, tax.* | *Token classification, issuer liability, securities status, custody risk, transferability, investor expectation and digital double-spending.* | *Token classification, securities analysis, legal opinion, custody review, settlement review, no-double-counting controls, disclosure package, Claims Register status and governance approval.* | *Token representation not admitted. No tokenised PRU, tokenised RAP, transferable PRU or token-backed claim.* |
| *TRBK governance / access pathway* | *Token / membership / consumer law, DAO governance, securities analysis where economic rights are introduced.* | *Governance or access layer may be misread as investor entitlement, profit participation, asset backing or transferable right.* | *Functional classification, legal review, rights mapping, governance terms, transfer restrictions where applicable and disclosure boundary.* | *Governance / access thesis only. No investor-entitlement or economic-rights claim.* |
| *HoloFuel operational pathway* | *Payment / e-money / crypto-asset / operational infrastructure analysis where applicable.* | *Operational transaction layer may be confused with PRU value, investor exposure or financial instrument.* | *Functional and jurisdictional review, operational-use boundary, payment / crypto classification where applicable and disclosure controls.* | *Operational infrastructure thesis only. No PRU value or investor-rights claim.* |
| *Revenue-sharing agreement* | *Contract law, securities law, tax, consumer / commercial law.* | *Revenue exposure may trigger securities or investment-contract analysis, especially if transferable or marketed to investors.* | *Legal opinion, revenue-source verification, transfer restrictions, investor eligibility, tax review, disclosure package and Claims Register status.* | *Not capital-facing. No revenue entitlement or yield claim.* |
| *Land-related right, lease, usufruct, easement or conservation instrument* | *Real estate law, land law, tax, environmental law, securities analysis where bundled with investment exposure.* | *Land rights may be overstated, bundled incorrectly or confused with PRU / RAP rights.* | *Land-title review, rights mapping, enforceable instrument, jurisdiction-specific legal opinion, disclosure package and separation from PRU / RAP analysis.* | *Land-pathway under review only. No land entitlement from PRU, RAP, token or dashboard status.* |
| *Public procurement contract or public-program pathway* | *Procurement law, public finance, eligibility rules, state-aid / subsidy rules where applicable, audit rules.* | *Procurement eligibility or public recognition may be overstated from evidence or market interest.* | *Procurement counsel review, eligibility analysis, public-authority pathway, auditability, MRV alignment, Anchor Demand File and Claims Register status.* | *Public-program thesis only. No procurement-ready or public-recognised claim.* |
| *Insurance-linked structure* | *Insurance regulation, reinsurance review, actuarial / model validation, Solvency-type analysis where applicable.* | *Risk-compression claims may be used without actuarial evidence or insurer recognition.* | *Insurer / reinsurer review, hazard model, loss / resilience evidence, model validation, legal review, disclosure package and Claims Register status.* | *Insurance-relevance thesis only. No insurance-recognised or underwriting-ready claim.* |
| *Bank, credit or project-finance pathway* | *Banking regulation, credit underwriting, collateral law, tax, security interests, disclosure.* | *PRU / RAP may be misread as collateral, secured exposure, credit enhancement or guaranteed risk reduction.* | *Bank review, credit memo, collateral analysis, legal opinion, financial model, evidence package, risk disclosures and Claims Register support.* | *Bank-reviewable only. No bankability, collateral or credit-enhancement claim.* |
| *Carbon, biodiversity, nature-credit or certificate pathway* | *Carbon / biodiversity standards, environmental registry rules, anti-greenwashing, no-double-counting controls, legal review.* | *Prometheus evidence may be mistaken for a credit, offset, certificate, registry unit or transferable environmental asset.* | *External standard eligibility, registry review, legal opinion, crediting methodology, additionality / permanence / leakage review where applicable, no-double-counting controls and Claims Register status.* | *Evidence-supporting pathway only. No credit, offset, certificate or transferable-unit claim.* |
| *Data-room access for investors or counterparties* | *Securities disclosure, confidentiality, data protection, due diligence, anti-greenwashing.* | *Data-room completeness may be misread as market validation, legal admission, certification or investment readiness.* | *Access policy, confidentiality terms, evidence index, role-based access, legal / MRV / integrity / runtime / market layers, version control and Claims Register links.* | *Diligence environment only. Data room does not create demand, rights, liquidity or regulatory approval.* |
| *Dashboard used for external reporting* | *Software governance, anti-greenwashing, data protection, securities disclosure where investor-facing, runtime review.* | *Dashboard state may exceed evidence status, legal status, market status or Claims Register status.* | *Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package, dashboard claim-status control, Claims Register alignment, data-room link, legal / market review where applicable and Runtime Release Decision Record.* | *Dashboard prototype or internal reporting only. No dashboard-certified, PRU-certified or RAP-certified claim.* |
| *API used for external reporting or integration* | *Software governance, cybersecurity, data protection, market conduct, securities disclosure where investor-facing.* | *API output may be treated as legal, market, evidentiary or financial authority.* | *Runtime package, API scope definition, access-control review, logging, security review, Claims Register alignment, data-room reference and release decision record.* | *API under review only. No API-certified or externally relied-upon claim.* |
| *Runtime release, zome, schema, AI/offline module, bridge connector, registry connector, data pipeline or VPS deployment* | *Cybersecurity, operational resilience, data protection, AI governance, custody / settlement review where applicable.* | *Code existence, source commit, build success or local test may be overstated as production readiness or institutional deployment.* | *Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package, source commit hash, build artefact hash, test-suite reference, security review where applicable, rollback, incident response, monitoring, Runtime Release Decision Record and Claims Register alignment.* | *Design specification, prototype, sandbox, internal test build or release candidate only. No production-admitted or externally relied-upon claim.* |
| *Bridge, registry-sync, custody or settlement pathway* | *Crypto / custody regulation, settlement rules, registry rules, cybersecurity, AML / KYC, legal transferability.* | *Double-counting, double-representation, replay, invalid transferability, custody failure or settlement overclaiming.* | *Event identity tuple, representation tuple, No-Double-Spending Threat Model, security review, custody / settlement review, legal review where applicable, quarantine pathway, adverse integrity record and Claims Register update.* | *Interoperability thesis only. No bridge-ready, custody-ready, settlement-ready or transferable claim.* |
| *AI-assisted MRV, analysis or disclosure pathway* | *AI governance, EU AI Act-type risk analysis, data protection, MRV assurance, auditability.* | *AI outputs may be mistaken for evidence, verification, legal review, market validation or governance authority.* | *AI role classification, human oversight, data governance, audit trail, model-output boundary, evidence-source separation, reviewer trail and Claims Register alignment.* | *Assistive analysis only. No AI-verified, AI-certified or autonomous-authority claim.* |
| *Research publication, pilot report or scientific claim* | *Research ethics, scientific integrity, data protection, publication standards, anti-greenwashing.* | *Pilot findings may be overstated as general proof, investment validation or market validation.* | *Pre-registration, baseline, comparator, measurement protocol, confidence range, reviewer trail, limitations, Claims Register update and separation from investor-facing claims.* | *Research context only. No generalized performance, PRU uplift or investment-readiness claim.* |

> **Instrument-risk interpretation rule: no instrument type may inherit regulatory status from another instrument type. The lowest unresolved legal, regulatory, evidence, market, integrity, runtime, custody, settlement, data-protection, tax, disclosure or governance gate controls the permitted external claim.**

**AA.4 Regulatory Disclosure Requirements**

Any Prometheus-derived material that is external, partner-facing, investor-facing, procurement-facing, insurance-facing, token-facing, dashboard-facing, API-facing, registry-facing, custody-facing, settlement-facing or public shall disclose its regulatory boundary.

Disclosure is not decoration. Disclosure is a gate-control mechanism. Its purpose is to prevent architecture, methodology, evidence, PRU analysis, RAP modelling, data-room completeness, legal structuring, market engagement, token representation, runtime output or dashboard display from being translated into a stronger claim than the relevant gate permits.

| ***Disclosure area*** | ***Recommended content*** | ***Relevant framework / controlling boundary*** |
|----|----|----|
| *Regulatory status* | *Statement that Prometheus is not, by itself, a regulated fund, security, securitisation vehicle, tokenised instrument, public offering, regulated market, rating agency, credit product, insurance product or certified environmental-credit issuer.* | *Securities law, fund regulation, MiCA / crypto-asset regulation, market conduct, anti-greenwashing.* |
| *Non-offering status* | *Clear statement that the material is architectural, methodological, research, pilot, diligence, technical or informational unless a legally reviewed offering document states otherwise.* | *Securities disclosure, investor-protection rules, marketing restrictions.* |
| *Jurisdictional limits* | *Identification of applicable jurisdiction, governing law assumptions, unresolved jurisdictional questions and jurisdictions where the pathway is not admitted.* | *Legal Opinion Package, cross-border offering rules, regulatory perimeter analysis.* |
| *PRU methodology* | *Statement that PRU is an analytical value-analysis interface, not a security, land right, cash-flow right, governance right, token right, legal entitlement, credit, offset, certificate, rating or regulated valuation by itself.* | *PRU boundary, valuation gate, anti-greenwashing, accounting / rating boundary.* |
| *RAP methodology* | *Statement that RAP is a portfolio-readiness and aggregation structure, not a fund, SPV, note, security, pooled investment product, market-active instrument or liquidity pathway by itself.* | *RAP-M ladder, fund regulation, securities law, market gate.* |
| *PRU-R / RAP-M status* | *Current PRU-R or RAP-M classification, date, reviewer status, evidence scope, data-room reference and Claims Register link.* | *PRU maturity gate, RAP market-readiness gate, Claims Register.* |
| *Claims Register status* | *Claim ID, claim type, claim status, admissibility class, permitted language, prohibited language and lowest unresolved gate.* | *Claims Register, disclosure control, governance review.* |
| *MRV evidence summary* | *Evidence source, method hash, data hash, timestamp, location, reviewer trail, missing-data notes, confidence model, uncertainty range and admissibility status.* | *Evidence gate, MRV assurance, anti-greenwashing, taxonomy / sustainability reporting where applicable.* |
| *Pilot evidence status* | *Whether the claim is hypothesis-level, pilot-required, pilot-observed, pilot-supported, reviewer-admitted or not yet admissible.* | *Gate 1 Pilot Evidence Boundary, Appendix T, Appendix V, Claims Register.* |
| *Valuation methodology* | *Bounded PRU function, U_max rationale, Conf_total, normalized indicators, sensitivity analysis, reproducibility package and uncertainty disclosure.* | *Valuation gate, accounting boundary, investor-disclosure boundary.* |
| *Risk factors* | *Ecological, MRV, operational, governance, legal, regulatory, market, liquidity, token, custody, settlement, interoperability, no-double-counting, AI, runtime, cybersecurity, data-protection, tax and execution risks.* | *Securities disclosure, prudential / insurance / procurement diligence, operational-risk review.* |
| *Legal wrapper* | *Entity, governing law, rights created, rights not created, instrument terms, investor eligibility, transfer restrictions, redemption limits, dispute forum and legal-opinion status.* | *Legal gate, securities / fund / contract / land / revenue-rights review.* |
| *Rights boundary* | *Explicit statement that PRU, RAP, TRBK, HoloFuel, dashboard status, API output, data-room record, governance participation or access credential does not create enforceable rights by itself.* | *Rights-Creation Boundary, Legal Opinion Package.* |
| *Market-readiness status* | *RAP-M status, Anchor Demand File status, ADE classification, counterparty qualification, binding / non-binding status, price-discovery memo, liquidity and exit limitations.* | *Gate 3, Anchor Demand File, market gate, market-claims language control.* |
| *Anchor Demand File disclosure* | *Whether external demand evidence is absent, non-binding, under review, legally wrapped, binding, closed or operationally effective.* | *Anchor Demand File, RAP-M5 / RAP-M6 / RAP-M7, market validation.* |
| *Liquidity and exit* | *Hold period, exit route, refinancing pathway, secondary-market limitation, redemption limitation, transfer limitation and absence of guaranteed liquidity.* | *Market gate, securities disclosure, investor-protection review.* |
| *Custody and settlement* | *Custody provider status, settlement pathway, transfer-agent pathway, registry status, custody risks, settlement risks and whether custody / settlement is admitted or pending.* | *Custody review, settlement review, legal gate, integrity gate.* |
| *Token-facing pathway* | *Token classification status, token rights, rights not attached, transferability, issuer status, custody, settlement, AML / KYC, legal review and prohibited token language.* | *MiCA / crypto-asset regulation, securities law, AML / KYC, custody / settlement, no-double-counting.* |
| *No-double-counting controls* | *Event identity tuple, evidence lineage, representation tuple where applicable, No-Double-Spending Threat Model, duplicate-claim detection, quarantine process, adverse integrity record and Claims Register update.* | *Gate 4, integrity gate, registry integrity, anti-greenwashing.* |
| *Registry / bridge / oracle disclosure* | *Registry synchronization status, bridge status, oracle dependency, finality assumptions, replay protection, external-settlement status and prohibited interoperability language.* | *Interoperability integrity, cybersecurity, custody / settlement, no-double-spending controls.* |
| *Data-room status* | *Data-room layer coverage, evidence index, version control, access policy, confidentiality terms, data-room maturity, missing artefacts and Claims Register links.* | *Due-diligence boundary, data-room governance, investor-access controls.* |
| *Dashboard disclosure* | *Dashboard purpose, claim-status source, Claims Register alignment, runtime status, permitted labels, prohibited labels and statement that dashboard output is not claim authority.* | *Runtime-support rule, Claims Register, anti-greenwashing, securities disclosure where investor-facing.* |
| *API disclosure* | *API scope, permitted external use, rate / access controls, audit trail, Claims Register alignment, runtime status and statement that API response is not legal, market, evidentiary or financial authority.* | *Runtime-support rule, cybersecurity, data protection, market conduct.* |
| *Runtime release disclosure* | *Runtime state, component ID, source commit hash, release version, build artefact hash, test-suite reference, security review where applicable, rollback, incident response, monitoring, Runtime Release Decision Record and expiry / review date.* | *Gate 5, Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package, Runtime Release Decision Record.* |
| *AI / offline module disclosure* | *AI role, human oversight, model-output boundary, data-governance controls, hallucination / inference risk, offline evidence continuity, sync conflict handling and statement that AI output is assistive only.* | *AI governance, data protection, MRV assurance, runtime-support rule.* |
| *Data protection* | *Data categories, personal-data exposure, lawful basis, consent where applicable, minimization, retention, access control, human-state research exclusion and data-room segregation.* | *GDPR / data-protection review, research ethics, access governance.* |
| *Use of proceeds* | *Eligible expenditures, excluded expenditures, evidence linkage, monitoring obligations, reporting cadence and absence of taxonomy / green-bond claim unless independently reviewed.* | *EU Taxonomy-type analysis, green bond frameworks, public-finance controls.* |
| *Investor eligibility* | *Eligible investor type, jurisdictional restrictions, suitability assumptions, minimum qualifications, transfer restrictions and onboarding controls.* | *Securities law, fund regulation, AML / KYC, investor-protection rules.* |
| *Conflicts of interest* | *Founder, operator, vendor, land, family, advisory, token, treasury, custodian, data-room, bridge, registry, dashboard, AI, capital-raising and reviewer conflicts.* | *Governance transparency, conflicts register, investor-protection review.* |
| *Incident / adverse record* | *Known incidents, adverse integrity records, data-quality issues, runtime incidents, security findings, claim corrections, quarantined records and remediation status.* | *Integrity gate, runtime release governance, data-room governance.* |
| *Ongoing monitoring* | *Reporting cadence, evidence refresh, Claims Register update cycle, legal-review refresh, market-review refresh, runtime-review refresh and downgrade / revocation triggers.* | *Governance review, release governance, investor reporting, public-claim control.* |

> **Disclosure escalation rule: internal research requires claim status; partner-facing material requires evidence status; counterparty-facing material requires data-room status; investor-facing material requires legal review; market-facing material requires Anchor Demand File status; token-facing material requires legal, integrity, custody and settlement review; dashboard/API-facing material requires Claims Register and runtime status.**
>
> **Final disclosure rule: no Claim ID, no public claim; no evidence status, no verified claim; no legal review, no rights claim; no Anchor Demand File, no market-readiness claim; no no-double-counting controls, no external representation claim; no Runtime Release Decision Record, no runtime-readiness claim; no governance decision, no capital-facing claim.**

**AA.5 Regulatory Engagement Recommendations**

Regulatory engagement is a staged diligence process. It is not regulatory approval, legal admission, compliance certification, market validation, investor protection or tax certainty unless a competent authority has issued a binding determination within the relevant jurisdiction and scope.

Prometheus regulatory engagement should be structured around the pathway being reviewed, not around the general architecture. A general discussion about Prometheus does not classify PRU; a general discussion about PRU does not classify RAP; a general discussion about software does not approve dashboards, APIs, zomes, runtime modules, AI/offline modules, registry connectors, bridge connectors or VPS deployments for external reliance.

| ***Engagement counterparty*** | ***Trigger*** | ***Purpose*** | ***Required preparation before engagement*** | ***Acceptable output*** | ***Claim boundary*** |
|----|----|----|----|----|----|
| *Jurisdiction-specific legal counsel* | *Any external legal, financial, token-facing, market-facing, procurement-facing, insurance-facing, investor-facing or rights-bearing pathway.* | *Determine whether the pathway remains architectural / analytical or creates enforceable rights, investor exposure, securities, fund, note, SPV, revenue, land-related, governance, access or token implications.* | *Pathway memo, rights map, proposed language, instrument draft if any, Claims Register status, data-room index and risk disclosure.* | *Legal Opinion Package, legal issue map, red-flag memo, structuring memo or no-go recommendation.* | *Legal counsel review is not regulatory approval. No enforceable-rights claim without valid legal instrument.* |
| *Securities / fund / financial-market regulator or competent authority* | *Fund, SPV, note, pooled capital, managed portfolio, revenue exposure, investor-facing subscription, transferable rights or public / private offering pathway.* | *Clarify whether registration, exemption, filing, authorization, marketing restriction, investor eligibility or regulated-service-provider involvement is required.* | *Legal Opinion Package, term sheet, disclosure package, investor category, marketing plan, fund / SPV / note structure, risk factors and Claims Register status.* | *Regulatory feedback, filing pathway, exemption analysis, no-action or informal guidance where available, or requirement for registration / authorization.* | *No material may imply regulator approval, exemption or authorization unless formally obtained and documented.* |
| *Crypto-asset / token / custody / settlement counsel or authority* | *Token-facing PRU, token-facing RAP, TRBK pathway, HoloFuel-related pathway, custody record, settlement record, transfer pathway, bridge connector or registry synchronization.* | *Determine crypto-asset, securities, payment, e-money, custody, transfer, AML / KYC, settlement and issuer implications.* | *Token function memo, rights map, transferability analysis, custody / settlement pathway, technical architecture, no-double-counting controls, disclosure package and Claims Register status.* | *Token classification memo, custody / settlement issue map, AML / KYC pathway, transfer restriction recommendation or no-go recommendation.* | *No token-ready, transferable, custody-ready, settlement-ready or externally redeemable claim without legal, integrity, custody and settlement review.* |
| *Environmental claims / anti-greenwashing counsel or authority* | *Public regenerative, biodiversity, soil, water, carbon, resilience, credit-like, offset-like, certification-like or impact claims.* | *Determine whether public environmental language is evidence-bounded, non-misleading and compliant with applicable green-claims rules.* | *MRV evidence package, method hash, data hash, confidence range, limitations, Claims Register entry, no-double-counting controls and public-claims draft.* | *Claims language review, required disclaimers, evidence threshold memo, correction requirements or prohibition of proposed language.* | *No environmental claim may exceed evidence scope, confidence range, reviewer trail, missing-data notes or Claims Register status.* |
| *Tax counsel or tax authority where appropriate* | *Revenue pathway, token pathway, land-related structure, public incentive, subsidy, credit, grant, conservation mechanism, SPV, fund, note or cross-border structure.* | *Clarify tax classification, reporting obligations, incentive eligibility, withholding, VAT / sales tax where applicable, revenue recognition and entity-level treatment.* | *Legal structure, flow of funds, rights map, revenue model, incentive assumptions, jurisdictional facts, accounting assumptions and disclosure package.* | *Tax memo, ruling request, authority response where available, filing recommendation or no-go recommendation.* | *No tax benefit, subsidy, credit, grant, incentive or tax-treatment claim without tax review and applicable evidence.* |
| *Accounting / audit / assurance provider* | *PRU reporting, RAP reporting, use-of-proceeds reporting, asset / revenue recognition, fair-value discussion, sustainability reporting or external assurance.* | *Determine accounting, assurance, auditability, evidence sufficiency and reporting limitations.* | *Methodology package, PRU function, U_max rationale, Conf_total, evidence package, data-room index, Claims Register entries and financial model.* | *Audit / assurance scope, accounting memo, limitation statement, evidence-gap report or assurance opinion where available.* | *No accounting, fair-value, revenue-recognition, audit-ready or assurance claim without scoped review.* |
| *Data-protection counsel, DPO or authority* | *Participant data, household data, human observations, access logs, geolocation, health-related context, AI/offline modules, dashboards, APIs or data-room access.* | *Determine lawful basis, consent needs, data minimization, retention, access controls, cross-border transfers, data-room segregation and human-state research boundaries.* | *Data map, processing-purpose memo, data categories, access roles, retention schedule, consent materials, DPIA where applicable and security controls.* | *Data-protection memo, DPIA, consent pathway, access-control requirements, retention limits or remediation plan.* | *No GDPR-compliant, privacy-compliant or data-protection-ready claim without scoped review.* |
| *AI governance counsel, AI risk reviewer or competent authority* | *AI-assisted MRV, AI/offline modules, automated classification, investor-facing AI outputs, dashboard inference, decision support or external assistant integration.* | *Determine AI role classification, risk category, human oversight, model-output boundary, data governance, audit trail and prohibited automated authority.* | *AI system description, model-output boundary, data sources, human oversight plan, audit trail, risk assessment, runtime release record and Claims Register links.* | *AI governance memo, risk classification, human-oversight requirements, model-risk controls or prohibition of external use.* | *AI output remains assistive. No AI-verified, AI-certified, autonomous-authority or compliance-status claim without review.* |
| *Public procurement counsel or authority* | *Procurement pathway, public-program eligibility, grant pathway, municipality / agency engagement, resilience programme, public-private partnership or public funding.* | *Determine eligibility, procurement route, audit obligations, state-aid / subsidy constraints where applicable, authority mandate and public-claim boundary.* | *Procurement memo, eligibility criteria, MRV evidence, public-benefit rationale, budget assumptions, Anchor Demand File where applicable and disclosure package.* | *Eligibility memo, procurement pathway, grant / programme requirements, public-authority feedback or no-go recommendation.* | *No procurement-ready, public-program-recognised, grant-eligible or public-approved claim without documented authority pathway.* |
| *Insurance / reinsurance reviewer, actuary or regulated insurance counterparty* | *Risk-compression claim, resilience claim, parametric pathway, underwriting pathway, insurance recognition or risk-transfer structure.* | *Determine whether evidence, hazard model, loss distribution, resilience indicators and counterparty requirements support insurance review.* | *Hazard model, loss / resilience evidence, pilot data where required, MRV package, claims boundary, Anchor Demand File where applicable and risk disclosures.* | *Insurer / reinsurer feedback, actuarial issue map, model-validation requirements, insurance-pathway memo or no-go recommendation.* | *No insurance-recognised, underwriting-ready, risk-compression-admitted or risk-transfer claim without counterparty and model review.* |
| *Bank, credit, project-finance or prudential counterparty* | *Bankability, project-finance, credit underwriting, collateral discussion, refinancing path, credit enhancement or institutional debt pathway.* | *Determine whether PRU / RAP evidence can support diligence, risk review, collateral analysis, credit memo or financing conditions.* | *Financial model, data room, legal wrapper, evidence package, market / revenue assumptions, risk factors, collateral memo and Claims Register status.* | *Bank feedback, credit issue map, diligence request list, term-sheet pathway or no-go recommendation.* | *No bankability, collateral, credit-enhancement, financing-ready or prudential-recognition claim without counterparty review.* |
| *Environmental registry / credit-standard operator* | *Carbon, biodiversity, nature-credit, certificate, offset, public registry or transferable environmental-asset pathway.* | *Determine eligibility, methodology compatibility, registry requirements, additionality / permanence / leakage requirements where applicable and no-double-counting constraints.* | *MRV package, project boundary, baseline, evidence lineage, legal rights to claim, no-double-counting controls, registry mapping and Claims Register status.* | *Registry eligibility feedback, methodology gap analysis, listing pathway, rejection or remediation plan.* | *No credit, offset, certificate, registry-listed, biodiversity-unit, carbon-unit or transferable environmental-asset claim without external standard and registry review.* |
| *Cybersecurity / operational-resilience reviewer* | *Runtime release, dashboard, API, zome, schema, bridge connector, registry connector, data pipeline, VPS deployment, custody / settlement connector or key-management pathway.* | *Determine security posture, operational resilience, access control, key management, logging, rollback, incident response and release-readiness.* | *Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package, source commit hash, build artefact hash, test-suite results, dependency review, access-control review, key-management review and incident-response plan.* | *Security review, unresolved findings, remediation plan, release recommendation, downgrade requirement or no-go decision.* | *No runtime-verified, security-reviewed, production-admitted or externally relied-upon claim without scoped review and Runtime Release Decision Record.* |
| *Independent methodology / MRV reviewer* | *New PRU method, indicator, normalization rule, Conf_total adjustment, evidence source, pilot result, Claims Register upgrade or public MRV claim.* | *Determine methodological validity, evidence sufficiency, confidence treatment, reproducibility and admissibility.* | *Methodology package, data package, reproducibility package, reviewer trail, missing-data notes, Claims Register entries and pilot protocol where applicable.* | *Methodology review, evidence-gap report, admissibility recommendation, downgrade / upgrade decision or rejection.* | *No verified, admissible, PRU-relevant, RAP-relevant or investor-facing MRV claim without review.* |
| *Governance body / Independent Methodology Committee* | *Any gate progression, Claims Register update, canon update, runtime release, RAP-M upgrade, market-facing language or capital-facing material.* | *Determine whether all required evidence, legal, market, integrity, runtime and disclosure conditions support the proposed status.* | *Data-room evidence, legal opinion where applicable, Anchor Demand File where applicable, Runtime Release Decision Record where applicable, conflict disclosures and proposed permitted / prohibited language.* | *Green, Yellow, Red or Parked decision record.* | *Governance cannot override missing evidence, legal review, market validation, no-double-counting controls or runtime release requirements.* |

> **Engagement rule: no pathway memo, no engagement; no Legal Opinion Package, no rights-bearing claim; no Anchor Demand File, no market-facing claim; no no-double-counting controls, no external representation claim; no Runtime Release Decision Record, no runtime-readiness claim; no disclosure update, no external circulation; no governance decision, no status upgrade.**

**AA.6 Limitations and Caveats**

This Regulatory Alignment Matrix is an orientation tool. It is not a legal opinion, regulatory advice, tax advice, accounting advice, financial advice, investment advice, offering document, compliance certification, regulatory approval, securities classification, fund classification, token classification, custody approval, settlement approval, procurement qualification, insurance recognition, market-validation document or production-readiness assessment.

The regulatory treatment of any Prometheus-derived pathway depends on the actual facts, jurisdiction, instrument structure, rights created, rights not created, transferability, counterparty type, investor type, marketing language, revenue exposure, custody pathway, settlement pathway, data-processing context, AI use, runtime reliance, ecological-claim scope and external-use context.

The matrix is based on the current architecture and current regulatory understanding. Regulations, regulatory interpretation and supervisory practice evolve. Token, custody, settlement, AI, nature-finance, green-claims, sustainability-disclosure and data-protection frameworks may evolve materially.

> **No Prometheus material may rely on this matrix as a substitute for jurisdiction-specific professional review.**

PRU is not a regulated valuation, rating, credit assessment, security, land right, cash-flow right, governance right, token right, certificate, credit, offset or legal entitlement by itself.

RAP is not a fund, SPV, note, pooled investment product, securitisation vehicle, market-active instrument or liquidity pathway by itself.

TRBK does not create investor entitlement by itself.

HoloFuel does not create PRU value, investor exposure, securities status, market readiness or settlement readiness by itself.

A dashboard does not create legal status, market status, evidentiary status, PRU certification, RAP certification or investor protection.

An API response does not create legal, regulatory, market, evidentiary, financial, custody, settlement or tax status.

AI output does not create ecological truth, MRV admissibility, legal review, market validation, regulatory classification, investor entitlement or governance authority.

Runtime execution does not create production admission, software correctness, security assurance, operational resilience, external reliance or institutional deployment.

A data room supports diligence; it does not create legal admission, regulatory approval, market demand, liquidity, certification, custody readiness, settlement readiness or production readiness.

A legal wrapper may create rights only within the instrument, jurisdiction, disclosure package and legal opinion that support it; it does not create market demand or liquidity by itself.

A counterparty conversation does not create offtake, financing, insurance recognition, procurement readiness, bankability, binding demand or market-active status.

A token representation does not create enforceable rights, transferability, redemption, custody readiness, settlement readiness, investor protection or asset backing by itself.

A bridge, registry entry, oracle input, custody record or settlement record does not prove ecological uniqueness, legal admissibility, transferability, no-double-counting or market readiness by itself.

Regulatory engagement does not create regulatory approval unless a competent authority has issued an express determination within the relevant jurisdiction and scope.

Disclosure does not cure missing legal structure, missing evidence, missing market validation, missing integrity controls, missing runtime admission or missing governance decision.

> **Operative caveat: architecture is not approval; methodology is not compliance; evidence is not certification; PRU is not a right; RAP is not a fund; data room is not demand; legal wrapper is not liquidity; counterparty review is not binding demand; token representation is not enforceable right; dashboard output is not authority; API output is not legal status; AI output is not verification; runtime execution is not production admission; regulatory mapping is not regulatory approval.**

**AA.6A Legal Opinion and Review Dependency Rule**

Any Prometheus-derived pathway that moves from architecture, methodology, research, internal analysis, pilot design or operational coordination into external legal, financial, token-facing, market-facing, procurement-facing, insurance-facing, public-program, investor-facing, dashboard-facing, API-facing, registry-facing, custody-facing, settlement-facing, runtime-reliant or rights-bearing use shall require the applicable professional review package before external reliance.

The review package shall be pathway-specific, jurisdiction-specific where legal, regulatory, tax, procurement, insurance, custody, settlement, token, data-protection, AI or investor-facing issues arise, instrument-specific where rights or obligations may arise, and component-specific where dashboard, API, runtime, bridge connector, registry connector, AI/offline module, data pipeline, VPS deployment or software release is externally relied upon.

The Legal Opinion Package shall determine, where applicable:

whether the pathway remains architectural, analytical, methodological, operational, research-only or internal;

whether the pathway creates enforceable rights;

whether the pathway creates investor exposure;

whether the pathway creates securities, fund, note, SPV, membership, revenue, land-related, governance, access, token-related, custody-related or settlement-related implications;

whether marketing, solicitation, distribution, investor-eligibility or transfer rules apply;

whether disclosure, transfer restrictions, redemption restrictions, holding-period assumptions or liquidity limitations are required;

whether custody, settlement, transfer-agent, registry, bridge, oracle, AML / KYC, sanctions, tax, accounting, data-protection, AI, consumer-protection or procurement rules apply;

whether regulatory engagement, filing, registration, exemption, authorization or approval is required;

whether the Claims Register status must be changed;

whether the proposed external language is permitted.

The Market Review Package shall determine whether an Anchor Demand File exists, whether ADE classification is complete, whether counterparty qualification has been documented, whether demand evidence is absent, informal, non-binding, under review, legally wrapped, binding, closed or operationally effective, and whether RAP-M5, RAP-M6 or RAP-M7 language is supported.

The Integrity Review Package shall determine whether the no-double-counting invariant applies, whether event identity and representation tuples exist where applicable, whether the No-Double-Spending Threat Model exists, whether duplicate-claim detection has been defined, whether bridge/oracle/finality/replay/registry/custody/settlement risks have been reviewed, and whether the Claims Register supports the proposed representation.

The Runtime Review Package shall determine whether a Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package exists, whether repository reference, source commit hash, release version and build artefact hash are recorded, whether tests, security review, rollback, incident response, monitoring, governance approval and Claims Register alignment exist, and whether the proposed runtime language is permitted.

The Data-Protection and AI Review Package shall determine whether personal data, household data, participant data, geolocation data, access logs, human observations, health-related context or behavioral data are processed; whether lawful basis, consent, minimization, retention, access control and segregation requirements are satisfied; and whether AI output boundaries, model risk and human oversight are documented.

> **Dependency rule: no Legal Opinion Package, no rights-bearing claim; no Anchor Demand File, no market-facing claim; no Integrity Review Package, no external representation claim; no custody review, no custody-ready claim; no settlement review, no settlement-ready claim; no Runtime Release Decision Record, no production-readiness claim; no data-protection review where applicable, no privacy-compliance claim; no AI review where applicable, no AI-compliance claim; no Claims Register support, no external claim.**
>
> **The lowest unresolved legal, regulatory, evidence, market, integrity, runtime, custody, settlement, data-protection, AI, tax, disclosure or governance gate controls the permitted claim.**

**AA.7 Final Regulatory Principle**

Prometheus provides evidence, methodology, governance, data-room, integrity and runtime-readiness infrastructure for verified regeneration. It does not create regulatory status, legal rights, investor protection, market demand, liquidity, custody readiness, settlement readiness, token readiness, procurement eligibility, insurance recognition, AI compliance, data-protection compliance or production admission by itself.

Regulatory treatment of any Prometheus-derived pathway depends on the specific legal structure, jurisdiction, instrument, counterparty, investor profile, rights created, rights not created, transferability, custody pathway, settlement pathway, marketing language, revenue exposure, data-processing context, AI use, runtime reliance, ecological-claim scope and intended external use.

Prometheus supports regulatory alignment through evidence-before-value discipline, bounded PRU methodology, Claims Register control, data-room governance, rights-separation discipline, Legal Opinion Package dependency, Anchor Demand File dependency, no-double-counting and interoperability controls, Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package dependency, disclosure discipline, conflict-of-interest governance, professional-review dependency and lowest-unresolved-gate control.

> **Final regulatory boundary: no jurisdiction-specific legal review, no legal-status claim; no valid legal instrument, no enforceable-rights claim; no Claims Register support, no public regulatory claim; no Anchor Demand File, no market-readiness claim; no no-double-counting controls, no external representation claim; no Runtime Release Decision Record, no production-readiness claim; no disclosure package, no investor-facing claim; no governance decision, no status upgrade.**

Prometheus may be described as regulatory-alignment infrastructure only when the statement is clearly bounded as architectural, methodological, evidentiary or diligence-supporting.

Prometheus may not be described as approved, compliant, regulated, exempt, authorised, registered, taxonomy-aligned, SFDR-ready, MiCA-compliant, securities-compliant, fund-compliant, procurement-eligible, insurance-recognised, custody-ready, settlement-ready, token-ready, market-ready, investor-ready, dashboard-certified, API-certified, runtime-verified or production-admitted unless the corresponding professional review package, data-room evidence, Claims Register status and governance decision support that classification for the relevant jurisdiction, instrument, component, counterparty and intended use.

No positive classification may be generalized beyond the scope in which it was reviewed. No informal feedback may be treated as approval. No non-binding feedback may be treated as authorization. No jurisdiction-specific review may be generalized across jurisdictions. No instrument-specific review may be generalized across instruments. No counterparty-specific demand may be generalized across counterparties. No component-specific runtime release may be generalized across components. No claim-specific Claims Register status may be generalized across claims.

> **Final rule of Appendix AA: regulatory mapping is not regulatory approval; legal structuring is not market validation; market validation is not liquidity; runtime release is not legal authority; token representation is not enforceable right; custody record is not transferability; settlement pathway is not market readiness; dashboard output is not certification; API output is not legal status; AI output is not verification; data-room completeness is not investability; capital-readability is not capital deployment.**
>
> **The lowest unresolved evidence, valuation, integrity, legal, market, runtime, custody, settlement, token, AI, data-protection, tax, disclosure or governance gate controls the permitted regulatory claim.**

**Appendix BB — Investor FAQ: Addressing the Critical Gaps**

**Purpose**

This appendix provides a concise, direct and institutionally disciplined FAQ for capital allocators, family offices, fund managers, public-sector partners, insurance or reinsurance partners, procurement officers, legal counsel, technical reviewers and institutional counterparties.

It is an orientation tool. It is not an offering document, investment advice, legal advice, financial advice or regulatory advice. It does not create investor rights, market demand, liquidity, regulatory approval or production readiness.

All answers are governed by the source-layer discipline of the Prometheus White Paper, the Claims Register, the data room, the Legal Opinion Package, the Anchor Demand File, the no-double-counting and interoperability controls, the Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package and the lowest unresolved gate.

> **FAQ interpretation: evidence before value; integrity before representation; legal review before rights; anchor demand before market-facing language; runtime verification before external software reliance; governance before capital-facing use; lowest unresolved gate before every claim.**

**BB.1 Empirical Validation**

**Q1: Is there validated empirical evidence of cooperative syntropic surplus, H5, or Prometheus-specific regenerative asset performance?**

A: No. At the time of this release, Prometheus is a pre-pilot constitutional and architectural framework.

Claims concerning cooperative syntropic surplus, syntropic productivity, risk compression, time-positive value, regenerative asset performance, portfolio-grade regenerative infrastructure and H1-H5 effects remain hypothesis-level unless and until supported by pilot-collected evidence.

The cooperative syntropic surplus claim, including any formulation equivalent to Y_system \> Sum(Y_isolated_components), is an empirical hypothesis. It may be studied, modelled and tested. It may not be presumed.

No H1-H5 claim may be used for PRU uplift, RAP formation, investor-facing material, insurance-facing material, procurement-facing material, token-facing language, dashboard certification, API-facing claims, capital-facing reporting or public financial representation unless it has passed the applicable pilot evidence gate, Claims Register update, governance review and legal review where applicable.

Gate 1 is closed at specification level only. It remains open at evidence level until pilot data have been collected, quality-checked, reviewed, linked to the Claims Register and admitted through the relevant governance and legal pathway.

**Q2: What evidence must exist before empirical claims can support PRU or RAP use?**

A: A claim must move through a controlled evidence sequence.

claim

pre-registration

baseline

comparator

measurement

evidence package

method hash

data hash

source

timestamp

location

missing-data notes

confidence range

reviewer trail

Claims Register update

governance review

admissibility decision

No baseline means no improvement claim. No comparator means no superiority claim. No confidence range means no institutional reliance. No reviewer trail means no admissibility. No Claims Register status means no public claim. No legal review where applicable means no capital-facing use.

External scientific literature may inform research design, indicator selection, comparator design, ecological rationale, operational-risk framing, training requirements and pilot protocols. It may not substitute for Prometheus pilot evidence.

**Q3: What happens if pilots fail or H1-H5 hypotheses are not supported?**

A: The corresponding claims must be narrowed, downgraded, parked or abandoned. Pilot failure is not a governance failure if it is recorded honestly. It is a falsifiability outcome.

Prometheus is designed to preserve credibility by allowing claims to fail without collapsing the architecture. If H1-H5 hypotheses are not supported, the relevant Claims Register entries must be updated, permitted language downgraded, PRU uplift removed or withheld where applicable, RAP eligibility restricted where applicable and investor-facing, insurance-facing, procurement-facing, token-facing, dashboard-facing, API-facing or public financial language corrected.

record the result

preserve the data

update the Claims Register

revise the claim

adjust the methodology if justified

disclose the limitation

avoid narrative rescue

No claim may be preserved through language if the evidence no longer supports it.

**Q4: What is the timeline to institutional investability?**

A: There is no automatic timeline. Prometheus may move toward institutional investability only through gate completion, not calendar passage.

A time estimate such as 3-5 years may be used only as a non-binding scenario for pilot evidence, legal structuring, market engagement, runtime implementation and governance maturation. It must not be presented as a forecast, guarantee, capital-readiness date, fund-launch date, token-launch date, liquidity date, regulatory-approval date or investor-entry date.

pilot evidence quality

baseline and comparator completeness

H1-H5 validation status

MRV reliability

Claims Register maturity

bounded PRU methodology

RAP-M progression

Anchor Demand File evidence

jurisdiction-specific legal review

custody and settlement pathway where applicable

no-double-counting and interoperability controls

Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package where software is externally relied upon

disclosure package

governance approval

counterparty demand

regulatory and professional review

The correct investor-facing answer is: Prometheus is not investable today as a regulated product, security, fund, tokenised instrument or public offering. It is a pre-pilot institutional architecture that may become capital-facing only if the relevant evidence, valuation, integrity, legal, market, runtime, disclosure and governance gates are satisfied.

**Q5: Can early participants rely on expected future validation?**

A: No. Early participants may engage with Prometheus as a research, pilot, governance, technical, philanthropic, procurement-exploration, ecological design, diligence or partnership pathway, depending on the relevant documents and legal structure.

They may not rely on future empirical validation as if it already existed. Forward-looking statements, scenario analyses, value envelopes, timelines and pilot expectations are conditional. They are not guarantees, forecasts, offers, evidence or investor protection.

The permitted language is: pilot hypothesis; research pathway; evidence-gated development; capital-readiness workstream; validation pending; Claims Register update required; legal review required before capital-facing use.

**BB.2 Legal and Regulatory**

**Q6: What legal protection do investors have today?**

A: None through Prometheus itself. At the time of this release, Prometheus is not a regulated investment product, not a fund, not a note, not a security, not a securitisation vehicle, not a tokenised instrument, not a public offering and not an asset-management product.

Investor rights do not arise from the White Paper, PRU analysis, RAP maturity, TRBK, HoloFuel, dashboard status, API output, data-room access, governance participation or runtime execution. Investor rights arise only through valid legal instruments that have been drafted, reviewed, admitted and disclosed within the relevant jurisdiction.

Until a jurisdiction-specific legal instrument exists, Prometheus-related materials must be treated as architectural, methodological, research, pilot, diligence, technical or informational only.

**Q7: What is the distinction between PRU and a security?**

A: PRU is an analytical value-analysis interface. It is not a security, fund interest, note, token, land right, revenue right, cash-flow entitlement, governance right, certificate, environmental credit, regulated valuation, rating, market price or investor protection by itself.

PRU may support analysis only when evidence, provenance, confidence scoring, bounded methodology, admissibility gates and Claims Register status support that analysis.

A security, fund interest, note, tokenised instrument, revenue right, land-related right or other regulated instrument may arise only if a separate legal structure creates rights, obligations, investor exposure, transferability, redemption, revenue participation, pooled exposure, managerial reliance or another legally relevant feature.

No material may imply that PRU is investable, transferable, redeemable, asset-backed, yield-bearing, ownership-bearing, security-like, token-ready or legally enforceable without a Legal Opinion Package and valid instrument.

**Q8: What is the distinction between RAP and a fund or SPV?**

A: RAP is a portfolio-readiness and aggregation structure. It is not a fund, SPV, note programme, securitisation vehicle, pooled investor exposure, liquidity or market demand by itself.

RAP-M4 means portfolio-readable. RAP-M5 means counterparty-reviewable. RAP-M6 means legally wrapped. RAP-M7 means market-active only within the relevant instrument, counterparty, jurisdiction, disclosure package, custody / settlement pathway where applicable, governance decision and Claims Register status.

No RAP may be described as investable, liquid, market-ready, secondary-market-ready, offtake-backed, insurance-recognised, procurement-ready or portfolio-deployable unless the Anchor Demand File, RAP-M Decision Record, legal review, disclosure package, data-room record and Claims Register status support that classification.

**Q9: Can I invest in Prometheus today?**

A: No. At the time of this release, there is no regulated investment vehicle, no fund, no security, no note, no securitisation vehicle, no tokenised instrument, no public offering, no market-active RAP structure, no secondary market and no admitted investor-facing product associated with Prometheus.

Permitted current language includes research participation, pilot participation, legally structured philanthropic or catalytic support, technical collaboration, non-investment governance participation where legally bounded, procurement exploration, insurance or banking review, public-program exploration, counterparty diligence and capital-readiness workstream.

Prohibited current language includes investment opportunity, public offering, security, fund interest, tokenised asset, guaranteed return, yield, redemption, liquidity, ownership, asset backing, investor protection, market-ready RAP, tradeable PRU, secondary market, custody-ready, settlement-ready, token-ready or production-admitted investment infrastructure.

**Q10: Could Prometheus later support an investment product?**

A: Potentially, but only through a separate legally admitted structure. A future investment product, fund, note, SPV, securitisation vehicle, tokenised instrument, revenue-sharing agreement, land-related instrument, public-private structure, procurement contract, insurance-linked structure or other capital-facing pathway would require, at minimum:

pilot evidence where empirical claims are relied upon

Claims Register support

bounded PRU methodology

RAP-M status where portfolio logic is used

Anchor Demand File where market-facing language is used

jurisdiction-specific Legal Opinion Package

rights mapping

disclosure package

investor eligibility rules

AML / KYC and sanctions review where applicable

tax and accounting review where applicable

custody and settlement review where applicable

no-double-counting and interoperability controls where external representation is used

Runtime Release Decision Record where software is externally relied upon

data-room completeness

governance approval

Even if these conditions are pursued, no future instrument is guaranteed. Prometheus may define the pathway. Only legal instruments may create enforceable rights.

**Q11: Are TRBK or HoloFuel investor assets?**

A: Not by themselves. TRBK, if used, is a governance, coordination, access or participation layer subject to its own legal and functional boundary. It does not create investor entitlement, PRU value, RAP maturity, ownership, revenue participation, redemption, liquidity, securities exposure or asset backing by itself.

HoloFuel, if used, is an operational transaction and infrastructure layer within agent-centric digital systems. It does not create PRU value, investor rights, settlement readiness, custody readiness, securities status or market readiness by itself.

Any economic, financial, token-facing, transferable, custody-facing, settlement-facing or investor-facing use of TRBK, HoloFuel or related digital representation requires legal classification, rights mapping, disclosure, custody / settlement review where applicable, no-double-counting controls where applicable, governance approval and Claims Register support.

**Q12: Do dashboards, APIs or runtime systems create legal status?**

A: No. Dashboard display does not create legal status. API output does not create legal status. Runtime execution does not create investor protection.

A zome, schema, dashboard, API endpoint, AI/offline module, bridge connector, registry connector, data pipeline, VPS deployment, repository release or software build does not become legally or institutionally admissible merely because it exists, compiles, runs locally or appears functional.

Dashboards and APIs may display or transmit only the status that the Claims Register, evidence record, legal review, market review, integrity review, runtime release record and governance decision support.

Any external reliance on runtime output requires a Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package, Runtime Release Decision Record, data-room record, governance approval and Claims Register alignment.

**Q13: What legal document is required before any rights-bearing or capital-facing pathway?**

A: A jurisdiction-specific Legal Opinion Package and a valid legal instrument are required.

The Legal Opinion Package must determine whether the pathway remains architectural, analytical, methodological, operational or research-only, or whether it creates enforceable rights, investor exposure, securities, fund, note, SPV, membership, revenue, land-related, governance, access, token-related, custody-related or settlement-related implications.

Where the Legal Opinion Package is absent, the pathway remains architectural, analytical, research-only, internally operational or non-capital-facing.

**Q14: What should an institutional reader do if they see investment language attached to Prometheus?**

A: Request the gate evidence. The reader should ask for Claim ID, Claims Register status, evidence package, PRU-R or RAP-M status where applicable, Anchor Demand File where market-facing language is used, Legal Opinion Package, valid legal instrument, disclosure package, investor eligibility rules, custody and settlement review where applicable, no-double-counting controls where external representation is used, Runtime Release Decision Record where software is externally relied upon, data-room record and governance decision.

If these materials are absent, incomplete, expired, contradicted or inconsistent, the claim should be treated as non-admitted.

> **Final legal FAQ rule: no legal opinion, no rights claim; no valid instrument, no investor entitlement; no Claims Register support, no public claim; no Anchor Demand File, no market-facing claim; no custody review, no custody-ready claim; no settlement review, no settlement-ready claim; no Runtime Release Decision Record, no runtime-readiness claim; no governance decision, no status upgrade.**

**BB.3 Financial Model and Returns**

**Q15: Does Prometheus currently generate investor returns?**

A: No. At the time of this release, Prometheus does not generate investor returns through any admitted investment product, fund, note, security, tokenised instrument, public offering, market-active RAP, secondary market or regulated capital vehicle.

Prometheus defines an architecture through which verified regeneration may become capital-readable. It does not itself create financeable assets, cash flows, yield, liquidity, investor entitlement or asset backing.

Any future investor return would have to arise from a separate legally structured and disclosed pathway, such as a productive cash-flow asset, contractual revenue source, legally admitted investment vehicle, project-finance structure, public-private structure, procurement contract, insurance-linked structure, credit pathway, land-related instrument or other valid legal instrument.

**Q16: What is the difference between PRU value and investor return?**

A: PRU value is analytical. Investor return is legal, financial and contractual.

PRU analysis may help interpret verified regenerative performance, confidence-adjusted ecological value, maturity, risk and capital-readability. It does not create return, yield, cash-flow entitlement, redemption, appreciation, collateral, securities exposure or investor protection.

Investor return can exist only if there is a legally reviewed source of return, such as productive revenue, contractual revenue, lease revenue, offtake revenue, procurement revenue, insurance-linked payment, project-finance repayment, fund distribution, note interest, equity appreciation or another enforceable financial pathway.

**Q17: Can PRU value be used as market price?**

A: No. PRU value analysis is not market price, fair value, appraised value, accounting value, collateral value, liquidation value, secondary-market price, token price, redemption value or investor NAV by itself.

A PRU-related value expression may be used only as a bounded analytical output when supported by admissible MRV evidence, method hash, data hash, confidence model, bounded PRU function, U_max rationale, Conf_total, normalized indicators, sensitivity analysis, reproducibility package, reviewer trail, Claims Register status, legal review where capital-facing use is proposed, and disclosure of uncertainty and limitations.

Market price requires actual market evidence, counterparty evidence, transaction evidence, legally valid instrument terms or other documented price-discovery support.

**Q18: What cash flows may be used in a base-case financial model?**

A: Only documented or reasonably supportable productive, operational or contractual cash flows may be used in a base-case model.

agricultural or food-forest production revenue

hospitality revenue

education or training revenue

consulting or operational service revenue

legally structured subscription or membership revenue

procurement revenue

public-program revenue

offtake revenue

lease or land-use revenue

insurance-related payments where legally admitted

project-finance or credit-related repayments where legally structured

other documented contractual revenue

No ecological performance value may be used as base-case cash flow unless it is linked to a documented productive, contractual, public-program, insurance, procurement, credit, legally admitted or otherwise enforceable revenue pathway.

**Q19: How should Layer 1, Layer 2 and Layer 3 value be treated?**

A: They must be separated. Layer 1 productive and operational value may be modelled where actual or reasonably supportable revenue, cost reduction, production, service income or operational cash flow exists.

Layer 2 contractual or institutional value may be modelled only where a contract, public-program pathway, offtake, procurement pathway, insurer pathway, bank pathway, credit pathway or legally reviewable counterparty pathway exists.

Layer 3 systemic co-benefit, ecological optionality, social benefit, resilience externality, health-adjacent benefit, biodiversity value, water-cycle benefit or territorial benefit remains non-admissible for financial use unless separately admitted through a valid legal, contractual, regulatory, market or public-program pathway.

Layer 3 may inform narrative, research, public-policy discussion, philanthropic rationale, strategic context or non-admissible scenario analysis. It may not be used as base-case investor return, guaranteed value, collateral, liquidity, asset backing, token backing or valuation uplift for investor-facing material unless the Claims Register, legal review, market validation and disclosure package support the specific use.

**Q20: Can Prometheus show financial projections?**

A: Yes, but only as bounded scenarios. Any financial projection must be clearly labelled as scenario, illustrative, conditional, non-binding, not a forecast, not a guarantee, not an offer, not investor advice, not return assurance and not liquidity assurance.

A Prometheus financial model shall disclose base-case revenue sources, contractual status, evidence source, assumptions, sensitivity analysis, downside case, stress case, capex, opex, maintenance reserve, operator cost, MRV cost, data-room cost, governance cost, legal and compliance cost, custody / settlement cost where applicable, runtime cost where applicable, tax assumptions, liquidity limitations, exit limitations, Claims Register constraints and unresolved gates.

No model may imply guaranteed return, guaranteed yield, guaranteed appreciation, guaranteed exit, guaranteed refinancing, guaranteed liquidity, guaranteed ecological improvement, guaranteed risk compression or guaranteed market demand.

**Q21: Can time-positive value be presented as financial upside?**

A: Only as hypothesis-level or scenario-level unless validated and admitted. Time-positive value means that regenerative performance may improve over time under defined ecological, operational and governance conditions. It does not mean guaranteed appreciation, compounding, risk reduction, cash flow or investor return.

Time-positive value may be discussed only with time horizon, ecological baseline, comparator, performance indicator, confidence range, hazard model, regression probability, sensitivity analysis, management assumptions, climate and disturbance assumptions, evidence status and Claims Register status.

Where pilot evidence is absent, time-positive value remains hypothesis-level or scenario-level. Where contractual revenue is absent, time-positive value must not be converted into investor yield. Where market validation is absent, it must not be converted into liquidity. Where legal review is absent, it must not be converted into investor rights.

**Q22: Does Prometheus reduce risk for investors?**

A: Not automatically. Prometheus may support risk analysis by making ecological, operational, MRV, governance, legal, market, integrity and runtime risks more visible, reviewable and manageable. It does not eliminate risk or guarantee risk compression, insurance recognition, bankability, lower cost of capital or portfolio resilience.

Risk-compression claims require evidence. Insurance-recognition claims require insurer or reinsurer review. Bankability claims require bank or credit-counterparty review. Portfolio-risk claims require portfolio evidence, correlation analysis, CVaR or equivalent risk review, stress testing and sensitivity analysis.

**Q23: Is there liquidity?**

A: No liquidity is admitted at the time of this release. There is no secondary market, redemption pathway, admitted transfer pathway, admitted custody pathway, admitted settlement pathway, admitted tokenised market or market-active RAP unless separately documented through RAP-M7 status.

Any future liquidity pathway would require legal review, instrument terms, investor eligibility rules, transfer restrictions or permissions, custody pathway where applicable, settlement pathway where applicable, market operator or counterparty pathway where applicable, AML / KYC and sanctions review where applicable, no-double-counting controls where external representation exists, disclosure package, Claims Register status and governance decision.

Until those conditions exist, the permitted language is: illiquid; non-transferable unless legally admitted; no secondary market; no redemption unless legally documented; no liquidity assumption; exit pathway not yet admitted.

**Q24: Can a token create liquidity or asset backing?**

A: No. A token does not create liquidity, asset backing, PRU value, RAP maturity, enforceable rights, investor protection, market demand, custody readiness or settlement readiness by itself.

A token may represent only what a valid legal, technical, custody, settlement, integrity and governance pathway allows it to represent.

Any token-facing pathway requires token classification, securities analysis where applicable, rights mapping, disclosure package, custody review, settlement review, no-double-counting controls, Claims Register support and governance approval.

**Q25: What is the correct financial interpretation for capital allocators today?**

A: The correct interpretation is engaged observation, not portfolio allocation.
