Prometheus may be evaluated as a pre-pilot institutional architecture, verification-first methodology, regenerative infrastructure thesis, capital-readiness framework, pilot participation pathway, data-room discipline, legal and market structuring workstream and future investment-product research pathway.

It should not be evaluated as a current investment opportunity, current security, current fund, current note, current tokenised instrument, current yield product, current liquidity pathway, current asset-backed product, current market-ready RAP or current secondary-market opportunity.

> **Final financial FAQ rule: no evidence, no value claim; no bounded PRU methodology, no PRU value claim; no contractual revenue, no cash-flow claim; no Legal Opinion Package, no investor-rights claim; no Anchor Demand File, no market-readiness claim; no RAP-M7, no market-active claim; no custody review, no custody-ready claim; no settlement review, no settlement-ready claim; no no-double-counting controls, no external representation claim; no Runtime Release Decision Record, no software-reliance claim; no disclosure package, no investor-facing claim; no governance decision, no capital-facing claim.**

**BB.4 Technology, Runtime and AI**

**Q26: When will the technology be operational?**

A: No fixed operational timeline should be represented as a promise. Runtime maturity is governed by release status, testing, security review, data-room evidence, Runtime Release Decision Record, governance admission and Claims Register alignment, not by calendar passage alone.

Prometheus distinguishes design specification, prototype, sandbox build, internal test build, release candidate, production-admitted release and externally relied-upon release. A prototype is not production. A sandbox build is not institutional infrastructure. A release candidate is not external admission. A production-admitted release is not automatically externally relied upon.

Time estimates may be used only as internal planning scenarios and must be labelled non-binding, conditional, subject to technical validation, subject to security review, subject to governance release and not a commitment to production readiness.

**Q27: Does a dashboard prove that PRU or RAP status is valid?**

A: No. A dashboard is not a claim authority. It may display only statuses supported by the Claims Register, evidence records, legal review, market review, integrity review, runtime release record and governance decision.

A dashboard view does not create ecological truth, legal rights, PRU value, RAP maturity, market demand, investor entitlement, no-double-spending assurance, production safety or regulatory status.

External dashboard use requires dashboard claim-status control, Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package, Runtime Release Decision Record, data-room link and Claims Register alignment.

**Q28: Does an API response create legal, market or evidentiary authority?**

A: No. An API response is not a legal opinion, market validation, PRU certification, RAP certification, production-readiness proof, regulatory approval or investor protection.

API outputs must be bounded by access-control rules, logging, audit trail, data lineage, Claims Register alignment, runtime release status and legal / market review where applicable.

No API may be described as API-certified, externally relied-upon, PRU-certified by software, RAP-certified by software, legally admissible by code or market-ready by dashboard unless the Runtime Release Decision Record supports that classification.

**Q29: What happens if AI generates false evidence?**

A: AI output is assistive only. AI does not create ecological truth, MRV admissibility, legal review, PRU value, RAP maturity, market demand, investor entitlement, governance authority or production safety.

If AI generates false, synthetic, hallucinated, poisoned, unverifiable or unsupported evidence, the affected output must be quarantined, excluded from Claims Register upgrade, excluded from PRU uplift, excluded from RAP eligibility and excluded from investor-facing, insurance-facing, procurement-facing, token-facing, dashboard-facing or API-facing claims.

AI-assisted evidence requires source separation, human review, provenance, method disclosure, audit trail, model-output boundary, reviewer trail and Claims Register alignment.

**Q30: Can AI approve PRU value, RAP maturity or investor-facing material?**

A: No. No AI model, external assistant, dashboard, API route, runtime bridge, zome call, VPS service or automated workflow may approve PRU value, validate MRV evidence, create legal rights, issue tokens, approve RAP maturity, authorize investor-facing claims or upgrade Claims Register status by itself.

AI may assist drafting, summarization, anomaly detection, workflow triage, data organization or internal review. It may not replace evidence review, legal review, market review, integrity review, runtime release review or governance decision.

**Q31: What is required for external software reliance?**

A: Any production-facing or externally relied-upon runtime component requires a Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package, runtime and release safety data-room layer, source commit hash, build artefact hash, release version, validation rule set, test-suite reference, security review where applicable, rollback procedure, incident-response procedure, monitoring plan, Runtime Release Decision Record, governance approval and Claims Register alignment.

Where a component affects PRU calculation, RAP status, Claims Register status, data-room evidence, legal status, token representation, bridge logic, registry synchronization, custody, settlement, market-facing language or investor-facing reporting, the runtime component remains non-admitted until the relevant evidence, integrity, legal, market and governance gates also support its use.

**Q32: How does Prometheus handle offline operation?**

A: Offline-first and local evidence continuity are implementation requirements. Prometheus should think locally, remember locally, recover locally and synchronize globally only when the network is available.

Offline operation must preserve local signed evidence bundles, local audit trails, local queueing, recovery procedures, synchronization conflict handling, duplicate-claim detection where applicable, data-integrity tests and Claims Register consistency.

Network loss must not convert unverified local records into admissible value. Synchronization must not overwrite evidence lineage, Claims Register status or no-double-counting controls.

**Q33: What is the final technology and AI FAQ rule?**

A: Runtime supports evidence, governance, reporting and coordination only within admitted scope. Runtime does not create the claim, validate the claim, finance the claim, legalize the claim or market-activate the claim.

> **No Runtime Release Decision Record, no runtime-readiness claim. No Claims Register alignment, no dashboard/API claim. No human review, no AI-supported evidence claim. No security review where applicable, no production admission. No rollback and incident response, no operational deployment. The lowest unresolved runtime, evidence, integrity, legal, market, security, operational, custody, settlement, AI, data-protection or governance gate controls the permitted technology claim.**

**BB.5 Governance, Tokens and Human Dignity**

**Q34: How does Prometheus protect human dignity?**

A: Through the Access Covenant, the Human Dignity and Voluntary Research Boundary, governance consent disciplines and the prohibition on using human-state data for PRU, RAP, governance, market or financial eligibility.

No biometric, behavioural, psychological, coherence, health, emotional, spiritual, educational, household or human-state data may enter PRU or RAP analysis unless it is explicitly quarantined from financial admissibility and handled through separate voluntary, ethics-reviewed, data-protected research modules where legally permitted.

Human dignity is not a productivity metric. It is a constitutional boundary.

**Q35: Is Prometheus a DAO?**

A: Prometheus may use DAO-compatible coordination mechanisms, but constitutional meaning is not token-weighted. Constitutional boundaries, Access Covenant commitments, Eternity Clauses, stewardship rules and human dignity protections cannot be overridden by token voting, capital weight, dashboard state, API output or runtime execution.

Tokens may coordinate operations, access or participation only within legally and governance-bounded scope. They may not create investor rights, constitutional authority or economic entitlement by themselves.

**Q36: What is the role of TRBK?**

A: TRBK is a governance, coordination, access or participation layer subject to its own legal and functional boundaries. It is not PRU. It is not RAP. It is not a security by itself. It is not a land right, revenue right, cash-flow right, investor exposure instrument or capital-facing product by itself.

Any investor-facing, transferable, revenue-linked, token-facing, custody-facing, settlement-facing or market-facing use of TRBK requires legal classification, rights mapping, disclosure, no-double-counting controls where applicable, custody / settlement review where applicable, governance approval and Claims Register support.

**Q37: What is the role of HoloFuel?**

A: HoloFuel, if used, is an operational transaction and infrastructure layer within agent-centric digital systems. It does not create PRU value, RAP maturity, investor rights, securities status, tokenised regenerative asset status, settlement readiness, custody readiness or market demand by itself.

Any external, regulated, payment-like, custody-facing, settlement-facing or investor-facing use requires separate functional classification, legal review, disclosure boundary and governance decision.

**Q38: How are conflicts of interest handled?**

A: Capital-facing Prometheus materials require governance transparency and conflict disclosure. Founder, operator, vendor, land, family, advisory, token, treasury, custodian, data-room, bridge, registry, dashboard, AI, capital-raising and reviewer conflicts must be disclosed, managed and linked to decision records where relevant.

No reviewer may be compensated on the basis of PRU uplift, RAP-M progression, financing success, offtake closure, token issuance, valuation uplift, dashboard launch, API circulation, runtime release, custody activation, settlement activation or market-facing approval.

**Q39: What is the final governance and token FAQ rule?**

A: Governance coordinates. It does not create evidence. It does not create legal rights. It does not create market demand. It does not create production readiness. It cannot override missing evidence, legal review, market validation, no-double-counting controls, runtime release requirements or Claims Register status.

> **No valid legal instrument, no enforceable rights. No Claims Register support, no public claim. No governance decision, no status upgrade. No token classification, no token-ready claim. No custody and settlement review, no custody-ready or settlement-ready claim.**

**BB.6 Research and Non-Admissible Claims**

**Q40: What is SyntroGIFT and why is it quarantined?**

A: SyntroGIFT is a historical exploratory concept about qualitative relationships among regenerative food systems, nature exposure, education, movement, community participation and preventive-health inquiry.

It has no standing in the Prometheus core methodology. It cannot support PRU value, RAP eligibility, Conf_total, investor-facing claims, insurance-facing claims, procurement-facing claims, token-facing language, dashboard claims, API outputs or financial admissibility.

Any future investigation must occur through separate voluntary, ethics-reviewed, data-protected research modules where legally permitted and must remain outside PRU, RAP, investor-facing and capital-facing use unless separately admitted through valid scientific, legal, ethical and governance review.

**Q41: How should frontier, philosophical or speculative material be handled?**

A: Frontier material may be preserved as research context, philosophical framing or future inquiry only when it is excluded from PRU value, Conf_total, Claims Register upgrade, investor-facing claims and financial admissibility unless separately validated and admitted.

Research context is not evidence. Plausibility is not proof. Narrative coherence is not admissibility. A model is not pilot data. A theory is not an investor claim.

**Q42: Can human-health or wellness outcomes support PRU or RAP value?**

A: Not by default. Human-health, wellness, psychological, coherence, behavioral, educational or community-wellbeing claims are sensitive and must remain outside PRU and RAP value unless a separate lawful, ethical, voluntary, data-protected and professionally reviewed research pathway exists.

Health-adjacent claims may inform non-financial research context, program design or public-health inquiry. They may not be used for investor-facing valuation, token claims, insurance claims, procurement claims or capital-facing reporting without appropriate review and legal admissibility.

**Q43: What is the final research boundary?**

A: Research material may guide inquiry. It may not create value, rights, market demand, regulatory status, production readiness or investor reliance.

> **No pre-registration, no pilot claim. No baseline, no improvement claim. No comparator, no superiority claim. No confidence range, no institutional reliance. No Claims Register support, no public claim. No legal and ethical review where applicable, no sensitive human-data claim.**

**BB.7 Final Disclaimer**

This FAQ is an orientation tool, not legal, financial, regulatory, tax, accounting, insurance, procurement, technical-security, data-protection or investment advice.

All answers are subordinate to the Prometheus Canon, the Institutional Status Declaration, the Claims Register, the data room, the Legal Opinion Package, the Anchor Demand File, the no-double-counting and interoperability controls, the Runtime Verification and Release Safety Package, the Runtime Release Decision Record and the relevant governance decisions.

Capital allocators, family offices, procurement officers, insurance or reinsurance partners, public-sector partners, legal counsel, technical reviewers and institutional counterparties must conduct their own due diligence, jurisdictional review, legal structuring, market validation, data-room review, runtime review and risk assessment.

Prometheus provides architecture, methodology, evidence discipline, governance logic, data-room discipline and capital-readability infrastructure. It does not replace regulated asset management, legal counsel, financial advice, securities review, tax advice, insurance underwriting, procurement qualification, technical security review, runtime release review or investor diligence.

> **Final FAQ rule: follow the gate; read the claim boundary; verify the evidence; check the Claims Register; inspect the data room; require legal review; require market validation; require runtime release records where software is relied upon; and never permit a stronger claim than the weakest unresolved gate allows.**

**Part 5: Verification Instructions**

**Prerequisites**

- gpg installed

- Public signing key

- Release package

**Step 1: Import key**

gpg --import prometheus_public_key.asc

**Step 2: Verify signature**

gpg --verify SHA256_SUMS.txt.asc SHA256_SUMS.txt

**Step 3: Verify file integrity**

sha256sum -c SHA256_SUMS.txt

**Step 4: Verify manifest**

Check version, file list, hashes.

**Step 5: Verify constitutional documents**

Confirm hashes for:

- Syntropic_Sovereignty_Header.md

- h•eart•h_intelligence_Manifesto.md

- CAL‑1.0_Syntropic_rider_license.md

If any step fails, the package should not be trusted.

**Part 6: Contribution Discipline and Implementation Reviewability**

All contributions must:

- Preserve semantic fidelity

- Include validation and reviewability

- Respect scope discipline

- Be explicitly proposed

Changes affecting canonical semantics, validation logic, or subsystem boundaries require governance approval.

**Without reviewability, continuity decays. Without continuity, constitutional intent becomes vulnerable to drift.**

**Part 7: Fork Governance Summary**

| **Fork Type** | **Permitted Use**                     | **Status**          |
|---------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------|
| Canonical     | Preserves source‑layer fidelity       | Official            |
| Compatible    | Attribution + boundary rules          | Registry‑recognised |
| Independent   | Uses ideas without claiming lineage   | Not Prometheus      |
| Experimental  | Sandbox only                          | Sandbox             |
| Hostile       | Misrepresents or weakens source layer | Quarantined         |

No fork may claim canonical status without fidelity and registry recognition.

**Part 8: Constitutional Maxims (Recapitulation)**

Scope before scale.\
Validation before execution.\
Life before abstraction.\
Evidence before authority.\
Review before automation.\
Admissibility before value.\
Legal structure before rights.\
Rights before representation.\
Regeneration before capital.\
Stewardship before monetization.\
Coherence before expansion.\
Semantics before speed.\
Runtime fidelity before deployment.\
Repository visibility before public reliance.\
Cryptographic verification before canonical trust.\
Registry status before public claim.\
Source‑layer fidelity before implementation.\
Human dignity before optimization.\
Ecological reality before financial convenience.

No dashboard is authority by itself.\
No AI output is authority by itself.\
No token creates ecological value by itself.\
No fork creates lineage by itself.\
No investment creates legitimacy by itself.\
No institution owns the meaning of Prometheus by itself.\
No durable value can emerge from the degradation of life‑support systems.

Future generations are present stakeholders.\
The source layer is the memory of Prometheus.\
Verifiable lineage is the continuity of Prometheus.\
Living systems are the origin of Prometheus.\
Regeneration is the purpose of Prometheus.
